races.race_name: CAA Stellar Great Cheshire Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Respond
runners.spotlight: In good form on the AW since returning in February, easily justifying short odds at Chelmsford (1m2f) in March; no problem with the return to turf having won over this trip at Haydock (good) last August; can take a hold, but may settle better in this big field and he remains unexposed; a player.
runners.comment: Both wins over 1m2f on turf/AW; may settle better in this big field; remains unexposed

runners.name: Whip Cracker
runners.spotlight: Without a win since his second start as a 2yo, but several good efforts since as when beaten a length into third of 24 in the Newbury Spring Cup (1m, good ) last month; Ryan Moore keeps the ride and rain shouldn't be an issue, but the draw could have been kinder.
runners.comment: No win since second start as a 2yo, but several good efforts since; drawn wider than ideal

runners.name: Bragbor
runners.spotlight: Both wins have come on the AW at Newcastle (1m/1m2f) and was far from disgraced on his return in the Easter Classic there (1m2f) five weeks ago; makes his turf debut in a competitive race, albeit stall 1 has taken three of the last eight runnings.
runners.comment: Makes turf debut in a competitive race; stall 1 has taken three of the last eight runnings

runners.name: Triple Double A
runners.spotlight: Twice held off 1lb higher since making a successful return from eight months off at Newcastle (1m2f, AW) in February, including when behind two of these in the Rosebery; needs a resurgence and could probably do without the ground easing.
runners.comment: Twice held since making a successful return at Newcastle in February; wouldn't want rain

runners.name: Clouds Hill
runners.spotlight: Enjoyed the run of the race when making all to beat four rivals on his Yarmouth return (1m2f, good) eight days ago; has a 5lb penalty to carry and ground softer than good would be an unknown, but he remains unexposed after just five starts so makes the shortlist.
runners.comment: Made all on his Yarmouth return eight days ago; remains unexposed; makes the shortlist
runners.owner: Sheikh Hamdan bin Mohammed Al Maktoum

runners.name: Callianassa
runners.spotlight: All four wins have come over 7f/1m on the AW, the latest in February; back off the same mark as for that success, but she is 0-10 on turf and has never attempted this far before.
runners.comment: Back off last winning AW mark, but is 0-10 on turf and has never attempted this far before

runners.name: El Burhan
runners.spotlight: Has a fine strike-rate on turf (4-8) including a win at this three-day meeting (7.6f, good) a year ago; would have gone closer with a clearer run when fifth of 13 in the Rosebery at Kempton (1m3f, AW) on his reappearance (Respond third); still open to improvement and rain wouldn't be a problem; major player.
runners.comment: Has a fine strike-rate on turf (4-8); open to improvement and rain wouldn't be a problem

runners.name: Great David
runners.spotlight: Highly tried after making a winning reappearance at Nottingham (1m2f, good) a year ago; proven fresh, but hard to know how well treated he is for this return to handicap company.
runners.comment: Proven fresh, but hard to know how well treated he is for this return to handicap company

runners.name: Echalar
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced on turf; out of the frame in five starts since winning at Wolverhampton (7f, AW) in January; has plenty to prove over this trip back on grass and is 3lb out of the weights.
runners.comment: Has plenty to prove over this trip back on grass and is 3lb out of the weights

runners.name: Bravais
runners.spotlight: Winner in France and Ireland, but has beaten a total of one rival in four starts on the AW since joining this yard; down another 3lb, but hard to warm to unless the market suggests otherwise; stable also runs Echalar; non-runner 4.10 Chester Thursday.
runners.comment: Beaten just one rival in four starts on AW since joining yard; non-runner here yesterday

runners.name: Auld Toon Loon
runners.spotlight: All four wins at around this trip including one here (soft) in the summer of 2023; looked unlucky when beaten a head in this race off 3lb lower on his reappearance a year ago; won't mind what the weather does, but stall 14 isn't ideal.
runners.comment: Looked unlucky when beaten a head in this race a year ago, but stall 14 isn't ideal

runners.name: Janey Mackers
runners.spotlight: Twice highly tried after winning a handicap at Newbury (1m2f, good to soft) from 7lb lower in September; still unexposed, but she may need this after six months off and is drawn wide.
runners.comment: Still unexposed, but she may need this after six months off and is drawn wide

races.race_name: Boodles Darley EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB/GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Joulany
runners.spotlight: Derby and Irish Derby entry; 800,000gns Sea The Stars yearling, brother to multiple Group 3 winner Al Aasy (1m2f-1m5f); turned over at odds-on in his two starts last autumn, but gave it a good shot when always-prominent third of ten in blanket finish to a strong novice at Newbury (1m2f, good) on reappearance; the hood worn last time is now removed; top on ratings here and likely to have come on from that run.
runners.comment: Top on ratings and likely to have come on from his reappearance third at Newbury
runners.owner: Al Shaqab Racing, Amo Racing & Valmont

runners.name: Galiyan
runners.spotlight: Closely related to 1m1f-1m2f winner Integrant (including Listed; RPR 108); 7-1 for novice at Newmarket (1m2f, good) three weeks ago but looked green on his way to 7l fourth of five; that's still good enough to enter serious calculations.
runners.comment: Green 4th at Newmarket three weeks ago but still good enough to enter serious calculations

runners.name: Glory Of The Seas
runners.spotlight: Sea The Stars colt who cost 550,000gns as a yearling; Dante and Derby entries have gone but he's still in the Irish Derby; that's after his fourth of eight in a strong-looking maiden at Newbury (1m3f, good; 4-1) three weeks ago on second start, in which he saw it out 4l better than Eben Al Khawaneej; major contender, type to improve with his racing.
runners.comment: Fourth in strong 1m3f Newbury maiden three weeks ago; major contender, type to improve

runners.name: Distorted Truth
runners.spotlight: Camelot filly who was a well-backed second of nine in maiden at Kempton (1m3f, AW) in January but a remote last of six when 18-1 for a novice at Southwell (1m3f, AW) in February; others seem more likely.
runners.comment: 2nd at Kempton in January but remote last of six at Southwell (also 1m3f, AW) in February

runners.name: Morningtoncrescent
runners.spotlight: 150-1 and on toes when 11l sixth of 14 to a 6l winner in maiden at Newbury (1m2f, good) three weeks ago, which was a lot better than debut; further progress is likely but this race may prove too tough.
runners.comment: 150-1 and on toes when sixth at Newbury three weeks ago; this race looks too tough
runners.owner: Emma Banks , Rocco, De Ramsey & Dobson

runners.name: Take A Chill Pill
runners.spotlight: 65,000gns yearling by Kingman out of Listed-placed 7f/1m winner (105); 18-1, just under 10l fifth of ten to an impressive winner in newcomers race at Newmarket (1m, good) three weeks ago, prominent long way; could do a lot better in due course but others may be more ready this time.
runners.comment: 18-1, just under 10l fifth in newcomers race at Newmarket three weeks ago; may do better

runners.name: Eben Al Khawaneej
runners.spotlight: Camelot colt (from speedy family) who looked backward and green for a strong-looking maiden race at Newbury (1m3f, good; 16-1) three weeks ago and ended up sixth of eight, with Glory Of The Seas 4l ahead; could emerge into something much better and he needs a second look, for same connections who won this with a twice-raced colt last year.
runners.comment: Backward and green on Newbury debut, 4l behind Glory Of The Seas; needs a second look

races.race_name: Deepbridge Huxley Stakes (Group 2)

runners.name: Lambourn
runners.spotlight: Very much at the top his generation last year, winning the Chester Vase at this meeting before going on to classic glory at Epsom and the Curragh (all three wins over 1m4f on good ground); he was undeniably disappointing in the Great Voltigeur (1m4f, good to firm) and St Leger (14.5f, soft), but this looks a good starting point for his 4yo career given the race conditions and he should take the beating if anything like back to his best; cheekpieces are given a go.
runners.comment: Dual Derby winner; this looks a good starting point for his 4yo career; cheekpieces on
runners.owner: Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith

runners.name: King's Gambit
runners.spotlight: No wins since landing two of his first four starts having been highly tried in the meantime, but he has a consistent record in Group/Listed company shy of the top level (22363522); again ran well when chasing home the smart Damysus on his return in the Earl Of Sefton at Newmarket (1m1f, good) last month; shortlisted with the run under his belt.
runners.comment: Has consistent record in Group/Listed company shy of the top level (22363522); shortlisted

runners.name: Sky Safari
runners.spotlight: Smart on the AW (1111121), her latest success coming in the Group 3 Winter Derby at Lingfield (1m2f) in February; her winning racecourse debut came on turf (7f, soft), but this looks a tough task on her return to grass.
runners.comment: Smart on the AW (1111121), but this looks a tough task on her return to grass

runners.name: Starford
runners.spotlight: Progressive in handicaps last season but took his form to a new level when getting the better of a stablemate in a Group 3 at the Curragh (1m2f, soft) last month; may yet be capable of even better, but he could do with some rain.
runners.comment: Won a Group 3 at the Curragh last month; capable of better, but could do with some rain

runners.name: Royal Rhyme
runners.spotlight: Did well early in his carerer including when beating three rivals in the 2024 Brigadier Gerard (1m2f, soft); 0-8 since, albeit twice placed at the highest level; pulled too hard when beaten over 6l into sixth of 12 behind Starford on his return in a Group 3 at the Curragh (1m2f, soft) last month, but that should have taken the freshness out of him; could probably do with the ground easing, though.
runners.comment: Pulled too hard on his reappearance but that should have taken the freshness out of him
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: High Stock
runners.spotlight: Highly tried after his winning Newmarket debut in April last year and ran well on a couple of occasions, as when beaten a neck in the Dee Stakes over C&D (good); has a bit to find to mix it with the majority of these, though.
runners.comment: Beaten a neck in last year's Dee Stakes but has a bit to find with the majority of these

runners.name: Ice Max
runners.spotlight: Ended last season with a pair of 1m victories, the latest in a Nottingham Listed event (soft); not totally disgraced when fifth of eight in a Group 3 at Dusseldorf (1m, heavy) on last month's return, but that does indicate his limitations; races beyond an extended 1m for the first time; stable also runs Royal Rhyme.
runners.comment: Fifth in Dusseldorf Group 3 on his return; races beyond an extended 1m for the first time
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Sparks Fly
runners.spotlight: Admirable mare who is 12-21 on turf including wins at Group 3/Listed level; has winning form over C&D, but will need to improve plenty on her reappearance effort at Saint-Cloud in March if she is to play a part here.
runners.comment: 12-21 on turf but will need to improve plenty on her reappearance effort in March

runners.name: Bay City Roller
runners.spotlight: Showed some high-class form last year including when beaten less than a length by Daryz in the Prix Egene Adam; hit the big time when making all to win the Group 1 Grosser Allianz Preis von Bayern by 7l at Munich (1m4f, soft) in November; his 5lb penalty won't make things easy and this should set him up nicely for bigger targets ahead, but he goes well fresh and shouldn't be underestimated.
runners.comment: Has a 5lb penalty to carry, but he goes well fresh and shouldn't be underestimated

races.race_name: Ladbrokes Chester Cup (Heritage Handicap) (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Berkshire Sundance
runners.spotlight: Missed most of last season but won on good/AW in four of his six starts since, the latest in clearcut fashion with first-time cheekpieces at Newcastle on AW Championships day; raised another 7lb, drawn wide and lacks any race experience at this track, but he's going from strength to strength.
runners.comment: Missed most of last season but won on good/AW in four of his six starts since; drawn wide
runners.owner: Berkshire Parts & Panels Ltd No1 Fanclub

runners.name: Peaky Blinder
runners.spotlight: 4yo who has had just six races, on last two occasions winning well in handicaps at Redcar (1m6f, good to soft) in October and Southwell (slowly run 2m, AW; gelded) four weeks ago; raised another 6lb and substantially in class, but he's probably going places and is one for the shortlist.
runners.comment: Six races, winning well last two; up another 6lb and substantially in class, but shortlist

runners.name: Chemistry
runners.spotlight: His time with Aidan O'Brien and Ivan Furtado was on the chequered side but three bits of useful form read well compared to this mark, including his staying-on fourth at Newcastle (2m, AW) in December off 5lb higher; made a successful yard/hurdles debut at Sedgefield four weeks ago, which is a light year away from this assignment but a case can be made.
runners.comment: Three bits of useful form read well compared to this mark and a case can be made for him
runners.owner: James Paul Services To Industry Ltd

runners.name: Blindedbythelights
runners.spotlight: Versatile tactically; his only two wins were in 2023 but this highly reliable handicapper has been runner-up in six of his seven races since, including in useful company and also over 2m when he wore cheekpieces on last three occasions (hung right and lost out to a rallying rival last time); now tries blinkers and has each-way claims from low draw.
runners.comment: Highly reliable and has been runner-up in six of his last seven races; different headgear

runners.name: Moon Over Miami
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 5yo who won the first three of his four races on turf, including a narrow rallying win at Newbury (2m, good) last April; absent afterwards until below-form joint-second favourite from the front at Musselburgh (1m6f) five weeks ago but he should be a lot more potent with today's trip and that run under his belt; interesting.
runners.comment: Lightly raced 5yo; should be a lot more potent again with last month's run under his belt

runners.name: Team Player
runners.spotlight: Led on the post here for the first of two 1m4f wins last summer for Gemma Tutty and, with tongue-tie enlisted, hit major new heights for new yard this year with his wins from off the pace at Newcastle (2m, AW) and Musselburgh (1m6f, soft); raised another 9lb, which demands more, but those last two wins were delivered in some style and he has to be considered seriously.
runners.comment: Raised another 9lb today, which demands more, but he won his last two starts in some style

runners.name: Spirit Mixer
runners.spotlight: One of three for the yard; won the Northumberland Plate at Newcastle (2m, AW) last June and has a 4-7 record at Chester, although his two performances in this race were muted (from double-figure draws); reappearance six weeks ago matched his best 2025 form but a bit extra will be needed.
runners.comment: Has a 4-7 record at Chester, although his two performances in this race were muted

runners.name: Galileo Dame
runners.spotlight: Yard runs three; well beaten in Listed races last autumn but, fit from hurdling, made a good Flat return when second at Navan (1m6f, heavy) at the end of March; that was both her Flat handicap debut and the furthest she's been in this sphere, which opens up possibilities for her as a Flat stayer.
runners.comment: Made a good return to the Flat when second at Navan (1m6f, heavy) at the end of March
runners.owner: Annus Mirabilis Synd'/Sean O'Driscoll/Middle Cow Unlimited

runners.name: Puturhandstogether
runners.spotlight: One of three for Joseph O'Brien; won 4yo handicap hurdle at 2025 Cheltenham Festival and also took a huge prize for last September's Irish Cesarewitch on Flat at the Curragh (2m1f, yielding); underwhelming in his three handicap hurdles in November-April, which is a concern, as is today's widest stall, but his Curragh win (bursting clear 1f out) was delivered with considerably more authority than the winning margin suggests; respected.
runners.comment: Underwhelming over hurdles recently but two big wins (hurdles/Flat) in 2025 demand respect

runners.name: Aimeric
runners.spotlight: Won sole start here, a 2022 maiden; smart at 1m3f-1m6f for Roger Varian and Karl Burke but reappearance was easily the best of three runs last year and, after a 21,000gns sale in November, well beaten at 11-10 for hurdles/yard debut two weeks ago; that's not the most promising stepping stone to this.
runners.comment: Formerly smart at 1m3f-1m6f; well beaten at 11-10 for hurdles/yard debut recently

runners.name: A Piece Of Heaven
runners.spotlight: One of three runners for the yard; lightly raced 8yo who was second in the 2m1f Irish Cesarewitch in 2024 and also in non-handicaps over 10.5f and 1m6f on his latest two starts this year; will probably be in good form but needs a career best.
runners.comment: Lightly raced 8yo; second in 2024 Irish Cesarewitch and also on his latest two starts

runners.name: Alphonse Le Grande
runners.spotlight: Held up; an excellent 2024 included wins in the Chester Plate on this card (his only run here) and the Cesarewitch at Newmarket; 2025 campaign was initially promising then frustrating, including with trouble in running, but ended up being disappointing; has a recent 1m2f run under his belt and would be competitive off this slightly reduced mark if back on song.
runners.comment: Won 2024 C&D Plate; promising, frustrating then disappointing in 2025, but not ruled out

runners.name: Duraji
runners.spotlight: Sole win (ten starts) for Dermot Weld was a maiden in 2024; has tried Group/Listed races but his most competitive show last year came when fourth of 14 in 1m6f handicap at Glorious Goodwood, off 3lb higher than today; tried a hood on AW debut at Lingfield in February and has since changed stables (same owners); has a bit to prove and such a wide draw is unlikely to help.
runners.comment: Just a maiden win for Dermot Weld; also fourth of 14 in 1m6f handicap at Glorious Goodwood

runners.name: Maxi King
runners.spotlight: Reliable sort with a good first-time-out record, and he looks well worth this first foray beyond 14.5f, but there is something to prove if the ground if firmer than good; promising 7lb claimer has his first ride at Chester.
runners.comment: Good first-time-out record; worth first foray beyond 14.5f; query on firmer than good

runners.name: Zanndabad
runners.spotlight: An unlucky, strong-finishing third in this race in 2024 (good to firm; his only run here) and has a 1lb lower mark today; flickers of that ability on occasions since, though had to have his sights lowered for two hurdle wins last autumn; tailed off at 40-1 in a Grade 1 novice hurdle four weeks ago, after a winter break, and could be more interesting this time.
runners.comment: Unlucky, strong-finishing third in this in 2024; 1lb lower today and some flickers since

runners.name: Lips Freedom
runners.spotlight: Three Flat wins when trained in Germany, in Italian 1m4f Listed race on last two occasions (August 2023 and 2024); slow to find his feet in Ireland but winning a 2m2f handicap hurdle on latest outing was a positive development in tongue-tie first time; he'd have had a much higher mark two years ago but others have more solid claims.
runners.comment: Hurdle win was a positive development in first tongue-tie but others have stronger claims

runners.name: Tashkhan
runners.spotlight: Fit from a mixed hurdles campaign; formerly very smart on Flat, including with a C&D handicap win in 2023 but he's not scored in this sphere since, had a disappointing 2025 and all wins have been on soft or heavy ground.
runners.comment: Fit from a mixed hurdles campaign; all wins have been on soft or heavy ground

races.race_name: Tom Jones Live At Chester Racecourse Handicap

runners.name: The Good Biscuit
runners.spotlight: Ended 2025 in top form and he has returned to action with two solid placed efforts; 2lb ahead of the handicapper and no obvious reason why he wouldn't give it another good shot.
runners.comment: Still improving and he is 2lb ahead of the handicapper; should make another bold bid

runners.name: Jonny Concrete
runners.spotlight: Ended 2025 with a couple of modest efforts but his form in the first half of the year brings him right into the reckoning; can go well fresh and he looks a leading contender eased in class.
runners.comment: Ended 2025 quietly but his best efforts last year give him good claims at this level

runners.name: Queue Dos
runners.spotlight: 4-10 on AW but 0-11 on turf; unlikely to be enhancing that record from the outside stall.
runners.comment: 4-10 on AW but 0-11 on turf; unlikely to be enhancing that record from the outside stall

runners.name: Clonmacash
runners.spotlight: Fully exposed 5yo but he recorded the best RPR of his 50-race career when winning at Dundalk last month (6f); while effective on turf, he looks high enough in the weights now returning to grass; well drawn but others appeal more for win purposes.
runners.comment: Career-best RPR when winning at Dundalk last month (6f); turf form is not quite as good

runners.name: Kirkdale
runners.spotlight: 7f turf win as a 2yo (soft ground); he has looked an improved performer in two runs this spring, coming from off the pace to win 6f handicaps at Wolverhampton and Southwell; drawn wide but likely to be dropped in and he can't be discounted granted normal luck in running.
runners.comment: He has looked an improved performer this spring, winning twice on AW (6f); tricky draw

runners.name: Hoodie Hoo
runners.spotlight: Yet to win a handicap but he went close at Nottingham last October and again finishing second on last month's reappearance at Lingfield (6f, AW); this is more competitive but he isn't fully exposed.
runners.comment: 0-8 in handicaps but he has threatened, finishing 2nd twice; still relatively low mileage
runners.owner: Ged Mason, Sir Alex Ferguson, Jim McGoff

runners.name: Aisling Oscar
runners.spotlight: He has been a revelation on AW for this yard, winning eight times since November; better over longer trips, yet to place in ten turf runs and drawn wide so this is unlikely to be his day.
runners.comment: Prolific on AW this winter but 0-10 on turf, drawn wide and best over further

runners.name: Rosenpur
runners.spotlight: Well suited by a left-hand turn, winning twice here over 5f last summer; promising seasonal return at Catterick (6f, good) but he wasn't in the same form at Musselburgh (5f, good) nine days ago; handy draw and he can't be discounted.
runners.comment: Conditions should suit and he isn't handicapped out of things; one to consider

runners.name: Secret Guest
runners.spotlight: He hasn't been at his best in recent starts but he has dipped to a dangerous mark and he has landed a good draw; this is a tough race in which to try and turn things around though.
runners.comment: Not at best in recent starts and looks vulnerable despite a reduced mark and good draw

runners.name: Kiniro
runners.spotlight: Won twice for Harry Charlton, both over 6f on Lingfield's AW track; has shown promise on turf; sold for 40,000gns in October; double-figure draw and revised mark both ask stiffer questions of him but interesting that Oisin Murphy, who rode him to success when last seen, is back in the saddle.
runners.comment: Two AW wins for Harry Charlton; sold 40,000gns; drawn wide but Oisin Murphy is booked

runners.name: Miraculous
runners.spotlight: Two C&D runs for Roger Varian yielded a win and a second; may have needed the outing on last month's stable debut and he's well treated if that run brings him on; more appealing than many.
runners.comment: C&D record reads 12; sharper for last month's stable debut; more appealing than many

runners.name: Paws For Thought
runners.spotlight: Six times a course winner, including over this trip; can go well fresh and not handicapped out of things but slower ground would have been preferable.
runners.comment: Six-time course winner; can go well fresh and feasibly weighted; slower ground ideal

runners.name: Bright
runners.spotlight: His last three wins have come on the AW at Newcastle but he is effective on turf and this mark shouldn't be beyond him; stall 15 is a severe hindrance though.
runners.comment: Not handicapped out of things but defying stall 15 would be a notable achievement

runners.name: Intervention
runners.spotlight: Won last season's Great St Wilfrid at Ripon off a 5lb higher mark; didn't run badly at Nottingham 12 days ago (5f, good) but he has a double-figure stall to contend with and could prove vulnerable.
runners.comment: On a dangerous mark but he has a wide stall to contend with and may prove vulnerable again

runners.name: Manila Scouse
runners.spotlight: On a competitive mark with a handy inside draw but he is returning from a seven month absence and his connections are also represented by Miraculous.
runners.comment: Good mark and well drawn but returning from seven months off; connections run two

races.race_name: Ladbrokes Get A Free Race Bet Today Chester Plate Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: St Faz
runners.spotlight: Seen mostly over jumps in his Irish days; getting hampered at the start prevented his usual prominent ride on penultimate outing but he's otherwise swept all before him for new yard this year, including when returned to turf for eight-runner race at Musselburgh (2m, good) 12 days ago; has 3lb penalty (the same as he's due to go up anyway) but wide draw is the chief worry.
runners.comment: Sole defeat for new yard this year when hampered at the start two runs ago; wide draw here

runners.name: Carlton
runners.spotlight: Highly fruitful in his two and a half years with this yard, including two wins (Flat/hurdles) this February/March; needs to bounce back from a rare flop but the chief concern is his 0-15 record on Flat turf.
runners.comment: Needs to bounce back from a rare flop; chief concern is 0-15 record on Flat turf

runners.name: Shrimp Shady
runners.spotlight: Well beaten at Newmarket (good) last September on handicap debut but pretty consistent in his five AW races since, on last two occasions winning over 1m6f and second of 14 over 2m; peak AW form needs to be transferred.
runners.comment: Pretty consistent in his five AW handicaps; peak AW form needs to be transferred

runners.name: Tribal Star
runners.spotlight: Seen mostly on AW, including fourth of 20 in the Northumberland Plate at Newcastle (2m) last June; running well on AW this year and although he's 0-6 on turf, that includes a close third here (2m, good to soft) in September; each-way player.
runners.comment: Running well on AW this year and close third here in September; each-way player

runners.name: Morning Air
runners.spotlight: Beaten about 30l in all three handicap hurdles this year; the 2025 Chester Cup was more encouraging, as she was beaten just over 9l into ninth of 16, having been in touch for a long way at 66-1, and she is 6lb lower today; others have more pressing claims but her trainer won this in 2021 and 2022.
runners.comment: 9th in 2025 Chester Cup; others preferred, but her trainer won this in 2021 and 2022
runners.owner: Lady Price, C Shaw, C Boylan & I Tagg

runners.name: It's All About You
runners.spotlight: His only appearance at Chester was a successful one in eight-runner handicap last August (2m, good to firm) off 3lb lower than today, in a manner that was encouraging for this test; absent since a lesser show in September but scored in 2024 after a 113-day layoff and needs a close look.
runners.comment: Won his only Chester race (last August) and won in 2024 after a break; needs a close look
runners.owner: A Baxter, S, Sowray, K Ward

runners.name: Imperial Sovereign
runners.spotlight: Sole win (18 races) was a novice in 2023; ended 2025 bang there in two AW handicaps (2m/1m6f) and ran respectably on both starts this term, back on good ground latest, but drawn wide and others are preferred.
runners.comment: Ran respectably both starts this term, back on good ground latest, but others preferred

runners.name: Gibside
runners.spotlight: Strong finish from the rear secured this prize last year (good ground) off 1lb lower than today, but also from stall 4 compared to today's stall 16; also won over 1m4f on soft at Catterick in October; needs to leave his two efforts this season (1m4f, good) a long way behind him but this may be a much more favourable scenario if the draw does not scupper him.
runners.comment: Won this in 2025 off 1lb lower; back in more favourable scenario if not scuppered by draw

runners.name: Wahraan
runners.spotlight: Quirky and sometimes blows the start; well handicapped judged on lots of 2024 form over 1m4f-2m, but he's seldom sparked in the same way since.
runners.comment: Well handicapped judged on lots of 2024 form over 1m4f-2m but has seldom sparked since

runners.name: Boxing Great
runners.spotlight: Runner-up twice on good ground; ninth start when off the mark in 14-runner handicap at Dundalk (2m, AW; favourite) in December when last seen; needs to improve on that form but he's relatively lightly raced.
runners.comment: Off the mark in December at Dundalk; needs to improve on that; relatively lightly raced

runners.name: Sax Appeal
runners.spotlight: Something amiss on sole start here (2024); very busy since and he's had his share of wins, including four over 2m in 2025, but that's been mostly on AW (which has dominated career) and his turf form needs improvement.
runners.comment: Busy; has had his share of wins but career dominated by AW; turf form needs improvement

runners.name: Kate O'Riley
runners.spotlight: Dual Wolverhampton AW winner (8.5f/1m4f) for Michael Bell, a hurdles winner in October and back on the Flat with three good runs this year, when facing a very stiff task in Listed race on latest; unraced beyond that 1m6f on Flat but she's half-sister to a staying celebrity in Big Orange, which heightens interest.
runners.comment: Unraced beyond 1m6f on Flat but she's half-sister to a staying celebrity in Big Orange

runners.name: Call My Bluff
runners.spotlight: Bang there every time in his four races at Chester, including the Cup in 2023 but absent 758 days afterwards; back to life at Newbury (2m, good) three weeks ago, making all in a six-runner race; has a 3lb penalty and drawn higher than ideal but he was another 6lb higher at his peak and has returned to serious calculations.
runners.comment: Back to life at Newbury latest and has fine record here; returned to serious calculations

runners.name: Kingstonian
runners.spotlight: Fifth start when winning a 1m4f Tramore maiden last August, looking as if he would stay further; it was heavy ground when he had a respectable attempt at 1m6f handicap next time, so he remains unexposed on this first start since leaving Jessica Harrington for 90,000gns in October; could be major player.
runners.comment: Won 1m4f Tramore maiden and his 1m6f attempt was on heavy going; remains unexposed

runners.name: Hamsiyann
runners.spotlight: Met with heavy defeats in his two Flat races last term and hasn't lived up to expectations as a hurdler; 2024 was much more promising, including with his second in this race off 1lb lower than today, and it may be good a pointer to see how his two stablemates get on in the Chester Cup.
runners.comment: 2nd in this race off 1lb lower in 2024; stablemates in Chester Cup may be a good pointer

runners.name: Mr Escobar
runners.spotlight: Beaten a nose here (2m, soft) in September 2024 off 1lb lower and gave further encouragement that year, but that promise has not been fulfilled and his jumps career (latest start two months ago) has gone badly the wrong way; first-time blinkers replace his usual hood.
runners.comment: Beaten a nose here in 2024, promise that has not been fulfilled; different headgear now

runners.name: Who's Glen
runners.spotlight: 9-1 when fifth in the Chester Cup last year for Andrew Balding, which is as good as he's ever been on Flat; 7lb lower today, having been right off the boil for that yard later in 2025; recent hurdle efforts for new stable aren't enough to persuade, especially given such a wide draw.
runners.comment: 7lb lower than Chester Cup 5th last year; recent hurdle efforts are not enough to persuade

races.race_name: Irish Thoroughbred Marketing Handicap

runners.name: Alvin
runners.spotlight: Wolverhampton novice winner (5f) who ran a blinder for third in a 6f handicap at Lingfield on Good Friday, hampered early then racing wide throughout but still producing a strong finish; confirmed the merit of that effort when outclassing his rivals in a 0-80 at Bath 11 days ago (5f, firm); he is a sprinter firmly on the up but stall 11 will not make life easy.
runners.comment: Confirmed promise of Lingfield third with easy Bath win; well treated but stall 11 to defy

runners.name: Henrythenate
runners.spotlight: Three 5f wins around a left-hand turn on AW; clobbered by the handicapper for his two wins for Stuart Williams over the winter and he has come up short in better company since; sold for 8,000gns last month; vulnerable back on turf on his stable debut.
runners.comment: High in weights after 2 AW wins for Stuart Williams this year; sold 8,000gns since latest

runners.name: Wyle Cop
runners.spotlight: Latest Ripon run is not the one to judge him on as he didn't handle the track; earlier Southwell third didn't suggest he was ahead of the handicapper; well drawn back at 5f but this is a step up in class.
runners.comment: Third at Southwell two runs back came in a lesser handicap; good draw back at 5f

runners.name: Watcha Snoop
runners.spotlight: His Ascot novice win last September came over 6f and on soft ground; well beaten over 6f here on Wednesday and he has stall 14 to contend with now dropping to 5f for the first time.
runners.comment: Down the field over 6f here on Wednesday; poor draw now dropping to 5f

runners.name: Schrodinger's Cat
runners.spotlight: Won a Redcar novice (5f, good) on his final run for Harry Charlton; sold for 30,000gns in October and gelded; made a fine start for new connections when second of 14 in a Class 3 handicap at Thirsk three weeks ago (5f, good), showing bright speed; 2lb higher now but has a handy draw and this is just his second handicap.
runners.comment: Good 2nd at Thirsk on stable/handicap debut last month; up 2lb but speedy and well drawn

runners.name: Proof
runners.spotlight: Effective on good to firm ground but his best turf results have come on a slower surface; got the better of Ambishio at Southwell on reappearance four weeks ago; found plenty for pressure there and should give it another good go.
runners.comment: Game effort to beat Ambishio at Southwell on reappearance; should go well again

runners.name: One And Gone
runners.spotlight: Just one win to his name but he boasts some solid AW form and ran well on his return to the turf when third at Sandown two weeks ago; stall 10 isn't great but he is usually a rapid starter and it's not out of the question that he'll get to the front.
runners.comment: Tricky draw here but fine gate speed was evident once again at Sandown two weeks ago

runners.name: Exclamation
runners.spotlight: Doncaster novice winner (5f, good to soft) last July and ran well for second in a soft-ground nursery at Sandown one month later; highly tried since and came up short, albeit running a good race when fifth in the Group 2 Flying Childers; neither start this year suggests he is the answer today.
runners.comment: Capable of fair form on his day but neither run in 2026 suggests he is the answer here

runners.name: Kolkata Knight
runners.spotlight: Won twice as a 2yo, notably a 5f nursery (good to soft) at Haydock in September; gelded afterwards; made a promising seasonal return at Wolverhampton in March, narrowly failing to reel in one who has since boosted the form; appeals as being ahead of his mark, despite a 2lb rise for Wolverhampton, but stall 12 isn't ideal.
runners.comment: Promising return at Wolverhampton; likely to be ahead of his mark but stall 12 is tricky

runners.name: Ambishio
runners.spotlight: Improved for a gelding operation, winning a Southwell handicap in January and running well in defeat twice last month (closely matched with Proof on Southwell form); has the inside stall and should make a bold bid.
runners.comment: Improved for a gelding operation; sharp 5f suits; major player from the inside stall

runners.name: Champonie
runners.spotlight: The winner of a 6f maiden at Newcastle on her debut in February and a 6f fillies' novice at Wolverhampton in March; on both occasions she has been slowly away before producing a strong finishing effort and almost certainly better than the bare form; missing the break at 5f in a competitive handicap may cause an issue but her ceiling is yet to be established.
runners.comment: 2-2, both wins coming over 6f on AW; needs to be sharper at the start but she's unexposed

runners.name: Arlo's Girl
runners.spotlight: She ran out the comfortable winner of a fillies' novice at Lingfield last August (5f, AW); flopped in a Southwell novice in January; unexposed but she makes her handicap debut in a competitive race.
runners.comment: Ready win (5f, AW) on debut last summer; well held at Southwell in January; unexposed

runners.name: Temple Of Athena
runners.spotlight: C&D winner as a 2yo (good to soft); improved effort to win at Wolverhampton on reappearance and then ran well enough in the face of a stiff task at Bath last month; raced too freely over 6f here on Wednesday before weakening; this trip is more suitable and she has fared well with the draw again.
runners.comment: C&D winner; too free in the early stages before dropping away over 6f here on Wednesday

runners.name: Inca Heights
runners.spotlight: Didn't really kick on from his debut win as a 2yo (5f, good) but his two defeats in the autumn came in good company; gelded over the winter and still has some potential but stall 13 is tough on his handicap debut.
runners.comment: Struggled in warm races on final two runs in 2025; poor draw for handicap debut

races.race_name: Chester Roman Day On 30th May Apprentice Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Patagonia Girl
runners.spotlight: Won twice (once here) over about 1m2f last June and returned from layoff in prime form at Yarmouth (1m2f, good) last month, losing out only by a short head; still unexposed over 1m4f but has awkward draw.
runners.comment: Went close over 1m2f on seasonal debut; unexposed over 1m4f but has an awkward draw

runners.name: Nightsinwhitesatin
runners.spotlight: Consistent filly who won twice over 1m2f last summer and posted career-best form when close second on last month's reappearance at Thirsk (1m4f, good; clear third has won since); well drawn and Toby Moore looks excellent value for his 7lb claim against other apprentices; good chance.
runners.comment: Ran big race in defeat on last month's seasonal debut and has a good draw; big player
runners.owner: David Kilburn, Mrs Corbett, Chris Wright

runners.name: Dance Time
runners.spotlight: 6-19 on AW but 0-9 on turf; came second here (1m6f, heavy) in September but returns to turf on a 5lb higher mark, and still has something to prove on this surface; sports first-time blinkers.
runners.comment: Second on heavy ground here in September but has done all her winning on AW

runners.name: L'Eagle Aid
runners.spotlight: Usually races prominently but came from the rear, having fluffed the start, to dead-heat over C&D (good; subsequently awarded outright win) last summer; now only 1lb above that mark and returns here after respectable run in quite valuable Musselburgh race last month; one to consider.
runners.comment: Won over C&D last summer and returns here after a respectable run; one to consider

runners.name: Sportingsilvermine
runners.spotlight: Placed five consecutive times on turf/AW last summer/autumn and now well handicapped on those exploits but finished well down the field on first two appearances this year (1m6f/1m2f); hood removed after one use.
runners.comment: Well down the field on both appearances this year and needs to turn things around

runners.name: Newtown Duke
runners.spotlight: Led close home to open account at Newcastle (1m, AW) in November, his tenth start; not seen again since but has a favourable draw and could plausibly have untapped potential over middle distances on turf.
runners.comment: Off since narrow 1m AW win in November but middle distances on turf seem okay

runners.name: Imperial Trooper
runners.spotlight: Placed on three of his four starts this year, most recently when keeping on well for third on last month's turf return at Redcar (1m2f, good to firm); will presumably be dropped in again from his wide draw today; might need some luck but looks well worth a crack at 1m4f.
runners.comment: Will need some luck from stall 16 but ran well over 1m2f last time and 1m4f might suit

runners.name: Siouxfonic
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 5yo who gained his wins in a bumper and a 1m4f AW maiden; disappointed when dropped to 1m2f for last month's handicap debut and needs a good boost from the new cheekpieces here.
runners.comment: Bumper/1m4f AW maiden winner; disappointed on handicap debut (1m2f); cheekpieces now added

runners.name: Terrorise
runners.spotlight: Ended last season in poor form and, despite a subsequent wind op, also looked out of sorts when back in action last month; needs to get career back on track and is probably better suited by longer trips than this now.
runners.comment: Needs to get career back on track and is probably better suited by longer trips now

runners.name: Secret Beach
runners.spotlight: Won twice over this trip for Ben Brookhouse last year but his stable/hurdle debut in December was underwhelming and he made a low-key return to the Flat last month; others have much less to prove.
runners.comment: Safely held on stable/hurdle debut in December and made low-key Flat return last month

runners.name: Renesmee
runners.spotlight: Led close home at Chelmsford (1m, AW) in March and has held her form well since (7f-9.4f); should be fine back on turf but current mark demands a career-best performance on this first attempt at 1m4f.
runners.comment: In good form on AW this spring but career-best effort needed on this first attempt at 1m4f

runners.name: Oman
runners.spotlight: Ended 2025 with emphatic AW win at Wolverhampton (9.4f, AW) and came second on both AW starts in January (1m2f/9.4f); absent since lesser effort in February and not ideally drawn today but has two course wins (including a division of this race in 2024) on his CV; could have a say.
runners.comment: Off since below-par AW run in February but this dual course winner is not ruled out

runners.name: Wise Counsellor
runners.spotlight: 11-race maiden; left Andrew Balding after running poorly in a seller in September and showed no significant promise in three subsequent hurdle races for new stable.
runners.comment: Has struggled over hurdles for current stable; makes limited appeal on Flat return

runners.name: Maasai Mara
runners.spotlight: Lost his way over hurdles last year and made only a mildly encouraging return to the Flat when keeping-on fourth of seven at Wolverhampton (1m4f, AW; first-time hood) last month; not the most obvious answer.
runners.comment: Lost his way over hurdles last year and recent Flat return was only mildly encouraging
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late K W Bell

runners.name: Al Mootamarid
runners.spotlight: Won a low-grade handicap hurdle last summer but this is his first Flat run since 2024, and he needs to prove he's worth his current mark.
runners.comment: Won a low-grade handicap hurdle last summer but this is his first Flat run since 2024

races.race_name: Peroni Nastro Azzurro 0.0% Handicap

runners.name: Albaydaa
runners.spotlight: Came on plenty for each 2yo run, making all in a 7f maiden on Wolverhampton AW in November; plenty to like about her reappearance in a 6f handicap at Newmarket (good) recently, where she initially failed to pick up but came home nicely to finish a close third of 13; should come on for the run and go well back over 7f; yard has won three of the last seven runnings.
runners.comment: Won 7f AW maiden on final 2yo start; staying-on 3rd in 6f Newmarket handicap latest

runners.name: May Angel
runners.spotlight: Ready winner of 7f novice on Southwell AW before highly tried in Group races on final 2yo starts; gelded and had wind surgery in the close season and returned with a useful effort to be third in a well-contested Newbury handicap (7f, good; just behind the penalised Colori Forever) last month; looks sure to be involved off the same mark.
runners.comment: Won 7f AW novice as 2yo; good 3rd in well-contested 7f Newbury handicap on return; solid

runners.name: Goldwork
runners.spotlight: Best 2yo form when winning a 6f novice at Goodwood last May and he was back near that level when a keeping-on third of eight on Kempton AW (6f) in April; heavy ground was probably against him when well held in his only 7f run but he still has stamina to prove and in tough company, too.
runners.comment: Won 6f novice at Goodwood last May; promising return on AW; stamina to prove at 7f

runners.name: Illy's Roo
runners.spotlight: All three wins over 7f, on turf and the AW, the latest on his yard debut in March; however, he's high in the weights now and his recent Newbury run (7f; 50-1) suggests another uphill battle awaits.
runners.comment: All 3 wins at 7f, on turf/AW; recent Newbury defeat suggests another uphill battle awaits

runners.name: Colori Forever
runners.spotlight: Impressive winner over 7f on Southwell AW reappearance; made favourite for a good 7f Newbury handicap since, when 6lb well in under a penalty, but failed to get into the action soon enough, finishing second to a more prominently ridden winner (May Angel just behind); still has potential despite running off his new mark and this stiff 7f can suit.
runners.comment: Impressive winner on AW comeback; creditable 2nd under penalty since; now 6lb higher

runners.name: Ay Up Duck
runners.spotlight: Unraced as a 2yo; improving with racing and he forged clear when getting off the mark on Southwell AW (back at 7f) recently; should progress further but he's taking on rivals who have already shown their mettle in turf handicaps and this is a tough start to his career on grass.
runners.comment: Forged clear on Southwell AW (7f) latest; faces a tough start to handicaps on turf debut

runners.name: Queen Tamara
runners.spotlight: Won 7f Chepstow maiden, then ran a stormer from the front in a 7f Newmarket handicap (good to firm) last September where she tended to change direction under pressure but showed no lack of fight in dividing subsequent Listed and Group-race winners; promising but this may be needed.
runners.comment: Smart run from the front when dividing subsequent Listed/Group winners on final 2yo start

runners.name: Angel Of Anfield
runners.spotlight: All turf races at sprint trips on good to firm and good, vigorously ridden when making all at Windsor last August from one who went on to better things; a similarly useful effort when runner-up on Kempton AW in October but he lacks a recent run and has a new trip to contend with.
runners.comment: All races at sprint trips, making all at Windsor in August; good AW run after; off since

runners.name: Aqua Bear
runners.spotlight: Had some useful form in Ireland as a 2yo but didn't need to improve on that when making a successful yard debut on Newcastle AW (7f) last November; gelded since; non-runner when due to make handicap debut in the 3.40 at Chester yesterday.
runners.comment: Winning yard debut on AW (7f) in November; non-runner in 3.40 at Chester yesterday

runners.name: Synners Kid
runners.spotlight: Greatly improved for the step up to 1m and switch to handicaps on final two starts for Joseph O'Brien, winning both attempts (yielding to soft/soft) last autumn; sold for 110,000gns since and gelded; has very different conditions today and goes without his regular tongue-tie.
runners.comment: Won both 1m handicaps for J O'Brien in the autumn; sold 110,000gns since; conditions query

races.race_name: Juddmonte EBF Fillies' Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands B/C/D) (EBF Restricted Qualifier) (GBB)

runners.name: Bint Archange
runners.spotlight: Early March sixth foal who is a half-sister to four winners; 45,000euros yearling; 4-1 for her debut when third of eight in a traditionally warm event at Newmarket (5f, good) last month; sets a clear standard on that form.
runners.comment: 4-1 when third in traditionally warm event (5f, good) last month; sets a clear standard

runners.name: Wondervision
runners.spotlight: Fairly late March foal by first-season sire Minzaal; 50,000gns yearling; fourth foal; half-sister to Listed-placed 7f AW 2yo winner Whip Cracker (RPR 103); dam once-raced half-sister to winners Advanced (smart 6f-7f) and Wunder (1m1f Group 3), out of German 7f winner; yard is already 2-5 with juveniles this year so interesting to see how she fares in the market ahead of this introduction.
runners.comment: Late March foal; 50,000gns yearling; yard already 2-5 with 2yos this year; watch market

runners.name: Half A Hoof
runners.spotlight: Fairly late March foal by Lucky Vega; 31,000euros yearling; eighth foal; half-sister to four winners including Fullness Of Life (Italian 6f-1m including 2yo/Group 3; RPR 99) and Sopran Brescia (Italian 1m1f-1m3f); dam Italian 6f 2yo winner, half-sister to HK 1m Group 1 winner Gentlemen Only; betting should be a good guide to expectations ahead of her debut.
runners.comment: Fairly late March foal by Lucky Vega; 31,000euros yearling; betting should be a good guide

runners.name: Magnesium
runners.spotlight: Late January foal by Ubettabelieveit; £30,000 yearling; seventh foal; closely related to 5f winner I'm Next (including 2yo/AW; RPR 86), half-sister to winners Estepona Sun (7f AW 2yo/1m2f turf; 89) and Gundogan (6f; 77); dam unraced; yard has a low strike-rate on turf with its 2yos over the last five years; still worth a check of the betting on this debut.
runners.comment: Late January foal by Ubettabelieveit; £30,000 yearling; yard has low 2yo turf strike-rate

runners.name: Romidijo
runners.spotlight: March foal by Ardad; 32,000euros yearling; first foal; dam 5f/6f winner (including 2yo; RPR 94), half-sister to 1m 2yo winner Orgetorix, out of 5f winning half-sister to 5f Listed winner Stern Opinion; has the pedigree to make a juvenile and market clues should be heeded.
runners.comment: March foal by Ardad; 32,000euros yearling; market clues should be heeded on debut

runners.name: Tallahassee Lassie
runners.spotlight: February foal by Tasleet; 38,000gns yearling; fifth foal; half-sister to winners Paborus (Group-placed 7f including AW; RPR 117) and Sixteen Tons (French 1m5f); dam 5f/6f winner (91), half-sister to HK 7f winner Ka Ying Excellent, out of 7f/1m winner; yard's 2yos generally improve for a run but still needs monitoring in the betting.
runners.comment: February foal by Tasleet; 38,000gns yearling; yard's 2yos generally improve for a run

runners.name: Summer Triangle
runners.spotlight: Late January foal by Land Force; dam 1m/1m2f winner (RPR 69), half-sister to 5f 2yo Group 3 winner Armor, out of useful French 1m winner; yard not noted for winning 2yo newcomers but worth a market check.
runners.comment: Late January foal by Land Force; yard not noted for winning 2yo newcomers

runners.name: Big Hitter
runners.spotlight: Early February foal by Starspangledbanner; 65,000euros yearling; first foal; dam 6f 2yo winner (including Group 2; RPR 100), half-sister to winners Oliver Hardy (1m2f/1m4f) and Dajla (Spanish 1m-1m3f), out of maiden sister to 6f/7f 2yo Group 2 winner Strategic Prince, family of Oaks winner Ramruma; first juvenile runner of the year for her yard and holds appeal on pedigree; market a likely guide.
runners.comment: February foal by Starspangledbanner; 65,000euros yearling; paper appeal so watch betting

runners.name: Resemblance
runners.spotlight: Late March foal by first-season sire Bayside Boy, who is already 2-5; £45,000 yearling; seventh foal; half-sister to winners Significantly (Listed-placed 5f/6f including 2yo/AW; RPR 111), Salvuccio (5f/6f including 2yo/AW; 103) and Dutch Finale (6f AW; 80); dam Group-placed 5f/6f 2yo winner (97); has appeal on paper and betting should reveal more on this debut.
runners.comment: March foal by first-season sire Bayside Boy, already 2-5; £45,000 yearling; likely type

runners.name: Beautiful Rainbow
runners.spotlight: Early April foal who was 11-2 for her first go when fifth of nine at Beverley (5f, good) a fortnight ago; displayed inexperience that day before looking to get the message late; should do better.
runners.comment: April foal; 11-2 when fifth of nine at Beverley (5f, good) 15 days ago; should do better

runners.name: Dee's Star
runners.spotlight: Fairly late January foal who showed ability when sixth of 13 at Bath (5f, good; 25-1) on her debut three weeks ago; respected but she's no certainty to confirm placings with Past Passion or Miss Moneypit, who were just a head and a head behind.
runners.comment: Sixth of 13 (5f, good) on debut; no certainty to beat Past Passion or Miss Moneypit again

runners.name: Miss Moneypit
runners.spotlight: Early January foal who was nibbled at in the betting when 12-1 but suffered a luckless debut when eighth at Bath (5f, good; Dee's Star sixth, Past Passion seventh) three weeks ago; yard introduced five-time winning juvenile Anthelia (including Listed National Stakes, Super Sprint and Group 3 Dick Poole) in that race last year; big contender to do better this time.
runners.comment: Early January foal; nibbled in market before luckless debut; big contender to step forward

runners.name: Past Passion
runners.spotlight: April foal who was 18-1 when missing the break and finishing seventh at Bath (5f, good; Dee's Star a head in front, Miss Moneypit a head behind) on her debut three weeks ago; contender to step forward on this second start.
runners.comment: Missed break when finishing alongside Dee's Star and Miss Moneypit on debut; can improve

races.race_name: Palmer & Co. Champagne Maiden Fillies' Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Tarrant
runners.spotlight: Dam's winning offspring include 1m1f Group 3 winner Viareggio; little market interest in her before recent Newbury debut (7f, good) but she held a good position throughout when third of 11; hard to assess the worth of that newcomers' race but she should come on for the run; major contender.
runners.comment: Promising 3rd of 11 on Newbury debut (7f); very likely to improve; major contender

runners.name: Gone By
runners.spotlight: 140,000euros yearling; unraced dam's winning siblings included a Listed winner at 1m5f; easy to back on Newbury debut (1m, soft) in October but she was the only one to make a race of it with a promising stablemate (finished third in the Cheshire Oaks on Wednesday), looking decidedly less wised up than the winner; interesting on return.
runners.comment: Promising 2nd to smart stablemate at Newbury (1m, soft) in sole 2yo start; interesting

runners.name: Light Of Paris
runners.spotlight: 500,000gns yearling; out of Listed winner whose successful offspring include Group 1 and Listed winners, but this one made a low-key start on Kempton AW (1m3f; 6-1) last month when dropping right out; ought to have some potential but major step forward is a must.
runners.comment: 500,000gns yearling; well-bred 3yo but she finished remote on AW debut (1m3f)

runners.name: Bintknight
runners.spotlight: 200,000gns foal; sixth foal; by Pinatubo; winning siblings include 1m2f AW winner Queen Of The Skies (RPR 100) and Cape Breton (1m AW 2yo/1m4f turf; 94); dam 1m winner (97), half-sister to 1m4f Group 2 winner Frankly Darling; trainer has fair strike-rate in maidens but this run may be needed.
runners.comment: 200,000gns foal; useful pedigree; trainer has fair strike-rate in maidens; may need this

runners.name: Cool Girl
runners.spotlight: 100,000euros yearling; second foal; by Pinatubo; dam twice-raced (runner-up at 1m4f; RPR 76) half-sister to winners Pakistan Star (Hong Kong 1m2f/1m4f Group 1) and Ninas Terz (useful German 6f 2yo); market can guide.
runners.comment: 100,000euros yearling; out of half-sister to Group 1 winner in Hong Kong; market helpful

runners.name: Pierpoint
runners.spotlight: 33-1 for recent Newbury debut over 7f where she never threatened to play a part but nevertheless hinted at ability, albeit 5l behind stablemate Tarrant; open to some improvement, probably when handicapping.
runners.comment: 33-1, hinted at ability on Newbury debut (7f) but 5l behind stablemate Tarrant

runners.name: Salamis Bay
runners.spotlight: Minor promise in ordinary 6f races on the AW this year and although there's stamina on the dam's side of the pedigree she does have a lot to find on the bare results.
runners.comment: Just minor promise in ordinary 6f races on AW this year but there is stamina on dam's side

runners.name: Antiquarian
runners.spotlight: Finished a remote last of five runners at Newmarket (6f; 66-1) on sole 2yo run; not sure to stay this far on pedigree and ordinary handicaps are likely to be the aim; has lots to prove.
runners.comment: Finished remote at Newmarket (6f) on sole 2yo start; trip query on pedigree; lots to find

races.race_name: Only Peroni Nastro Azzurro 0.0% Handicap

runners.name: Kodiac Thriller
runners.spotlight: Had a good season for new yard in 2025, winning on second start and running two excellent races at Ripon when returned to 6f, notably when first home on his side in the Great St Wilfrid (good to firm) in August; confirmed his liking for that course when winning there three weeks ago and needs to prove he's the same horse elsewhere.
runners.comment: Best form at Ripon, 2nd in their top handicap last August, then won there latest

runners.name: Elements Of Fire
runners.spotlight: Has done really well front-running over 7f on the AW for new yard, winning all three starts; has more to prove up another 5lb and dropped to 6f on the return to turf but he's perfectly capable of giving it a good shot on grass, having run well under a penalty in a good novice at Doncaster last spring.
runners.comment: 3-3 over 7f on AW for new yard; acts on turf; no surprise if he goes well in first 6f run

runners.name: Photosynthesis
runners.spotlight: Three wins for two previous yards and the first for this one when winning narrowly at Newmarket (6f, good) in September, following a protracted slog with the runner-up; comes here fit from the AW but he'll need a bit extra to win in a higher grade than at Newmarket.
runners.comment: Four wins, the latest for this yard at Newmarket (6f, good); needs extra in higher grade

runners.name: Betsen
runners.spotlight: Quite useful on turf in Ireland; kept almost entirely to AW since moving to this yard in the autumn but he might well have been involved in the finish at Leicester (6f, good to firm) two weeks ago but for being pushed nearly over the rail a furlong out; interesting to see what he comes up with here.
runners.comment: Fair turf form in Ireland; kept mostly to AW of late; badly hampered back on turf latest

runners.name: Walson's Law
runners.spotlight: Winless for previous yard but he won his first two turf starts for this one in 2025, over 1m at Windsor and 7f at Newbury (both on good to firm); it's possible that good to soft ground proved his undoing when last seen in August but a layoff since raises questions as does the return to 6f.
runners.comment: Won first 2 turf starts for the yard, over 1m and 7f; off since August; trip query over 6f
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Lady Cobham

runners.name: Brosay
runners.spotlight: Two 6f wins at the end of last summer, at Windsor (good to firm) and over C&D (good to soft); just 2lb higher than for the latter but he's hard to assess now, having been well held over an inadequate 5f on his yard debut (AW) then eased following serious interference at Newbury; too much to prove just now but one to keep an eye on.
runners.comment: Two 6f wins last summer, including C&D; had excuses for heavy defeats for new yard

runners.name: Hucklesbrook
runners.spotlight: Ran well on C&D return in 2025 before winning his next two over 6f, at Leicester (good to firm; made all) and York; his form took a downward turn after but his recent Newbury fourth was a step in the right direction and he's only 2lb higher than for the York win; looks good for a prominent show.
runners.comment: Just 2lb higher than for the second of two 6f wins in 2025; ran well on return; claims

runners.name: Toyotomi
runners.spotlight: First three French wins came at about 1m but better form when winning a Listed race at about 5f last April; took time to find his feet for this yard but he couldn't get out of a scrum at Goodwood (6f, good) in October and ran well for a long way on Newcastle AW last month; the likely strong pace will suit and he has a good run in him.
runners.comment: Listed winner in France (about 5f; has won at 1m); likely strong pace will suit

runners.name: Bolo Neighs
runners.spotlight: Wide-margin winner of a valuable sales race at the Curragh as a 2yo; hard to find the right race in a handful of starts since but he ran two fair races on the AW at the start of the year and this stiff 6f should be more up his street; can figure.
runners.comment: Won valuable sales race in Ireland as 2yo; two fair 6f runs on AW this year; can figure

runners.name: Saint Lawrence
runners.spotlight: Hit the handicap heights when winning the Wokingham over C&D in 2023 on debut for Archie Watson, then close up in the Group 1 Maurice de Gheest; desperately hard to place in handicaps since but he's had wind surgery since last seen in October and, even at this late stage, he might well turn it on for Kevin Ryan off a 17lb lower mark than at the start of last year.
runners.comment: Won the Wokingham over C&D in 2023; struggling now but well treated for new yard

runners.name: Mandurah
runners.spotlight: Winning 2yo debut was followed by a stout effort in a Group 3 over C&D (also good to firm) when leading from some way out and looked like holding on but couldn't quite sustain her effort; has rarely had her ground since but she ran well against an improver on AW (7f) in October on final start for the Crisfords; sold for 150,000gns; she might well have a good 4yo season.
runners.comment: Good to firm suits; useful C&D form in Group 3 as a 2yo; new yard; interesting

races.race_name: Ascot Hospitality Handicap

runners.name: Hallo Spaceboy
runners.spotlight: Consistent in four starts, including win at Newcastle (5f) in November and when second to Arry Up at Kempton (6f, also AW; stumbled start) on handicap debut/reappearance one month ago; acts on turf and shouldn't be far away from same mark.
runners.comment: Second to Arry Up (AW) latest; acts on turf and shouldn't be far away from same mark

runners.name: Arry Up
runners.spotlight: Fine stamp who is unbeaten since contesting handicaps; made all to land his third such race when defying penalty to score at Kempton (6f, AW; Hallo Spaceboy second, Night Mission fifth) one month ago; also won on turf (good to soft, acts on good) and doesn't have to lead; impossible to ignore in current mood.
runners.comment: Big boy who's firmly on the up; landed hat-trick latest and fancied to complete four-timer

runners.name: Empress Olivia
runners.spotlight: 28-1 winner of Ripon maiden (6f, good) in August and built on close reappearance second when beating 12 others in handicap over that C&D (good to soft) three weeks ago; evidently likes that track but has form elsewhere and she's not ruled out despite 5lb rise.
runners.comment: Won 6f Ripon handicap three weeks ago; not ruled out despite 5lb rise

runners.name: Wedonttelllies
runners.spotlight: Beat 13 others to land Kempton novice (AW) in September but only fifth when favourite for handicap debut at Newmarket (also 7f, good to firm) the following month; subsequently gelded and his comeback run at Bath (5.5f, good but almost certainly softer) can be written off; booking of William Buick (1-4 for yard this season at start of the day) catches the eye.
runners.comment: Only fifth on h'cap debut last term but comeback can be ignored & William Buick takes over

runners.name: Night Mission
runners.spotlight: Clear ability when placed all three starts (6f, good/good to soft) at two; gelded and stuck on from the rear when fifth to Arry Up at Kempton (6f, AW) on reappearance/handicap debut one month ago; will need to have come on for the outing but that's not beyond the realms.
runners.comment: Stuck on from rear when fifth to Arry Up (6f, AW) on reappearance/h'cap debut a month ago

runners.name: Moscow Power
runners.spotlight: All starts at 6f; best of two runs last term came when chasing home two smart Godolphin juveniles on debut at Haydock (good to firm) last May; gelded before justifying a shade of odds-on at Chelmsford (also 6f, AW) on return in March; should do better again and this mark looks fair ahead of his handicap debut.
runners.comment: Promising debut and justified short price on return (6f, AW); fair mark for h'cap debut

runners.name: Fair Y Isle
runners.spotlight: All starts on AW; picked up Wolverhampton fillies' novice in January and just held at Southwell (also 6f) on her handicap debut last month; this is tougher and a 2lb nudge demands more; still not ruled out.
runners.comment: Just held on h'cap debut (6f, AW); 2lb nudge demands more but still not ruled out

runners.name: Red Snapper
runners.spotlight: Last three starts at Bath and went close there (5.5f, good to firm; first-time cheekpieces) in September; narrowly off the mark (5f, heavy) the following month and handled the rise in grade well when second (5.5f, good; kept on) on his comeback three weeks ago; return to 6f unlikely to represent a problem and should run his race again.
runners.comment: Improved in cheekpieces and solid second on return; should run his race again back at 6f

runners.name: Star Of Albion
runners.spotlight: Went close on handicap debut in September; odds-on but still improved on fifth start to win Lingfield novice (6f, AW; first-time blinkers) in January; didn't back that up when down the field in classy affair over that C&D one month later but return to turf should be no problem.
runners.comment: No show in classy affair in February but return to turf should be no problem

runners.name: Bellini Spirit
runners.spotlight: Four starts, one each at 5f, 6f, 7f and 1m; best form by some way when second over this trip at Chelmsford (AW; 40-1) on second go for Anthony Carson, which could be a straw to clutch at, but form in a maiden and handicap for this yard leaves her with work to do.
runners.comment: Best run came at 6f but maiden/h'cap form for this yard leaves her with work to do

runners.name: Rogue Temptation
runners.spotlight: Haydock novice (6f, good) winner who made pleasing handicap debut when third of 12 at Doncaster (6.5f, good to soft) in September; finished last on final two starts of last year but reappearance fifth of 13 at Ripon (6f, good to soft; first-time blinkers) wasn't a bad effort three weeks ago; now needs to build on that.
runners.comment: Reappearance fifth wasn't a bad effort three weeks ago; needs to build on that

runners.name: Afton Down
runners.spotlight: Small step forward when comfortably winning Wolverhampton novice (6f, AW) on his return three weeks ago; will need more again on this handicap debut but that's by no means unlikely and he ran well on quick turf in October; respected.
runners.comment: Won novice (6f, AW) on return; sound turf no issue and he's respected on handicap debut

runners.name: Sizzling Seixas
runners.spotlight: Stepped up on her debut in March when making all in five-runner novice at Kempton (also 6f, AW) three weeks later; those tactics will be tough to repeat in this field but she brings plenty of potential into this turf and handicap debut.
runners.comment: Just two starts and won latest (6f, AW); firmly in the picture on h'cap/turf debut

runners.name: Twilight Glow
runners.spotlight: Second of 12 at Newbury (6f, only start on good to soft) was much the best of four runs last year; gelded and not disgraced when fourth at Kempton (7f, AW; 25-1) on his reappearance at the start of last month; needs to build on that.
runners.comment: Not disgraced when fourth (7f, AW; 25-1) on return last month; needs to build on that

runners.name: Duskaura
runners.spotlight: All starts at 5f; no impact in two Listed races after winning Ripon fillies' maiden (good) for Archie Watson last term; sights lowered for this yard but form of three handicap runs on Wolverhampton's AW suggest this extra furlong will need to give her quite the boost.
runners.comment: All starts at 5f; form of three AW h'caps suggest extra furlong needs to give a fair boost

runners.name: Dublin Bay
runners.spotlight: More exposed than most but he won third handicap when making all at Wolverhampton (7f, AW; just held on) four weeks ago; also won twice at 6f on sound turf so these conditions should be no problem, but a 4lb rise might be.
runners.comment: Won 3rd h'cap (7f, AW) last time; these conditions no problem but 4lb rise might be

runners.name: Secret Santa
runners.spotlight: All starts on good; beaten under 3l by subsequent Windsor Castle winner on 5f debut last May then finished third to subsequent Chesham winner at Newbury (6f) a fortnight later; latter event worked out extremely well so disappointing he couldn't get the job done at Bath (odds-on) the following month; not seen since but good chance of making a successful handicap debut/reappearance if ready and tapping back into that initial promise.
runners.comment: Disappointed when last seen; Newbury third a year ago makes him fascinating on h'cap debut

runners.name: Who Is Alice
runners.spotlight: Looked sure to progress in nurseries after winning Chepstow maiden (7f, good to firm) in July so it was disappointing she went 0-5 when inconsistent in that sphere subsequently; left Jack Channon (same owners) after finishing last at Wolverhampton (6f, AW) in October; new yard going well but she'll need to bring her A-game to this return.
runners.comment: 0-5 in handicaps; new yard going well but she'll need to bring her A-game to this return

runners.name: Ballybunion
runners.spotlight: Good step forward from debut when runner-up at Ripon, albeit beaten 5l in four-runner event, in September and backed that up when second again at Yarmouth (also 6f, good) the following month; has been gelded and interesting to see how he goes in the market ahead of this reappearance/handicap debut.
runners.comment: Runner-up twice (also 6f, good) last term; gelded ahead of this return/handicap debut
runners.owner: I Foster, D Fish, W Dale & S Dale

runners.name: Blue Orbit
runners.spotlight: Off the mark on second start (5f, good to firm) and ran respectably on handicap debut when beaten 3l at Glorious Goodwood (6f, good to soft; not clear run) in August; gelded before sharing second (5f, AW) on reappearance in March and again didn't have the cleanest shot when fourth at Bath one month later; not ruled out.
runners.comment: Solid second on comeback and didn't have the cleanest shot latest; not ruled out

races.race_name: Peroni Nastro Azzurro 0.0% Style Handicap

runners.name: Blue Courvoisier
runners.spotlight: Won 7f Leicester novice in September and was creditable second on last month's seasonal/handicap debut at Newmarket (1m, good), despite racing a bit too freely; that form has recently been well advertised by the third and this fellow is probably still on a good mark after 1lb nudge.
runners.comment: Creditable second on seasonal/handicap debut at Newmarket and only 1lb higher here

runners.name: Raging Raj
runners.spotlight: Won two AW nurseries in November (1m/7f) and was very respectable seventh in good Newmarket handicap (1m, good) contested by a few of these last month but might be vulnerable to less-exposed rivals again.
runners.comment: Last month's Newmarket run was very respectable but he's more exposed than some of these

runners.name: Luzon Heights
runners.spotlight: Gained only win from seven starts in a run-of-the-mill AW nursery but was close up in fifth on last month's seasonal debut at Newmarket (1m, good), and is closely matched with Blue Courvoisier and Arbaawy on that form.
runners.comment: Gained only win in run-of-the-mill AW nursery but ran well on last month's seasonal debut

runners.name: Arbaawy
runners.spotlight: A maiden after seven starts but only beaten about 1l when running-on fourth (just behind Blue Courvoisier) on last month's seasonal/handicap debut at Newmarket; no surprise if he's on the premises again.
runners.comment: 0-7 now but was close fourth in good Newmarket handicap on last month's reappearance

runners.name: Tiger Power
runners.spotlight: Defied a penalty when winning Southwell novice (1m, AW) on seasonal debut in February but ran poorly when switched to a handicap at Newmarket (1m, good) last month; needs new visor to help him rebound.
runners.comment: Won two of first three starts but ran poorly on handicap debut; visor now added

runners.name: Peel Park
runners.spotlight: Yet to progress from last summer's 6f maiden win but made quite encouraging seasonal debut when keeping-on sixth of 14 in Class 2 Newbury handicap (7f, good) last month, and looks well worth a crack at 1m.
runners.comment: Made quite encouraging seasonal debut over 7f and looks well worth a crack at 1m

runners.name: King's Trust
runners.spotlight: Delivered on earlier promise when winning five-runner Southwell maiden (7f, AW; first-time blinkers) in October, his third start; contests much deeper race here but still has potential; cheekpieces tried today.
runners.comment: Won five-runner AW maiden in October and can improve again in handicaps this year
runners.owner: HM The King & HM The Queen

runners.name: Exotic Baby
runners.spotlight: Won 1m AW maiden in September but safely held in both handicaps, latterly when upped to 1m2f for seasonal debut in March; subsequently gelded and now drops back in trip and sports new cheekpieces for turf debut.
runners.comment: Safely held in both handicaps; drops back in trip and tries cheekpices on turf debut

runners.name: Northern Empire
runners.spotlight: Made all in quite valuable 7f Lingfield novice in January, his third run, but ran poorly when among market leaders for valuable 6f Newcastle handicap on Good Friday; back up to 1m for turf debut; not easy to predict.
runners.comment: 7f AW novice winner; disappointed over 6f on handicap debut; back at 1m for turf debut

runners.name: Spirit Of Saxony
runners.spotlight: Placed in novice races on first two starts (6f/7f) and again displayed clear promise when fifth of 25 in Newbury maiden (1m, good) last month, racing prominently throughout; likely contender on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Prominent throughout when fifth of 25 in 1m Newbury maiden last month; handicap debut

runners.name: Outback Heat
runners.spotlight: Allowed to dictate at steady pace when winning Newcastle maiden (1m, AW) on debut in February and showed better form when third of six behind useful rival at Yarmouth (1m, good) last month; probably needs to take another step forward on this handicap debut but remains unexposed.
runners.comment: AW maiden winner; better form when third at Yarmouth last month; now makes handicap debut

runners.name: Seu Jo
runners.spotlight: 1-6 when based in France, signing off with win in 1m AW claimer in March; still unexposed over this trip but this looks a fairly tough assignment on British debut.
runners.comment: Won 1m AW claimer on last of six starts in France; makes British debut today

races.race_name: Peroni Nastro Azzurro 0.0% Apprentice Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Educator
runners.spotlight: Three wins on turf (good/good to firm) at up to 11.5f; also went fairly close at this trip on AW in January; respectable third at Bath (2m1f, good to firm; Lusaka second) on return from two-month break last time; might build on that.
runners.comment: Respectable third (2m1f) back from two-month break last time; might build on that

runners.name: Francesco Baracca
runners.spotlight: Best form on AW but has won on turf and it was his best effort on grass when third of six at Newbury (2m, good; plugged on) three weeks ago; each-way shout.
runners.comment: Best effort on turf when plugging-on third last time (2m, good); each-way shout

runners.name: Gemmari
runners.spotlight: All starts on AW; sole start at 2m resulted in short-priced flop at Chelmsford in November; won at that track (1m6f) next time but he let favourite backers down again when beating one home over that trip at Wolverhampton in March; 1m4f caught him out last time but others look safer.
runners.comment: Let favourite backers down either side of 1m6f AW win in January; 2m a query; turf debut

runners.name: Tarbat Ness
runners.spotlight: All five wins at 2m, including three on soft going, latterly at Catterick in October; went back-to-back at Newcastle (AW) at the start of the year and fourth at Ripon (2m, good) on latest outing was close to his best; same mark, so needs to find extra, but he can't be ruled out.
runners.comment: All five wins at 2m; latest fourth (2m, good) was close to his best; can't be ruled out

runners.name: Circuit Breaker
runners.spotlight: Can set the pace; twice runner-up at Wolverhampton (1m6f/16.5f) during the winter and a good third of 14 in competitive event at Lingfield (2m, also AW; Tarbat Ness sixth) on Good Friday; turf no problem and he should give a good account.
runners.comment: Good third in competitive event (2m, AW) latest; turf no issue; can give a good account

runners.name: Expressionless
runners.spotlight: Three wins at 1m6f and it was his sixth win in total when beating four others over 1m4f at Leicester (good) last month; has won off this mark before and 2m looks worth a go; respected.
runners.comment: Has won at 1m6f; scored over 1m4f (good) latest; mark fine and 2m worth a go; respected

runners.name: Believitanducan
runners.spotlight: Placed in all four staying handicaps (includes 2m win) for Jessica Macey in 2024; three hurdle wins since joining this yard and a good effort on the Flat when second at Kempton (2m, AW) in April 2025; respected on his return to this sphere.
runners.comment: Sole Flat start for yard when second (2m, AW) in April 2025; has won over hurdles since

runners.name: Naval Tribute
