runners.spotlight: Beaten a nose, a head and a neck in three bumpers; earned his mark over 1m and predictably did better upped in trip when scoring at Kempton (1m3f, AW; first-time cheekpieces) in March; rebounded from a blip next time when going in again at Beverley (1m4f, good to firm; first-time visor) a fortnight ago; dam half-sister to St Leger winner Brian Boru and this new trip could unlock more again; key player.
runners.comment: 2-3 since handicapping up in trip in headgear; now tackles 2m and it could unlock more

runners.name: Lusaka
runners.spotlight: Scored for the sixth time when making all at Newbury (13.5f, good to firm) off 1lb lower in July and gave it a fair go from the front when second at Bath (2m1f, good to firm; Educator third) three weeks ago; same mark and should make his presence felt.
runners.comment: Fair go from the front when second (2m1f) last time; same mark and he's not dismissed

runners.name: Aajej
runners.spotlight: Unplaced both turf starts and both 2m races; inconsistent on AW but took advantage of career-low mark to score by a neck over 1m6f in January on final start for the Crisfords; sported first-time blinkers that day and sold for 31,000gns the following month; cheekpieces are today's headgear and he has something to prove back on turf at 2m.
runners.comment: Sold for 31,000gns after winning last time; turf and 2m are queries; first cheekpieces

runners.name: Calvert
runners.spotlight: Sole success came when evens for a Bath novice (1m2f, good to soft) as 2yo; respectable efforts across five starts at 1m6f and 2m in 2025 and he went mighty close at Wolverhampton (1m6f, AW) in March after which he had a run over hurdles; strike-rate isn't a positive but he has the ability to figure.
runners.comment: Went very close (1m6f, AW) in March; strike-rate a negative but he has ability to figure

runners.name: Galactic Charm
runners.spotlight: In decent form when last seen on the Flat for Michael Bell but has been hurdling since December 2024 for this yard; did well as a juvenile in that sphere but last season didn't go well; mark fine if this return to the Flat perks him up.
runners.comment: Regressive over hurdles last term but mark fine if this return to the Flat perks him up

races.race_name: Molson Coors Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Fidendum
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced and headstrong 7yo who inexplicably jinked right and ran out at fourth fight when in front at Hereford last month; however, he was second to a useful rival over C&D (good) on his penultimate appearance in March 2025 and has good form claims if he behaves himself.
runners.comment: Ran out when last in action and comes with risk but has good form claims

runners.name: Edelak
runners.spotlight: Showed useful form when winning 1m4f Flat maiden at last summer's Galway festival (wore blinkers/tongue-tie), and reportedly had breathing issue when only sixth of seven in Listed juvenile hurdle on stable debut in December (no headgear); had wind surgery soon afterwards and could still have a good future over jumps; sports first-time cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Useful Flat racer who had excuse (breathing issue) for underwhelming hurdle debut

runners.name: Popalot
runners.spotlight: Shaped with significant promise when third of five in tactical course bumper in November but managed only fifth of eight when back here for another bumper last month; improvement required on hurdle debut.
runners.comment: Showed ability in two course bumpers but improvement is required on this hurdling debut

runners.name: Berks And Bucks
runners.spotlight: Not disgraced when midfield in Warwick bumper in December but was pulled up on first two hurdling starts (2m3f/2m4f); drops back in trip with tongue-tie added here.
runners.comment: Pulled up on both hurdling starts; back in trip with tongue-tie added here

runners.name: Heights Lane
runners.spotlight: 35,000euros 3yo; fifth foal; dam unraced half-sister to useful bumper and 2m hurdle/chase winner Publican; does not need to be anything special to play a part on debut but probably best watched all the same.
runners.comment: Does not need to be anything special to play a part on debut but probably best watched

runners.name: Pour Tout Le Monde
runners.spotlight: Always behind when 200-1 for hurdle debut over C&D last month.
runners.comment: Always behind when 200-1 for hurdle debut over C&D last month

runners.name: Calibos
runners.spotlight: Fair on the Flat at around 1m2f for the Kublers last year and made satisfactory stable/hurdle debut when fourth (albeit well held) against own age-group at Huntingdon (2m, good to soft) in March; ideally needs to settle better but the forecast faster ground here ought to suit; each-way claims.
runners.comment: Fair Flat racer who made satisfactory hurdle debut in March; considered each-way

runners.name: Kenobi
runners.spotlight: Well beaten on bumper debut in November and pulled up at triple-digit odds on both hurdling starts.
runners.comment: Pulled up at triple-digit odds on both hurdling starts

runners.name: Pink Walls
runners.spotlight: Lowly rated Flat maiden; had excuse when pulled up on stable/hurdle debut (saddle slipped) but was tailed off when 250-1 for Stratford maiden 19 days ago.
runners.comment: Tailed off when 250-1 for Stratford maiden 19 days ago

runners.name: Papa Oscar
runners.spotlight: Became disappointing on the Flat and has struggled on both hurdling starts.
runners.comment: Became disappointing on the Flat and has struggled on both hurdling starts

races.race_name: Lucas Machinery Novices' Limited Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Bunker Bay
runners.spotlight: Made a noise after an anonymous run at Perth last time; placed in two C&D class 5s before that, albeit never looking that potent a late threat when second in the most recent of those (good); bit more required.
runners.comment: Never quite getting there in time when second over C&D in April; anonymous run since.

runners.name: Lord Of The Glance
runners.spotlight: Improved for the switch to 2m good-ground handicaps, looking well placed to defy a 9lb hike for his earlier Stratford success when unseating at the last at Plumpton latest; up 1lb more but case is obvious.
runners.comment: A win and a last-flight exit (every chance) in 2m good-ground handicaps lately; player.

runners.name: Granny B
runners.spotlight: 0-16 over hurdles with limitations well exposed, but last month's 25l third at Hereford can be marked up as she'd tried to make all (not her usual run style) off the back of a 170-day absence; place claims.
runners.comment: Last month's Hereford 25l third is a better effort than it perhaps reads; place claims.

runners.name: Beorma
runners.spotlight: No improvement for handicaps following 15 months out, including returned to a sound surface latest; down another 2lb but unpersuasive.
runners.comment: No improvement for handicaps following 15 months out; down another 2lb but unpersuasive.

runners.name: Character Testing
runners.spotlight: 0-14 over hurdles, and C&D second in January (good to soft) was the fourth time he's been beaten under a length in this sphere; not obviously ungenuine for all that, however, and he'll likely be just right for this after a couple of recent Flat spins; no ground concerns.
runners.comment: Fit from the Flat; went as close as ever to a hurdles win over this C&D in January.

runners.name: Bouquet De Paris
runners.spotlight: Came off worst in a pace battle with Granny B and ultimately 6l behind her at Hereford latest (2m, good); could still race more sparingly, but 3lb lower today and fared okay in a C&D juvenile last winter.
runners.comment: 6l behind Granny B latest after trying to take her on; did okay over C&D once last winter.

runners.name: Susiesparkle
runners.spotlight: 0-10 over hurdles and still operating too far below her 2024-25 level to consider; pulled up behind Bunker Bay over C&D latest.
runners.comment: 0-10 over hurdles; pulled up behind Bunker Bay over C&D latest and couldn't consider.

runners.name: Klapton Boy
runners.spotlight: RPR of 99 for his early-2025 Wetherby novice third (2m4f, soft) increasingly looks an outlier; wind surgery hasn't been able to prevent two further weak finishes since, and hopes now rest with the tongue-tie.
runners.comment: Weak finisher on last four runs, including two since wind surgery; tongue-tie now added.

runners.name: Macho Sun
runners.spotlight: Fifth and about to post one of his better efforts when falling at the last behind Bunker Bay latest (C&D), not his first spill over hurdles; work on from further out of the weights today even with a clear round.
runners.comment: Last-flight faller behind Bunker Bay latest (C&D); further out of the weights today.

runners.name: Allibaba
runners.spotlight: Offered nothing during the briefest of comebacks in autumn 2024, and another long absence since; now 25lb lower than when first entering handicaps, however, and remains in the same ownership now as then; check for market confidence ahead of this stable debut.
runners.comment: Returning from another long absence, but could be worth a market check; stable debut.

runners.name: Made All
runners.spotlight: Tapeta winner in March (1m4f), and fair effort against a pace bias back on turf latest; no chances taken with initial hurdles mark (peak RPR 78 to date), but yet to encounter a sound surface in this discipline.
runners.comment: Stiff-looking opening mark, but may step up on previous efforts granted a sound surface.

races.race_name: Tiff And Ang Go West Stakes Handicap Chase

runners.name: El Muchacho
runners.spotlight: Holding form well since January's Catterick success (2m3f, good), actually achieving more on RPRs when a close third at Newcastle latest; still well treated on his 2023-24 peak form, and no ground concerns.
runners.comment: Holding form well since January's Catterick win (2m3f, good); mark, trip, ground all fine.
runners.owner: T J Stubbins and M E Sowersby

runners.name: Jasmin De Cotte
runners.spotlight: Gifted a 2m4f Uttoxeter race two starts back (soft), and 3m was clearly too far last time; not wholly convincing, but his one win on merit came at this time of year in 2024 on good (2m4f); tongue-tie added.
runners.comment: Drop back from 3m of last run makes sense; 2m4f winner on good this time two years ago.

runners.name: Forsa Bay
runners.spotlight: Ostensibly a chance on the form of either of last season's third places off up to 5lb higher, one of them logged over 2m3f here; not a conspicuously strong finisher either time, however, which doesn't augur well stepping up to a new trip here; cheekpieces replace visor.
runners.comment: Third here in December (2m3f, good to soft); needs to finish off races better; new trip.

runners.name: Lone Soldier
runners.spotlight: 0-20 and mixed signals all season over what his present optimum trip might be; staying on but no chance with winner when second at Warwick latest (2m, good), and this contest looks a shade stronger.
runners.comment: Staying on but held when second at Warwick latest (2m, good); this looks a bit stronger.

runners.name: Cloudy Wednesday
runners.spotlight: A win and (in this race) a half-length second, each off 2lb lower, to show from two C&D visits (good) last summer; plenty in his favour again once more, though a Fakenham flop latest needs forgiving.
runners.comment: Half-length second in this last year, and a C&D win soon followed; below par run latest.

runners.name: Isle Of Sark
runners.spotlight: Both hurdles wins (one at today's trip) came on sound surfaces last year, and April's staying-on Sedgefield chase third (2m5f, good) is his best effort to date for current connections; coming to the boil again.
runners.comment: Sedgefield chase third latest was a good step back in the right direction; turn nearing.

runners.name: Let Me Go Boys
runners.spotlight: No impact over 2m when last seen, so hike back up in trip is no surprise; 7lb lower than when first trying fences, and just a matter of whether this first start since November is needed; blinkers off.
runners.comment: No surprise he's back up in trip; to be seen whether first run since November is needed.

races.race_name: Summer Plate Ladies Day Saturday 18th July Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Bumpy Evans
runners.spotlight: Free-going front-runner with 5-9 strike-rate over fences; unable to justify favouritism but still ran well before weakening into third at Newton Abbot (2m5f, good) last month, after a break, and could be hard to catch here if that run has brought him on.
runners.comment: Free-going front-runner with 5-9 strike-rate over fences; could be hard to catch

runners.name: Classic King
runners.spotlight: Better known as a hurdler and has had some jumping issues over fences (even when he won) but he kept on well for clear second in first-time cheekpieces at Stratford (2m6f, good) six weeks ago and gets another chance off the same mark here.
runners.comment: Has had some jumping issues but his clear second at Stratford in March was a good effort

runners.name: Annsam
runners.spotlight: Returned to form with clear win off much-reduced mark at Ludlow (3m1f, good to soft) in December but three non-completions have followed, most recently when he was pulled up back at Ludlow last month; others look safer.
runners.comment: Won off much-reduced mark in December but three non-completions have followed

runners.name: Giovanni Change
runners.spotlight: Six-time course winner; not the force of old and has become inconsistent but looked in good nick when scoring at Wetherby (3m, good) in March, and last month's C&D third was perfectly respectable.
runners.comment: Six course wins; respectable third over C&D since Wetherby success in March; a possible
runners.owner: Readers & Wiggy, The 8 Amigos & J Burns

runners.name: Courtland
runners.spotlight: Seven-time chase winner (once here); returned from break with disappointing effort last month but current mark is workable if subsequent wind operation and/or first-time cheekpieces prompt a return to form.
runners.comment: Disappointed last month; had wind op since and now sports cheekpieces; on workable mark

runners.name: Ira Hayes
runners.spotlight: Well beaten in 3m4f Dublin National on chasing debut in March and almost certainly fortunate when winning Tramore beginners' chase (2m5f, good to yielding) last month, given he was in fourth (albeit close up) when the front three came two grief two out; that said, he's with a respected stable and is still unexposed over fences.
runners.comment: Fortunate when winning last month but with respected stable and unexposed over fences

races.race_name: Victor Lucas Memorial Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Coumeenoole
runners.spotlight: The same combination of a sharp track, good ground and stayers' trip of his peak Irish effort similarly worked the oracle at Bangor last time; a 9lb re-raise demands another big step forward, though that's not impossible having won with far more in hand than the near-3l verdict may infer.
runners.comment: Value for plenty further than the near-3l verdict at Bangor latest (2m7f, good); up 9lb.

runners.name: Huffin An A Puffin
runners.spotlight: A big gelding seemingly tricky to keep right, for an initial pair of wins at about 2m4f about 22 months apart have been followed by two pallid efforts (coughed after the first, unimproved by wind surgery the second); unraced over either this far or a track this sharp.
runners.comment: Won first two at about 2m4f, but problems twice since; new trip to contend with today.

runners.name: Idefix De Ciergues
runners.spotlight: Not wholly straightforward, but appreciated the return to hurdling sufficiently to record a new personal best in victory at Uttoxeter latest (2m7f, good to soft); blinkers debuted there will need to work at least as well now returned to his previous highest mark; has won on good.
runners.comment: 5lb rise for Uttoxeter score (2m7f) puts him back on his highest mark; has won on good.

runners.name: Ryebridge
runners.spotlight: The dead-heat victory and (off today's mark) a close fourth in 2m3f Carlisle 0-110s on his last two outings rate personal best efforts; more needed again up in class, and didn't convince over 3m in Irish points.
runners.comment: In personal best form win or lose at Carlisle lately (2m3f); trip and class questions.

runners.name: Porter In The Park
runners.spotlight: Found a warm Haydock class 2 stayers' handicap a step too far last month (22l last of nine), breaking a run of place finishes dating back to the autumn; races off her last winning mark back down in class.
runners.comment: Run of place finishes halted latest; races off her last winning mark back down in class.

runners.name: Escapologist
runners.spotlight: C&D scorer in March (good to soft) on his first realistic staying assignment, and 3lb badly in when not quite up to defying a penalty next time; of renewed interest after a break, and a winner on good also.
runners.comment: C&D scorer in March (good to soft); still early days for him as a staying handicapper.

runners.name: Ebony Warrior
runners.spotlight: Two good-ground novice wins in 2024-25 (2m, 2m5f); stamina for 2m7f still about to be put to the test when unseating at Worcester exactly a year ago, but two runs after didn't advertise his staying credentials.
runners.comment: Dual novice winner on good; hasn't really advertised his staying credentials since.
runners.owner: DL Adams, JA Adams & G McPherson

runners.name: Champagne Twist
runners.spotlight: 2024 EBF Final winner off 7lb higher; unseated too far out at Uttoxeter latest to say if and where he may have figured, but poor winter up to that point tempers enthusiasm either way; overlooked.
runners.comment: 7lb below mark of EBF Final win in 2024; not in the form to capitalise; unseated latest.

runners.name: Dramatic License
runners.spotlight: Last-gasp Downpatrick maiden hurdle winner last August (2m6f, good), but yet to offer anything for his newest connections.
runners.comment: Irish maiden hurdle winner last August (2m6f, good); no show yet for newest connections.

runners.name: Into The Park
runners.spotlight: Ran below his mark even when placing over the winter, and none the wiser as to his staying credentials after a subdued effort behind Tiger Orchid at Wetherby latest (3m); tongue-tie added on this stable debut.
runners.comment: None the wiser yet as to what he has to offer as a stayer; has left the Hobbs/White team.

runners.name: Tiger Orchid
runners.spotlight: All but broke a long losing run at Wetherby two runs ago (3m, good to soft), with Into The Park and Dramatic License both well behind; track type and going fine, and still comfortably below his last winning mark.
runners.comment: Good start so far for Edmunds yard, almost ending losing run in March; trip/tack fine.

races.race_name: Fiver Friday Raceday Friday 12th June Conditional Jockeys' Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Nottodaybobo
runners.spotlight: Ended 2025 in poor form but kept on well to take third of 13 at Newcastle (2m1f, good to soft; refitted tongue-tie) in March, after a break, and may benefit from moving back up in trip here; contender if able to build on that recent effort.
runners.comment: Ended 2025 in poor form but his keeping-on third at Newcastle in March was much better

runners.name: Blue Bear
runners.spotlight: Chase winner in early 2024; generally disappointing since returning from long absence this year but ran well for a long way before weakening into fourth in C&D (good) contest won by De Deli Counter last month and has possibilities if first-time hood helps him to settle better in early stages today.
runners.comment: Ran well for a long way when fourth behind De Deli Counter here last month

runners.name: Top Flight Century
runners.spotlight: Scored on stable debut at Sedgefield (2m4f, good to soft) on Boxing Day but two lesser efforts have followed this year, the latter back at Sedgefield last month; now has a point to prove.
runners.comment: Won on stable debut on Boxing Day but two lesser efforts have followed this year

runners.name: De Deli Counter
runners.spotlight: Came good over hurdles at eighth attempt, winning from the front over C&D (good) in first-time cheekpieces last month; subsequent 5lb rise doesn't look excessive and further progress is possible; must be considered.
runners.comment: Scored over C&D in first-time cheekpieces last month and 5lb rise does not look excessive

runners.name: Assorda
runners.spotlight: Ex-French mare; fortunate to win small-field Catterick novice (1m7f, good) for Paula Smith on British debut in March 2025 and soundly beaten in all four subsequent handicaps, the last three times for new stable this year.
runners.comment: Soundly beaten on all three starts for new stable this year

runners.name: Matoury
runners.spotlight: Absent since pulled up at Catterick on New Year's Day and far too inconsistent to rely upon but scored over C&D (good) last June and is now 1lb below that mark; can't be ruled out.
runners.comment: Too inconsistent to rely upon but won over C&D last year; can't be ruled out

runners.name: Bargain Hunter
runners.spotlight: Showed no significant promise when a big-priced outsider for three 2m maiden/novice hurdles last season; upped in trip for handicap debut; considered only if much stronger in the betting this time.
runners.comment: Showed no significant promise when big-priced outsider for three 2m maiden/novice hurdles

runners.name: Below The Belt
runners.spotlight: Well beaten in three maiden/novice hurdles towards end of last season, albeit some positives can be taken from last month's fifth at Chepstow (2m, good to soft); upped in trip for handicap debut; improvement required.
runners.comment: Well beaten in three maiden/novice hurdles towards end of last season; handicap debut

races.race_name: Harrington Concrete & Quarries Optional Claiming Race

runners.name: Divelment
runners.spotlight: Rated 69; placed twice in turf maidens at Roscommon last year; in okay form on AW when last seen earlier this year; gelded since and new headgear combination goes on; not straightforward but best treated by race conditions and the one to beat if ready after break.
runners.comment: Rated 69, best treated by race conditions, new headgear combo, the one to beat if fit

runners.name: Incredible Army
runners.spotlight: One of two for yard; sprang a surprise in three-runner rated race at Dundalk (6f) in February; well beaten twice since, including in 1m Gowran claimer latest on turf debut; needs to get back on track.
runners.comment: Poor twice since surprise 6f rated race win at Dundalk, needs to get back on track

runners.name: Borora Aura
runners.spotlight: Improved effort on second start at Gowran (1m, soft) last month but was still beaten 11.5l; not bred for this longer trip and others make more appeal.
runners.comment: Better effort on latest at Gowran (1m), not bred for this longer trip, others preferred

runners.name: Darwin's Angel
runners.spotlight: Best run for Stephen Thorne a 2l fourth of 14 in a Down Royal nursery last September off 61; soundly beaten in three runs for this stable; others look more convincing.
runners.comment: Soundly beaten in three runs for this stable, others more convincing

runners.name: Personified
runners.spotlight: One of three for yard; promise the last twice in 1m handicaps at Dundalk and Bellewstown (soft); pedigree suggests there's a good chance she'll stay this far; leading form chance, though concerning that Declan McDonogh jumps ship.
runners.comment: Solid runs in 1m handicaps last twice, should stay on breeding, solid form claims
runners.owner: Mrs John Magnier & Mrs A M O'Brien

runners.name: Moon Aura
runners.spotlight: Beaten 12.5l on debut in a 1m Leopardstown maiden; was 8.75l behind the winner in a 7f Limerick maiden (yielding) latest; not sure this longer trip will suit on pedigree; others preferred.
runners.comment: Modest efforts in maidens at Limerick and Leopardstown, stamina query upped in trip

runners.name: Lilo Pelekai
runners.spotlight: Very ordinary form so far on turf and the AW; yard looks to have a better chance with Incredible Army.
runners.comment: Very ordinary form so far on turf and AW, Incredible Army yard's best chance

runners.name: Teologia
runners.spotlight: One of three for yard; has shown ability in both starts; beaten 11.5l when fifth of 10 on debut at Gowran last October; midfield on seasonal return at Navan; sharper for that and place shout if building on it.
runners.comment: Ability both starts, strip fitter for comeback run at Navan, place shout if builds on that

runners.name: Aurevoir Tristesse
runners.spotlight: From a fine family but didn't show a whole lot in two Dundalk maidens earlier this year; now drops to claiming company in first-time cheekpieces; more needed.
runners.comment: Well bred but ordinary form in two Dundalk maidens, cheekpieces need to eke out more

runners.name: Schoolyard Days
runners.spotlight: Earned an official rating of 54 after three maiden runs at Navan (6f, good), Naas (5f, soft) and Dundalk (6f) on latest in February; stamina to prove upped markedly in trip.
runners.comment: Rated 54 after three maiden runs at 5f-6f, moves markedly up in trip, stamina a query

runners.name: Royal Sapphire
runners.spotlight: One of three for yard; looked of little account in four maiden runs at Dundalk; gelded since the latest one in February; hard to fancy, though interesting that McDonogh rides.
runners.comment: Poor form in four Dundalk maidens, gelded, interesting that McDonogh rides

races.race_name: Brian Forkan Construction Maiden

runners.name: Glamazon
runners.spotlight: 0-9 on turf; placed twice last year, in a Roscommon maiden (7.5f, yielding) and a Sligo handicap off 68 (6f, gd-yld); yet to race at beyond a mile; beaten 5l off 67 in Limerick handicap latest; cheekpieces need to eke out more.
runners.comment: Placed in maiden and h'cap last year, beaten 5l off 67 latest, up in trip, cheekpieces on

runners.name: Maxminelli
runners.spotlight: Best effort in two runs last year a modest 8.5l fourth of 14 at Dundalk (1m); weakened in the closing stages on seasonal return at Navan (1m2f, heavy); a drying track could help his cause, as could the first-time tongue tie; chance.
runners.comment: Promise at Dundalk last year, heavy may not have suited latest, tongue tie on, chance

runners.name: Cascade Canyon
runners.spotlight: Okay effort when midfield on debut at Navan (1m2f, heavy); didn't back it up there second time out; much more needed, though heavy rain would boost his claims.
runners.comment: Midfield on debut at Navan, not as good there latest, much more needed, rain would help

runners.name: Premier Princess
runners.spotlight: Long odds and no impact in maidens at the Curragh and Leopardstown; may need a bit more time.
runners.comment: Soundly beaten at huge odds in first two maidens, may need more time

runners.name: Lord Aus
runners.spotlight: Slowly away from the gates both starts at Navan and Gowran; may need a bit more time and experience.
runners.comment: Slowly away both starts at Navan and Gowran, may need more time and experience

runners.name: Pass The Buck
runners.spotlight: 0-4 but has hinted he has races in him, including when making some late progress over 1m at the Curragh (soft) on his seasonal return; could improve for this longer trip and slightly better ground; interesting for in-form yard with decent strike rate here on the Flat (13-60).
runners.comment: 0-4 but has shown potential, step up in trip a plus, sharper for latest, interesting

runners.name: Avec Grand Plaisir
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten in maidens at the Curragh and Leopardstown five months apart; will qualify for handicaps after this.
runners.comment: Soundly beaten in first two maidens, handicaps beckon after this

runners.name: Priorstown
runners.spotlight: Glimmers of ability in 1m maidens at Navan (heavy) and Gowran (soft); hinted as though he was ready for a step up in trip last time and gets that here; could also improve for better ground (brother Annerville won on good for this yard).
runners.comment: Glimmers of ability both starts, step up in trip a plus, better ground could suit

races.race_name: SIS, Supporting Irish Racing Maiden

runners.name: Mano Chicago
runners.spotlight: Rated 85; has returned from mammoth layoff with encouraging runs in a Gowran maiden (Cracking Cloud a nose behind) and in a handicap at Cork (10.5f, gd-yld); should be thereabouts returned to maiden company in first-time blinkers.
runners.comment: Two good runs since return from long absence, blinkers go on, thereabouts
runners.owner: Samuel M Mencoff & Anthony Paul Smurfit

runners.name: Wild Sapphire
runners.spotlight: Huge chance on the form of his debut second at the Curragh behind Expanded; two runs this season haven't been at that level, however, and he was well held behind Mano Chicago and Cracking Cloud at Gowran last time; visor replaced by cheekpieces; more needed.
runners.comment: Well held behind two of these at Gowran, visor replaced by cheekpieces, needs more

runners.name: Navinsky
runners.spotlight: Fine effort on second start last year when a neck second at Navan (1m2f, good); tailed off on seasonal return at the Curragh (1m, heavy); cheekpieces now go on; could rebound once there isn't too much rain.
runners.comment: Promise at Navan last year on good ground, bombed out on heavy latest, could rebound

runners.name: Marmeladova
runners.spotlight: Standout effort in five runs last year a nose second at Navan (1m, good); didn't back that up the next twice and a bit to prove on return from a 209-day break.
runners.comment: Hasn't built on promise of Navan second last year, off 209 days, a bit to prove

runners.name: Cracking Cloud
runners.spotlight: Related to winners and plenty to like about his staying on third on debut at Gowran (9.5f, soft) when a nose behind the runner up Mano Chicago (Wild Sapphire 2.5l behind); unexposed; enters calculations.
runners.comment: Fine effort on debut when a nose behind Mano Chicago, enters calculations

runners.name: Zammawar
runners.spotlight: Impossible to fancy on the evidence of four heavy maiden defeats at triple figure odds.
runners.comment: Impossible to fancy on the evidence of four heavy defeats at triple figure odds

runners.name: Nelliestar
runners.spotlight: 0-10 and yet to run to a higher RPR than 28; impossible to make a case for.
runners.comment: 0-10 and yet to run to a higher RPR than 28, impossible to fancy on form

runners.name: Glendower
runners.spotlight: Dual-purpose; off since last May; placed in four British Flat maidens for former yard at 9.5f-1m2f on good to firm ground; likely best watched after absence.
runners.comment: Dual-purpose, best British form on fast ground, off since last May, likely best watched

runners.name: Ryans Hope
runners.spotlight: Ran to an RPR of 63 when a 3.5l third on only start for Iain Jardine in a Newcastle maiden in February 2024; blinkers worn that day discarded; likely best watched on first run for new stable after 442-day absence.
runners.comment: RPR of 63 sole British start, blinkers discarded, new yard, watch after 442-day break

runners.name: North Ocean
runners.spotlight: Well bred and showed promise on sole start at Dundalk in November 2024; has been gelded; market can guide on return from mammoth layoff.
runners.comment: Well bred, promise sole start in Nov' 2024, off since, gelded, market best guide

races.race_name: MG Plant Sales Handicap

runners.name: Hiltons Pass
runners.spotlight: Long odds and no sign of winning potential in a trio of maidens at 6f-1m on varying ground; hard to make a case for on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Huge odds and well beaten in maidens, hard to make a case for in first handicap

runners.name: Accelereight
runners.spotlight: Modest form in maidens; similar story on seasonal return and handicap debut at Navan (1m2f, heavy) in March; cheekpieces worn that day discarded; could improve on this better ground and yard in form.
runners.comment: Modest run on heavy on h'cap debut, might improve for better ground, yard in form
runners.owner: Any Excuse For A Holiday Syndicate

runners.name: Survivor's Code
runners.spotlight: Didn't show much for Emmet Mullins but off the mark on second start for this stable at Gowran (7f, soft); didn't back that up off revised mark latest and blinkers now go on; needs to rebound stepped back up in trip.
runners.comment: Off the mark on penultimate, didn't back it up off revised mark latest, blinkers go on

runners.name: Swift Charm
runners.spotlight: No show in maidens and has finished well down the field in both handicap starts; steps up in trip after 10l defeat over 1m at Leopardstown latest; hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Beaten 10l at Leopardstown latest, steps up in trip, hard to make a case for on form

runners.name: Tenacious
runners.spotlight: Big improvement in first turf handicap run at Gowran (1m, gd-yld) three days ago, beaten 0.5l under a positive ride; cheekpieces go on; hope that she'll stay this far on dam side of pedigree; go well once this doesn't come too soon.
runners.comment: Much improved latest, same mark now, cheekpieces go on, should go well again

runners.name: Guildhall Graduate
runners.spotlight: Small glimmers of promise in maidens at 7f-1m; well beaten in first handicap when last seen at Dundalk (1m) in January; down 3lb and in class now; market check advised.
runners.comment: Flashes in maidens, poor on h'cap debut, off since January, drops in class, check market

runners.name: Balance Of Trade
runners.spotlight: Good fourth on handicap debut at Gowran last October (7f, heavy); kept on from the back for a 5.75l third of 13 at Bellewstown (1m, soft) latest; previously ran well in 1m Gowran claimer; worth another go at this sort of trip on the evidence of that effort; rain a plus.
runners.comment: Back to form with staying on third at Bellewstown (1m), worth another go at this trip

runners.name: A Pretty Penny
runners.spotlight: Best effort yet in third handicap run at Dundalk (1m) on latest in March, beaten 2l when fifth of 8; didn't look like she was crying out for this longer trip, however.
runners.comment: Beaten 2l when fifth at Dundalk latest (1m), moves up in trip, stamina a query

runners.name: Meriden
runners.spotlight: Never threatened in a trio of maidens at 6f-8f on soft and on the AW; potential improver on handicap bow; market can guide.
runners.comment: No threat in maidens on soft/AW, may improve for handicaps, market can guide

runners.name: Point Cartwright
runners.spotlight: Little to write home about in three maiden runs at 7f-1m on the AW and on soft ground latest; from a talented family so could do better now switched to handicaps; check the market.
runners.comment: Ordinary maiden form, from good family so could improve for h'caps, check market

runners.name: Femme Beauty
runners.spotlight: Maiden hit with 7lb rise for Gowran claimer third on penultimate and no impact back in a handicap at Gowran 3 days ago; same mark here.
runners.comment: Raised 7lb for claimer third penultimate, struggled back in handicap latest, same mark now

runners.name: Coul Dore
runners.spotlight: Huge odds and soundly beaten in maidens at 7f-1m on the AW and on yielding ground; can only be watched on handicap and stable debut.
runners.comment: Huge odds and no impact in maidens, can only be watched on handicap/stable debut

runners.name: Dawson Street
runners.spotlight: Modest form in first two handicaps at Dundalk at the backend of last year; dropped 6lb and returns from a 156-day absence; best watched unless market speaks.
runners.comment: Modest AW h'cap form last year, off since Dec', down 6lb, watch unless market speaks
runners.owner: Roderick Ryan & Mrs John M Weld

races.race_name: Burleigh Accountancy Group Handicap

runners.name: Lough Leane
runners.spotlight: All six wins came on the AW when trained in Britain; standout run for this yard came on opening day of season when runner-up in 26-runner Curragh handicap (heavy), most recently didn't seem to get home over this trip at Gowran last month (soft); stamina concerns remain.
runners.comment: Didn't seem to get home over this trip at Gowran last month (soft); stamina doubts remain

runners.name: Flying Fortress
runners.spotlight: Won C&D maiden on this card last year on debut for the Cromwell yard and later added Listowel win on heavy; goes well fresh so has to be respected given his record here.
runners.comment: C&D maiden winner last year goes well fresh so has to be respected given his record here

runners.name: Glen To Glen
runners.spotlight: Curragh maiden winner for Jim Bolger last season (6f) and pick of form for current yard when runner-up in 1m2f handicap at same venue; useful hurdler (won at Cork on testing ground) but well held in Fred Winter; Flat return was scuppered when badly hampered early on in Curragh premier handicap last month; drops in grade here with visor tried.
runners.comment: Recent Flat return scuppered when badly hampered; drops in grade here with visor tried

runners.name: Medieval Night
runners.spotlight: Pick of six runs for Dermot Weld when runner-up in 1m1f Leopardstown handicap (good); bought for 11,000gns and soon beaten on yard debut at Limerick last month after showing up early; entitled to come on for that, though may want better ground.
runners.comment: Entitled to come on for recent comeback run but may want better ground

runners.name: Hexagonal
runners.spotlight: Some decent maiden runs in defeat last term; opening handicap efforts have been underwhelming, hanging left at the Curragh last time (tongue-tie worn then now discarded); still has it to prove off this mark.
runners.comment: Opening handicap efforts have been underwhelming; still has it to prove off this mark

runners.name: Merlin The Wizard
runners.spotlight: Three wins in Britain in 2023 at up to 1m; regressive in last two seasons but best run for some time at Dundalk in March when good second over 1m; didn't seem to quite get home back on turf at Leopardstown last month and stamina concerns trying this longer trip for the first time.
runners.comment: Best form at 1m, obvious stamina concerns now upped in trip

runners.name: Expound
runners.spotlight: All three Flat wins have come over an extended 1m1f on good ground, including this race last year off 1lb lower; opening two runs this term on AW were okay, well held latest under a 10lb claimer at Limerick; respected back here but ideally wants better ground.
runners.comment: Last year's winner respected back here but ideally wants better ground

runners.name: Annie's Angel
runners.spotlight: Won a Galway maiden on second start for Joseph O'Brien in October 2024 but no joy since, although second to Flying Fortress in  Listowel handicap; three runs over hurdles since joining Ado McGuinness before tame Curragh effort last time; new headgear combination tried and handicapper giving her a chance.
runners.comment: Nothing yet for current yard, new headgear combination tried
runners.owner: Conor O'Connor & Colum Currie & Mark Devenny

runners.name: La Tulipe Noire
runners.spotlight: Won back-to-back 1m handicaps at Cork and Roscommon last autumn (yielding/soft), yet to hit that level this term in four attempts; returns to a longer trip.
runners.comment: Yet to hit form this season and still 9lb above her last winning mark

runners.name: Loingseoir
runners.spotlight: Seven-time winner at trips from 7f-1m4f, most recently at the Curragh last October; wasn't beaten too far on seasonal return there last month but didn't count six days later at Limerick; Billy Lee takes over.
runners.comment: Veteran has a bit to find on recent runs but Billy Lee now takes over
runners.owner: W Keeling & M C Grassick & Mrs M J Grassick

races.race_name: McGrath Limestone Works Handicap

runners.name: In The Minus
runners.spotlight: Limerick win last summer came over 7f (good); similar level of form hurdling (won Galway maiden), jumping has let him down of late; unproven over this far.
runners.comment: Unproven over this far and wants better ground

runners.name: Sayidah New Bay
runners.spotlight: Maiden placed a couple of times last autumn over longer trips on soft; encouraging recent seasonal return over an inadequate 1m at Limerick should have her ready for this.
runners.comment: Encouraging recent seasonal return over an inadequate 1m should have her ready for this

runners.name: Majestic King
runners.spotlight: Well-bred Godolphin cast-off makes a quick reappearance after landing a touch over a mile at Gowran on Tuesday in ridiculously easy fashion, value for much more than the 3.5l winning margin; due for a hefty rise so connections' quick to bring him out again under mandatory 7lb penalty and will take all the beating if staying this longer trip (bred to).
runners.comment: Effortless Gowran winner on Tuesday; will take all the beating if staying this longer trip

runners.name: She's Ideal
runners.spotlight: Has returned with a couple of good runs, staying on fourth over a mile at Gowran and stayed 1m2f trip well when only beaten a neck at Sligo five days ago, having led inside final furlong; slight drop in trip here could suit and cheekpieces tried.
runners.comment: Just denied over a bit further at Sligo 5 days ago; cheekpieces added; major player

runners.name: Jawhary
runners.spotlight: Having a good time of it of late on the AW, recording a third win there last month off 55; balance of turf form is on decent ground so could struggle in these conditions.
runners.comment: Having a good time of it of late on the AW but this ground possibly on the soft side

runners.name: Kartayaz
runners.spotlight: Four-time AW winner, also won on turf at Down Royal; recent AW form leaves him with plenty to find.
runners.comment: Recent AW form leaves him with plenty to find

runners.name: Calamint
runners.spotlight: No win since scoring off 2lb higher at Naas (1m, yielding) over two years ago; promising seasonal comeback second at Navan in March (1m2f) but 1m4f looked beyond her at Limerick last month; back to probably her optimum trip.
runners.comment: 1m4f looked beyond her at Limerick last month; back to probably her optimum trip

runners.name: Starting Monday
runners.spotlight: Back to form for third career win when landing Gowran handicap over this trip last month (soft) off career-low mark; 4lb rise workable but wide draw may not be.
runners.comment: Recent Gowran winner at third trip; 4lb rise workable but wide draw may not be

runners.name: Thru The Gap
runners.spotlight: Maiden has yet to trouble the judge; blinkers tried and handicapper relenting but remains hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Yet to trouble the judge; blinkers tried, handicapper relenting but remains hard to fancy

runners.name: Ethelwulf
runners.spotlight: Had one good run for the Gosdens, namely a Yarmouth second; nothing yet for current connections, last twice on soft ground at Gowran; down 14lb already this year; yard have a much stronger contender in Majestic King.
runners.comment: Nothing yet for current connections; down 14lb already this year

runners.name: Miss Australie
runners.spotlight: Ran well in 1m4f Curragh maiden last summer on final start for previous yard, nothing approaching that level of form since however, soft ground wouldn't have suited on last month's Gowran return; probably best watched.
runners.comment: Yet to convince in handicaps, remains opposable off this mark

runners.name: Piatra Neamt
runners.spotlight: First reserve; ran okay on handicap debut at Navan last October but well held twice afterwards over 1m4f; best watched on return.
runners.comment: Handicap form last autumn leaves her with lots to find; reserve

runners.name: Tero d'Rosa
runners.spotlight: Maiden shaped well in 1m2f Dundalk AW handicaps when last seen in November; could go well on turf return if handling softer conditions.
runners.comment: Decent AW placings in November; could go well on turf return if handling softer conditions

runners.name: Deuteronomy
runners.spotlight: In the form of his life last June, notching three wins on the trot at 1m/1m1f following addition of visor/blinkers; yet to hit form in three attempts this term though.
runners.comment: In form of his life last June; yet to hit form in three attempts this term though

runners.name: Smooth
runners.spotlight: Some promise in her maiden run at Cork last year, but nothing so far of note in handicaps; down 14lb from opening mark but still best watched on seasonal return.
runners.comment: Down 14lb from opening mark but still best watched on seasonal return

runners.name: Tasmanian Girl
runners.spotlight: Third reserve; surprise winner of Thurles handicap hurdle in March and has largely struggled since; reverts to the Flat with new headgear combination but 6lb out of handicap.
runners.comment: Hurdler reverts to the Flat and 6lb out of handicap; reserve

runners.name: Cuckaloo Hill
runners.spotlight: Second reserve; placed in good-ground 1m2f handicaps at Fairyhouse and Naas last summer for Paul Nolan; form so far for current yard modest, albeit mainly on unsuitably soft ground; reduced mark looks tempting.
runners.comment: Reduced mark looks tempting but wants better ground; reserve

races.race_name: BOYLE Sports Home Of The Early Payout Maiden

runners.name: Trojan Fighter
runners.spotlight: Ulysses colt shaped nicely on Navan debut in March (1m2f, heavy) but didn't build on that at Cork last month when tried in a hood and tongue-tie (both now left off), keeping on after being hampered early; Irish Derby entry looks optimistic but remains capable of better.
runners.comment: Navan debut promising, excuse at Cork; remains capable of better

runners.name: Ballyadam
runners.spotlight: High-class NH performer, most recently good fourth in BetMGM Handicap Hurdle at Cheltenham; two previous Flat runs were on the Dundalk AW, notably previous month when 2l third of 10 over 1m4f; ground no problem, not sure the tight track will suit.
runners.comment: High-class hurdler, placed on Dundalk AW penultimate; ground no problem, tight track query

runners.name: Battling Siki
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 4yo ran twice on the AW before improvement in Bellewstown maiden last month over just shy of this trip, not quite getting home on soft ground under a positive ride; considered but stamina doubts.
runners.comment: Bellewstown run puts him in the mix but there may be stronger stayers

runners.name: Diligo
runners.spotlight: Wootton Bassett colt was patently green on last month's Gowran debut, slow to break and wandering before running on strongly into fourth late on (9.5f); cheekpieces now fitted and upped in trip (half-brother a Group 3 winner at 1m6f); won't need to improve too much to play a major role here.
runners.comment: Patently green but showed enough on recent Gowran debut to suggest he'll play a major role
runners.owner: Al Shaqab Racing & AMO Racing Limited

runners.name: Oust
runners.spotlight: Palace Pier colt has shaped okay in two maidens over shorter trips; potential improver now upped in trip but may be more one for handicaps.
runners.comment: Potential improver now upped in trip but may be more one for handicaps

runners.name: Nothingbutadream
runners.spotlight: 50-1, last of 15 on last month's Bellewstown debut with a couple of these ahead; dismiss.
runners.comment: Last of 15 on last month's Bellewstown debut with a couple of these ahead; dismiss

runners.name: Chairmanforlife
runners.spotlight: E80,000 Y; fourth foal; half-brother to French 6f/6.5f 2yo winner Voodoo Magic (RPR 95); dam 5f AW 2yo winner (85); speedily-bred 3yo didn't count on last month's debut when midfield in much better-class maiden; entitled to improve for that.
runners.comment: Didn't count on last month's debut when midfield in better-class maiden; should improve

runners.name: Sir Trancealot
runners.spotlight: Finished last in maidens at the Curragh and Dundalk; likely to need more time.
runners.comment: Finished last in maidens at the Curragh and Dundalk; likely to need more time

runners.name: Goodie Girl
runners.spotlight: Three-time winning hurdler on decent ground; absent since faller at Fairyhouse in November and probably best watched on Flat debut.
runners.comment: Three-time winning hurdler absent since November; wants better ground and best watched

runners.name: Must Meet Cecil
runners.spotlight: Five-time winner over hurdles is rated 49lb inferior to Ballyadam in that discipline; belated Flat debut when well held in Bellewstown maiden last month (5.5l off Battling Ski); good bit to find.
runners.comment: Winning hurdler but lots to find on recent Flat debut at Bellewstown

races.race_name: KOG Logistics Handicap

runners.name: Carthusian
runners.spotlight: Lightly-raced 4yo was 0-5 in maidens last year but showed a fair amount of ability each time; trip/ground okay and worth considering on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Shaped well in maidens last year; trip/ground okay and worth considering on handicap debut

runners.name: Pillar Of Hope
runners.spotlight: Five turf wins include last year at the Curragh and when bolting up off 70 at Leopardstown in October; didn't count on seasonal return at the Curragh over a short enough trip; drops in class and upped to more suitable trip so has to be considered.
runners.comment: Inadequate trip last time; drops in class and more suitable trip so has to be considered

runners.name: Justenzia
runners.spotlight: Won a Clonmel handicap over just short of 1m2f last autumn and encouraging comeback run at Gowran last month over slightly shorter when second; 4lb rise for that but remains of interest.
runners.comment: Gowran comeback second raised 4lb but remains of interest

runners.name: Liberate
runners.spotlight: Won 1m2f Goodwood novice last year but safely held afterwards in handicaps for James Horton; bought for 5,000gns in December and two AW runs have been modest; seems best watched.
runners.comment: Bought for 5,000gns in December and two AW runs have been modest; seems best watched

runners.name: Amplitude
runners.spotlight: Rounded off last term with emphatic win in 9.5f Gowran maiden (heavy), pick of three handicap efforts this term when good fourth at Leopardstown (1m2f), tame enough finishing effort at Cork latest; doesn't appear an obvious candidate for this longer trip.
runners.comment: Fair mark but doesn't appear an obvious candidate for this longer trip

runners.name: Eben Zaabeel
runners.spotlight: Seven-race maiden in Britain for Charlie Hills, runner-up in AW handicaps final two starts; two runs for current yard underwhelming and now tried hooded.
runners.comment: Two runs for current yard underwhelming and now tried hooded

runners.name: Satono Chevalier
runners.spotlight: Three-time AW winner at 1m4f and finally made the breakthrough on turf in 1m2f Navan handicap in late March, positively-ridden by today's rider; tongue-tie worn then now discarded on first start for new yard; 7lb higher.
runners.comment: Navan winner raised 7lb but this rider gets a good tune out of him

runners.name: Steps In The Sand
runners.spotlight: Won three in a row last summer (1m5f/1m6f, good-yielding) and narrowly beaten over today's trip in big field at Galway; absent since October so may just need this (regular rider switches to Granville Street).
runners.comment: In fine form last summer but absent since October so may just need this

runners.name: Good Onya Mate
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 4yo showed a little in AW maiden in October but never counted over 1m there on seasonal return in March; tongue-tie fitted for handicap debut and likely improver upped to a more suitable trip for handicap debut.
runners.comment: Tongue-tie fitted for handicap debut and likely improver upped in trip for handicap debut
runners.owner: P J Morgan & Mrs S L Morgan

runners.name: Superficial
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to Group 1 winner Al Riffa not at same level, although mild promise last summer in Galway handicap and best effort yet on last month's return at Bellewstown when a clear second (third has won since) over just shy of 1m4f (soft), only worn down late; 6lb higher but respected, despite wide draw.
runners.comment: Clear Bellewstown second a career-best; 6lb higher but respected

runners.name: Migdam
runners.spotlight: Four-time winner over shorter trips and only just denied over 2m at Thurles on final start last season; recent hurdle run should have put him straight and not one to rule out.
runners.comment: Recent hurdle run should have put him straight and not one to rule out

runners.name: Mr Rango
runners.spotlight: Three-time winner at 1m2f on soft or heavy, latest over two years ago; okay runs latest two starts this term but more needed.
runners.comment: Yet to win over this far; okay runs latest two starts this term but more needed

runners.name: Avondale
runners.spotlight: Won consecutive 1m4f handicaps in 2024 at Roscommon and Galway (off 70); came back from an absence when winning over hurdles at Bellewstown last summer and ran well in novice events both starts afterwards; returns from a further 9 months' absence but goes well fresh.
runners.comment: 10lb above last winning mark; long absent but goes well fresh

runners.name: Granville Street
runners.spotlight: Only Flat win came over this trip at Galway in 2023 but went close at Navan last month, every chance in a three-way photo when beaten a neck (1m5f, good-to-yielding); 3lb higher but has his chance again.
runners.comment: Just denied at Navan recently; 3lb higher but has his chance again

races.race_name: Randox Maiden Hurdle

runners.name: Speculateur
runners.spotlight: Point winner was runner-up on sole bumper start but better efforts in five maiden hurdles up to 2m4f, including at Cork and Clonmel the last twice; has a bit to find on ratings with Roc Dino but should give that one a race; looks stable selected.
runners.comment: A bit to find on ratings with Roc Dino but can give that one a race; looks stable selected

runners.name: Roc Dino
runners.spotlight: Placed in hurdle races in France and this country but best effort when beaten 10l in the Martin Pipe Hurdle at Cheltenham; all those runs came on softer ground but if he handles this surface then is the one to beat.
runners.comment: Beaten 10l in the Martin Pipe Hurdle at Cheltenham; the one to beat if handles this ground

runners.name: Loch Cuan
runners.spotlight: One of two runners for yard; poor in a bumper on debut but a better effort next time in a Navan maiden hurdle, albeit well beaten by a stablemate; can run well but needs to improve a fair bit.
runners.comment: A well beaten 2nd to a stablemate last time; can run well but needs to improve a fair bit

runners.name: Hawk's Rock
runners.spotlight: The best of two bumper runs for former yard when beaten 2.5l by a useful sort at Galway last July; was well below that level on debut for this yard at Cheltenham since; however, could get involved on hurdles debut if returning to his best after a break.
runners.comment: Good bumper form though poor when last seen; could get involved on hurdles debut

runners.name: Union Boy
runners.spotlight: First foal; dam point/2m5f hurdle winner, out of half-sister to bumper winning dam of useful 2m/2m4f hurdle/chase winner Cruising Katie; by a useful sire and decent damline; best watched for future reference.
runners.comment: By a useful sire and decent damline; best watched for future reference

runners.name: Lady Cosmo
runners.spotlight: Point winner has been well beaten in two maiden hurdles and can only be watched for the time being; cheekpieces on.
runners.comment: Well beaten in two maiden hurdles and can only be watched for the time being; cheekpieces
runners.owner: Mrs Rose Boyd, Mrs Marie J. Armstrong, Seamus Carmichael

runners.name: Evangelic
runners.spotlight: Was badly regressive on the Flat last autumn and can only be watched on hurdles debut.
runners.comment: Was badly regressive on the Flat last autumn and can only be watched on hurdles debut

runners.name: Brohatna
runners.spotlight: Tailed-off in a Down Royal maiden hurdle on debut last year; can only be watched on return.
runners.comment: Tailed-off in a Down Royal maiden hurdle on debut last year; can only be watched

runners.name: Jenny's Bar
runners.spotlight: Has been poor in two maiden hurdles in testing ground and can't be fancied on those efforts.
runners.comment: Has been poor in two maiden hurdles in testing ground and can't be fancied on those runs

runners.name: Herecomesmybaby
runners.spotlight: Well beaten in a point and two maiden hurdles so best wait for handicaps.
runners.comment: Well beaten in a point and two maiden hurdles so best wait for handicaps

races.race_name: Road Stud Installations Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Kalsman
runners.spotlight: C&D winner in June 2024 on good ground; ran only once last year, fair fourth over C&D; showed the benefit of a comeback run when beating Justicialism by 3l at Down Royal last month; could be close in the rematch with that rival who was second again at Down Royal on Monday; Josh Williamson's claim a help.
runners.comment: Beat Justicialism by 3l at Down Royal, could be close in the rematch with that rival

runners.name: King Gris
runners.spotlight: Improved form after being switched to this yard, winning handicaps last December at Ayr and Hexham at around 2m4f on heavy ground; could manage only fifth on good ground at Down Royal last time; may have needed the run.
runners.comment: Wins at Ayr and Hexham were on heavy going, only fifth on good ground Down Royal last time

runners.name: Bearami Creek
runners.spotlight: Good form last autumn, highlighted by Navan win over 2m3f; totally out of sorts in two races last month; second reserve.
runners.comment: Second reserve, in good form last autumn, totally out of sorts in two races last month

runners.name: Joya Del Mar
runners.spotlight: An 18-race maiden over hurdles; one of her better runs when fourth at Down Royal in March; remote ninth over 3m at the same venue last time.
runners.comment: 18-race maiden over hurdles, one of her better runs when fourth at Down Royal in March
runners.owner: Mrs T Giles-Fahey & Miss S Mangan

runners.name: Artic Chief
runners.spotlight: Pulled up at Naas on handicap debut and tailed off at Down Royal; makes no appeal
runners.comment: Pulled up at Naas on handicap debut and tailed off at Down Royal, makes no appeal

runners.name: Justicialism
runners.spotlight: Over four years since he won back-to-back handicap hurdles for Brian Hamilton; lightly raced in recent seasons; bounced back to form on second start for Noel Kelly when second to Kalsman over 2m4f at Down Royal last month; up in trip after a fine second behind the improving Bella Union over 2m1f at Down Royal on Monday.
runners.comment: Up in trip after a fine second at Down Royal on Monday, previously beaten 3l by Kalsman

runners.name: Fool Me Once
runners.spotlight: Has achieved his best form at this venue, namely when twice second (in a maiden and a handicap) in the first half of 2025; moderate form last summer; always behind at Down Royal on return from a break; appears to be the trainer's second string.
runners.comment: Has achieved his best form at this venue, always behind at Down Royal on return from break

runners.name: Stonecarthy
runners.spotlight: Second in a 2m1f handicap at Wexford last May seemed promising; confidence dented by subsequent form; ex-Kieran Purcell, now with Ian Donoghue.
runners.comment: Failed to build on a good Wexford run last May, needs to find his form again on yard debut

runners.name: I'm For Home
runners.spotlight: Beaten around 12l when third in this race last season; 21-race maiden; lacks a recent run.
runners.comment: Beaten around 12l when third in this race last season, 21-race maiden, lacks a recent run

runners.name: Lecale's Diamond
runners.spotlight: Unplaced favourite over fences at Cork in January on his latest start; that followed a good Tramore run on his chasing debut; may improve with a tongue-tie fitted.
runners.comment: Unplaced favourite over fences on his latest start, that followed a good Tramore run

runners.name: Gangster Granny
runners.spotlight: Two Flat wins; best hurdles run when third over C&D on penultimate start; eased when chance had gone and tailed off in 3m Bellewstown event; first reserve.
runners.comment: First reserve, two Flat wins, best hurdles run when third over C&D on penultimate start

runners.name: Roxboro River
runners.spotlight: Stayed well when winning over 2m6f at Stratford last summer, showing a liking for quick ground; weak Irish form; struggled at Clonmel last month after a break.
runners.comment: Stayed well when winning over 2m6f at Stratford last summer, weak Irish form

runners.name: Loup De Tallane
runners.spotlight: Ordinary form last year; now back below the mark off which he won at Kilbeggan in 2024; pulled up at Limerick on final run before a break; new headgear combination now.
runners.comment: Ordinary form last year, now back below the mark off which he won at Kilbeggan in 2024

runners.name: Westin Pimpernel
runners.spotlight: Huge odds in four maiden hurdle runs; no sign of worthwhile form; third reserve.
runners.comment: Third reserve, huge odds in four maiden hurdle runs, no sign of worthwhile form

runners.name: Lady Iseult
runners.spotlight: Sole win came over 2m5f at Roscommon in 2022; 100-1 when pulled up at Down Royal last month on first outing since October 2023.
runners.comment: Only win was in 2022, pulled up at Down Royal last month on first outing since 2023

runners.name: Annarubyrose
runners.spotlight: Placed in a maiden at Sligo for Gavin Cromwell; big price when tailed off here last August on handicap debut; first run for Ian Donoghue now.
runners.comment: Big price when tailed off here last August on handicap debut, has changed stable

runners.name: Whatafoolbelieves
runners.spotlight: Some promise in bumpers; well beaten in four maiden hurdles; up in trip for handicap debut; chance is not obvious.
runners.comment: Some promise in bumpers, well beaten in four maiden hurdles, up in trip for handicap debut

runners.name: L'Un Deux Trois
runners.spotlight: Three-time Flat winner but remains a maiden over jumps; two poor runs over fences in 2025; hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Three-time Flat winner but remains a maiden over jumps, two poor runs over fences in 2025

races.race_name: SIS, Supporting Irish Racing Maiden Hurdle

runners.name: Endless Talking
runners.spotlight: Point-to-point winner on good ground for Donnchadh Doyle late last season; mild promise in a Fairyhouse bumper in November; soundly beaten in another bumper at Down Royal in December; better on last two starts; met interference when third behind the market leaders at Kilbeggan a fortnight ago; strong chance now.
runners.comment: Met interference when third behind the market leaders at Kilbeggan, strong chance now

runners.name: Lemmy Caution
runners.spotlight: Cost 250,000GBP after 8l debut point win at Monkgrange last year; bumper form has been nothing special; however, a reproduction of his Leopardstown third last Christmas would probably suffice to win a race of this standard; first reserve.
runners.comment: First reserve, cost 250,000GBP after debut point win, bumper form has been nothing special

runners.name: Flying Ace
runners.spotlight: Took third in a Lingstown point in November; changed hands for £35,000GBP in December; beaten almost 20l when fourth in a bumper at Navan; not ruled out in this modest grade.
runners.comment: Beaten almost 20l when fourth in a bumper at Navan, not ruled out in this modest grade

runners.name: Messire Des Bordes
runners.spotlight: Dam is a half-sister to Kopek Des Bordes; may improve from Bellewstown run; third reserve.
runners.comment: Third reserve, dam is a half-sister to Kopek Des Bordes, may improve from Bellewstown run

runners.name: Echinacea
runners.spotlight: Fair maiden on the Flat; has been in good form at Dundalk; runner-up in four of her six starts since last November, equally effective at 1m4f and 2m; could feature here with an adequate round of jumping
runners.comment: Has been in good form at Dundalk, could feature here with an adequate round of jumping

runners.name: Al What
runners.spotlight: Has been running in modest Flat handicaps without showing any sign of winning potential; second reserve.
runners.comment: Second reserve, has been running in modest Flat handicaps, no sign winning potential
runners.owner: Mrs M Cahill & Mrs Caroline Hendron & Peter Robin Parkhill

runners.name: Dancing Fruits
runners.spotlight: Very poor record in points; much better in two maiden hurdles runs; still hard to fancy but perhaps a handicap prospect in due course.
runners.comment: Very poor record in points, much better in two maiden hurdles runs, still hard to fancy

runners.name: Dorie Miller
runners.spotlight: Ran in good bumper company for Noel Meade, notably in races by The Wager and Boycetown who have contested Grade 1 events; tailed off in two bumper starts for this stable in recent months.
runners.comment: Ran in good bumper company for Noel Meade, tailed off in two starts for this stable

runners.name: Rare Times
runners.spotlight: Ran creditably in two academy hurdles last year; fifth behind the useful Quinta Do Lago (Punchestown festival winner now rated  131) on Cork debut (good); unplaced favourite at Punchestown, heavy ground might have been an issue.
runners.comment: Fifth behind the useful Quinta Do Lago on Cork debut, unplaced favourite at Punchestown
runners.owner: Gerard Mullins & Ms Mary Mullins & Ms Margaret Mullins

runners.name: Bootscootinboogie
runners.spotlight: Famous Name gelding; second foal out of a dual points winner; likely to need experience.
runners.comment: Famous Name gelding, second foal out of a dual points winner, likely to need experience

runners.name: Me Wee Nanny Fran
runners.spotlight: Down the field in two starts at Clonmel; 100-1 on latest; in good hands but best watched.
runners.comment: Down the field in two starts at Clonmel; 100-1 on latest, in good hands but best watched

runners.name: Mirfaq
runners.spotlight: A 25-1 chance when down the field at Cork on debut, will need to pick up more experience.
runners.comment: A 25-1 chance when down the field at Cork on debut, will need to pick up more experience

runners.name: Aboras Mahler
runners.spotlight: No worthwhile form in four maiden hurdle outings; her new trainer adds a tongue-tie.
runners.comment: No worthwhile form in four maiden hurdle outings, her new trainer adds a tongue-tie

runners.name: Betty's Glen
runners.spotlight: Beaten a long way in a six-runner Punchestown bumper on debut and can only be watched for now.
runners.comment: Beaten a long way in a six-runner bumper on debut and can only be watched for now

runners.name: Nevermindestranger
runners.spotlight: Dead-heated in a point in 2023; placed in a maiden hurdle at Sligo the same year; first run for a new stable after a long layoff.
runners.comment: Placed in a maiden hurdle at Sligo in 2023, first run for a new stable after a long layoff

runners.name: Free Vango
runners.spotlight: Raced too keenly and dropped away from two out on debut at Bellewstown last month.
runners.comment: Raced too keenly and dropped away from two out on debut at Bellewstown last month

runners.name: Shanliss Tricks
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to the very useful Amarillo Sky; failed to make any impact when 100-1 at Navan first time out.
runners.comment: Not a bad pedigree, failed to make any impact when 100-1 at Navan first time out

runners.name: Layenda
runners.spotlight: Well beaten in two maiden hurdles late last year; saddle slipped in a Fairyhouse bumper in January; tailed off at the same venue in February.
runners.comment: Well beaten in two maiden hurdles, tailed off in a bumper at Fairyhouse in February

races.race_name: Angie Orr Still Celebrating Her 70th Birthday Mares Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Sea Of Doubt
runners.spotlight: Has managed a couple of fair runs over fences, one of them at this venue, since her last hurdles run; definite chance if ready for this after a break.
runners.comment: Has managed a couple of fair runs over fences since her last hurdles run, definite chance

runners.name: A Snow White Park
runners.spotlight: A 66-1 winner at Ballinrobe last May, beating the subsequent Lartigue Hurdle winner Last Kingdom; fourth in a chase at Tramore last time her best effort since then; stayed on well having been set plenty to do; definite chance in ordinary contest.
runners.comment: 66-1 winner at Ballinrobe last May, fourth in a chase last time her best effort since then

runners.name: Camlin Park
runners.spotlight: Won a C&D maiden hurdle by 12l last August; pulled up in both subsequent starts; hard to be confident on overall form.
runners.comment: Won a C&D maiden hurdle by 12l last August, pulled up in both subsequent starts

runners.name: Katherine
runners.spotlight: Has struggled to achieve consistency since back-to-back 2m wins at Kilbeggan and Fairyhouse in the autumn of 2024; ran well at Listowel last June; failed to build on that in the autumn; has had a recent Flat run.
runners.comment: Has struggled to achieve consistency since back-to-back wins in the autumn of 2024

runners.name: Small Bucks
runners.spotlight: A 33-1 winner for this rider on good ground at Cork last summer; pulled up at Leopardstown in March; shaped quite well at Down Royal on Monday, staying on late over 2m; the ground is coming right for her now.
runners.comment: 33-1 winner on good ground at Cork last summer, shaped quite well at Down Royal on Monday

runners.name: Doing It In Style
runners.spotlight: Ordinary form in maidens and well beaten on handicap debut, better ground may help; trainer also runs Baby Fish.
runners.comment: Ordinary form in maidens and well beaten on handicap debut, better ground may help

runners.name: Splashing Out
runners.spotlight: Out of sorts since winning at Down Royal last October; finished more than 10l behind A Snow White Park in a recent handicap chase at Tramore.
runners.comment: Finished more than 10l behind A Snow White Park in a recent handicap chase at Tramore

runners.name: Katie's Casper
runners.spotlight: Third in a bumper at this venue two years ago; has struggled in bumpers and maiden hurdles since that initial run; rider's 5lb claim and the fact that the stable had a winner at Punchestown offer some hope
runners.comment: Third in a bumper at this venue two years ago, has struggled since then, handicap debut

runners.name: Baby Fish
runners.spotlight: Not beaten far in sixth in a weak Tramore maiden; may slot into handicaps effectively; trainer also runs Doing It In Style.
runners.comment: Not beaten far in sixth in a weak Tramore maiden, may slot into handicaps effectively

runners.name: Auntie Ida
runners.spotlight: Debut fourth in a bumper at Punchestown seemed promising; has shown little in maiden hurdles; trained by a top point-to-point producer; may improve.
runners.comment: Debut fourth in a bumper seemed promising, has shown little in maiden hurdles, may improve

runners.name: Mercurial Sea
runners.spotlight: Fair form in bumpers early last season; limited encouragement from a Navan handicap hurdle run; not disgraced in a maiden last time.
runners.comment: Fair in bumpers early last season, limited encouragement from a Navan handicap hurdle run

runners.name: Lagoon Nebula
runners.spotlight: Landed a competitive juvenile handicap hurdle at Cheltenham last spring on good; fair form on the Flat at up to 2m after; refused to race on chase debut at Clonmel; stayed on to some effect in a Flat handicap at Bellewstown last month, ground should suit.
runners.comment: Stayed on to some effect in a Flat handicap at Bellewstown last month, ground should suit

runners.name: Go Avel
runners.spotlight: Never a threat on handicap debut at Kilbeggan; pulled up in a 2m6f handicap on this card last year and absent since, weak overall form.
runners.comment: Pulled up in a 2m6f handicap on this card last year and absent since, weak overall form
runners.owner: Mrs Peter Casey & Mrs Paula Casey

runners.name: Frontier Rose
runners.spotlight: Modest form in bumpers and maiden hurdles for her previous trainer; mid-division Leopardstown run last Christmas on stable debut was a small step in the right direction.
runners.comment: Leopardstown run last Christmas on stable debut was a small step in the right direction

races.race_name: Peter Fitzpatrick & Sons Estate Agents And Chartered Surveyors Handicap Chase

runners.name: Tsavo Park
runners.spotlight: Maiden was running well when falling at the last on chasing debut at Thurles in February; was then pulled-up at Wexford but a better effort over hurdles at Bellewstown last time; chance if at best.
runners.comment: Was running well when falling on chase bow; solid hurdle run last time; chance if at best

runners.name: Finnians Row
runners.spotlight: His three chase wins includes one at this course and was in decent form last autumn; however, has questions to answer after being pulled-up both runs this spring; blinkers replace visor.
runners.comment: Questions to answer after being pulled-up both runs this spring; blinkers replace visor

runners.name: Just For Yuse
runners.spotlight: Maiden after 30 starts under all rules but did run well off this mark in a Down Royal chase last time in this sphere; needs to find more to get off the mark.
runners.comment: Ran well off this mark in a Down Royal chase last time in this sphere; needs to find more

runners.name: Katzoff
runners.spotlight: Maiden under all codes but did run well enough over hurdles on debut for this yard last time; needs to build on that from 5lb out of the handicap.
runners.comment: Ran well enough over hurdles on debut for this yard; needs to build on that and 5lb wrong

runners.name: Jouster
runners.spotlight: Won twice over hurdles and once over fences for Gavin Cromwell before losing his form last year; has continued his regression for this yard and is hard to fancy on his poor Kilbeggan comeback run.
runners.comment: Regressive on recent runs and is hard to fancy on his poor Kilbeggan comeback run

runners.name: Struck Oil
runners.spotlight: A maiden whose best hurdle run came when second on return at Bellewstown last time over 2m1f; is one to consider on chasing debut with the market a guide.
runners.comment: Second over hurdles last time; one to consider on chasing debut with the market a guide

runners.name: Bocelli's Voice
runners.spotlight: Second runner for yard; hurdle and chase winner has been regressive in both those spheres since last autumn; blinkers replace the visor worn last time; needs to turn a corner.
runners.comment: Regressive since last autumn; blinkers replace the visor worn last time; needs a revival

runners.name: Hello Johnjoe
runners.spotlight: Went closest to winning over hurdles when third at Clonmel in January; poor in two runs since but yard has hit form so can't be ruled out on chasing debut.
runners.comment: Poor last two runs but yard has hit form so can't be ruled out on chasing debut

runners.name: Driveonwill
runners.spotlight: Maiden has run well enough in defeat over hurdles at times; beaten 31l on chasing debut at Thurles and needs to do a lot better.
runners.comment: Maiden was beaten 31l on chasing debut at Thurles and needs to do a lot better

runners.name: Silent Dawn
runners.spotlight: Placed a few times over hurdles including at this course; poor on the Flat and over hurdles when last seen; needs best on chasing debut/return.
runners.comment: Placed over hurdles including here; poor on the Flat and over hurdles when last seen

races.race_name: Remembering Caughoo Handicap Chase

runners.name: My Good Pal
runners.spotlight: Is just 1-19 over hurdles but has posted two decent chase runs from three starts including here; down 1lb from Wexford last time; cheekpieces added; can get involved.
runners.comment: Is 1-19 over hurdles; posted two decent chase runs incl' here; cheekpieces added

runners.name: Hardy Diamond
runners.spotlight: Has won over hurdles and fences at Kilbeggan; was a solid second at Down Royal but made a bad mistake there last time and ran below form; has to rebound after just four days.
runners.comment: Solid 2nd at Down Royal but made a bad mistake there last time and has to rebound

runners.name: Dinoland
runners.spotlight: Dual hurdle winner has run well in defeat here and was running well on second chase start at Tipperary last year when making a bad mistake at the last; not easy to fancy on last two runs.
runners.comment: Dual hurdle winner has run well in defeat here; not easy to fancy on last two runs
runners.owner: Nigel O'Hare & J Mackle & Mark Devlin & Philip Smith

runners.name: Mastermind
runners.spotlight: Dual hurdle winner got off the mark over fences at Down Royal last month, beating Hardy Diamond by 1.75l; should run well again raised 3lb.
runners.comment: Got off the mark over fences at Down Royal last month and should run well raised 3lb

runners.name: My Cullen
runners.spotlight: Maiden posted some fair runs in bumpers and hurdles for Henrietta Knight; however, was beaten 38l on chasing/stable debut last time and has to do much better.
runners.comment: Maiden was beaten 38l on chasing debut last time and has to do much better

runners.name: Mini Getaway
runners.spotlight: Lightly-raced for her age; point winner in January ran well enough over hurdles at Naas since; chasing should suit better so considered.
runners.comment: Point winner in January ran well enough over hurdles since; considered

runners.name: Cocoplum
runners.spotlight: Maiden posted her best chase run here in 2023; pulled-up on return from long absence last time and makes no appeal.
runners.comment: Maiden posted best chase run here in 2023; pulled-up on return from long absence last time

runners.name: Coillte M C G
runners.spotlight: Maiden posted a cracking run on chasing debut at Cork but was badly hampered next time at Thurles; one to consider on handicap bow in this sphere.
runners.comment: Cracking run on chasing debut but badly hampered next time; one to consider

runners.name: Tullabrack Lad
runners.spotlight: Maiden ran well enough off this mark behind Mastermind last time at Down Royal, beaten 10l; needs a bit more to score.
runners.comment: Maiden ran well enough off this mark behind Mastermind last time; needs a bit more

runners.name: Shantou Show
runners.spotlight: Dual hunter chase winner in 2023 but has been regressive since last autumn (poor last twice) and needs a major revival.
runners.comment: Dual hunter chase winner has been regressive since last autumn and needs a revival

runners.name: Jimli's Cave
runners.spotlight: Sole hurdle win came here over 2m6f and his his best chase efforts have also been here; however, has been poor on last three starts and has to rebound in a big way.
runners.comment: Sole hurdle win came here along with his best chase efforts; poor latest starts

runners.name: Person Of Interest
runners.spotlight: Second runner for yard; sole win came over fences at 2m4f here last year; mostly poor since including beaten a long way the last twice so major questions.
runners.comment: Sole win came over 2m4f at this course last year; mostly poor since so questions

runners.name: Harley Street
runners.spotlight: Well-bred Flat winner is yet to score over obstacles; well beaten over hurdles four days ago and fell on chase debut previous run; needs his best to get involved.
