runners.comment: Well beaten over hurdles four days ago; fell on chase debut previous run; needs best

runners.name: Difficult Decision
runners.spotlight: Dual hurdle and chase winner has regressed after a decent comeback run in December from a long absence, so questions remain.
runners.comment: Dual hurdle and chase winner has regressed after a decent comeback run so questions

races.race_name: Joe Rea Memorial Mares INH Flat Race

runners.name: Kisuton Enki
runners.spotlight: Started 11/4 but tailed odd in a bumper on debut in 2024; found to be lame afterwards; second in a 2m5f Limerick maiden hurdle on return from 471 days away; quicker ground today and down in trip but a repeat of that will make her a big player back in this sphere.
runners.comment: Lame after a poor debut in 2024; 2nd in a 2m5f Limerick maiden h'dle on return; big player

runners.name: Jo's Secret
runners.spotlight: Runner-up on sole point start and solid efforts in two of her three bumpers; the best of two maiden hurdle runs came when beaten 6.75l at Fairyhouse last time; a repeat of that form back in a bumper will see her go close.
runners.comment: Solid bumper form and a fine third in a maiden hurdle last time; go close with a repeat

runners.name: Magical Sal
runners.spotlight: Well beaten in two bumpers and a point and can only be watched today with cheekpieces added
runners.comment: Well beaten in two bumpers and a point and can only be watched today

runners.name: Magical Act
runners.spotlight: Third foal; half-sister to 7f winner Dance Angel; dam unraced half-sister to HK 7f Listed winner Rewarding Hero and Group-placed 7f-8.5f winner Dance And Dance; a 1,000gns vendor buyback as a 2yo; tongue-tie on debut for this Flat-bred; best watched.
runners.comment: Tongue-tie on debut for this Flat-bred; best watched

runners.name: Largy Lilly
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to four winners inc Amazon Lady (Listed-placed 5f; RPR 104), Room To Roam (7f AW 2yo/2m turf Flat, 88; 2m hurdle/2m5f chase) and Malangen (1m2f Flat, 59; 2m-2m4f hurdle/chase); decent pedigree and cost E6,200 as a foal; tongue-tie for debut; worth a market look.
runners.comment: Decent pedigree and cost E6,200 as a foal; tongue-tie for debut; worth a market look

runners.name: Culldoo
runners.spotlight: First foal; dam French 14.7f winner, half-sister to French winners Plenty City (7.5f-9.5f), Boss D'Or (1m5f) and Hindou (2m1f chase); by a leading French NH sire and a decent dual-purpose damline; commands plenty of respect from this source so follow the market.
runners.comment: Well-bred filly commands plenty of respect from this source so follow the market

runners.name: Double Order
runners.spotlight: Beaten 29l in a Cork bumper on debut a year ago but ruined her chance by pulling hard; reappears for a new yard with a hood added and can do better; worth a market look.
runners.comment: Ruined her chance by pulling hard on debut a year ago; new yard; worth a market look

runners.name: Freeway
runners.spotlight: Beaten a long way in two bumpers at big prices and isn't easy to make a case for; tongue-tie on.
runners.comment: Beaten a long way in two bumpers at big prices and isn't easy to make a case for

runners.name: Coco's Legacy
runners.spotlight: Beaten 35l on debut at Down Royal last December and has to improve lots on that form.
runners.comment: Beaten 35l on debut at Down Royal last December and has to improve lots on that form

races.race_name: British Stallion Studs EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands C And D) (EBF Restricted Qualifer) (GBB)

runners.name: Red Spells Danger
runners.spotlight: 0-6 but he's recorded RPRs in the 70s in all of his runs, including when second at Catterick (7f, good) and Southwell (7f, AW) this spring; sets a clear standard back on turf and he has shaped as though this drop to 6f could suit.
runners.comment: 0-6 but he's been consistent and sets a clear standard back on turf; big player

runners.name: Tip Foster
runners.spotlight: Gelded son of Dream Ahead; weak 18-1 at Pontefract (6f, good to soft) last month but ran well for a long way before finishing 4l third behind a useful prospect; that was a promising start and has possibilities if he can build on that.
runners.comment: Promising third on Pontefract debut and has possibilities if he can build on that

runners.name: Soda
runners.spotlight: 4,000euros yearling; sister to 7f/1m winner Crossford (including Hong Kong; RPR 97), half-sister to four winners at 7f-1m4f; 18-1, came from off the pace to finish third of six on recent Catterick debut (7f, good) but was beaten about 5l and needs a big step forward on this drop in trip.
runners.comment: Showed ability at Catterick but she needs a big step forward on this drop in trip

runners.name: Ndoto
runners.spotlight: Beaten about 7l on Wolverhampton debut (6f) in March and same outcome in another small-field event at Southwell (7f) later that month; still early days but she needs a transformation on this switch to turf.
runners.comment: Beaten about 7l in two AW runs and needs a transformation on turf debut

runners.name: Rousham
runners.spotlight: Well held at massive prices in two runs this spring but he wasn't far away when he got badly hampered at a crucial stage at Doncaster (6f, good to firm) last month; showed definite signs of ability in that race and he's a half-brother to three sprint winners; one to keep an eye on.
runners.comment: Ran better than the bare facts at Doncaster and he's one to keep an eye on

runners.name: Highfield Jewel
runners.spotlight: 4yo who was well held in two runs at Southwell (7f/6f) this winter, showing modest form; has plenty to find on her return and handicaps look the way forward after this.
runners.comment: Well held in two runs at Southwell during the winter and has plenty to find on her return

runners.name: Sparkling Wine
runners.spotlight: 80-1 and outpaced before finishing eighth of 12 on Beverley debut (5f, good) last April; returns after long absence and she needs major improvement upped in trip.
runners.comment: Didn't show much in sole 2yo run (April) and needs major improvement on her comeback

races.race_name: Traditional Family Day Sunday 17th May Handicap

runners.name: Hood Wink
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the tenth attempt when dead-heating in a Wetherby handicap (1m, good to firm) 12 days ago; this is tougher under a penalty but he showed a good attitude last time and is open to more progress; respected.
runners.comment: Off the mark when dead-heating at Wetherby last month; respected under a penalty

runners.name: Masaban
runners.spotlight: Signs of ability in two runs for Dermot Weld last summer (7f, yielding/1m, AW); gelded and sold for 20,000euros in November; finished sixth of 14 on stable debut at Doncaster (6f, good to firm) last month but he made some late headway and that was promising after 252 days off; should benefit from that run and he looks interesting back up in trip on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Unexposed gelding and he looks interesting upped to 1m on handicap debut; watch market

runners.name: Resdev Time
runners.spotlight: Seven-race maiden who has not made an impact in four handicaps and was almost 3l behind Dalamara when fifth at Redcar (1m, good to firm) last month; continues to drop down the weights but she needs to raise her game.
runners.comment: Seven-race maiden who has not made an impact in four handicaps; needs to find more

runners.name: Dalamara
runners.spotlight: Finished well when scoring at 16-1 on handicap debut at Newcastle (1m, AW) in December; never-dangerous fourth behind an emphatic winner at Redcar (1m, good to firm) last month but that was a creditable effort after a break and she still has potential; shortlisted.
runners.comment: Won at Newcastle in December and creditable fourth at Redcar last month; shortlisted

runners.name: Clear Above
runners.spotlight: Five-race maiden who has not really progressed and he was well held in two handicaps (7/1m, Tapeta) last month; dropped another 3lb but he needs a transformation back on turf.
runners.comment: 0-5 and he's been well held in both handicaps; needs improvement back on turf

runners.name: Princess Coco
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to numerous winners but she's not shown much and was a long way behind two of today's rivals on handicap debut at Redcar last month; opposable.
runners.comment: Has not shown much in her five starts, including a Redcar handicap last month; opposable

runners.name: Miss Mambo
runners.spotlight: Showed some ability in maidens, including when third of six at Bath (1m, good to soft) three weeks ago; this is more realistic on handicap debut and she's a half-sister to four winners; needs watching in market.
runners.comment: Showed some ability in maidens and she needs watching in market on handicap debut

runners.name: Bobbys Pride
runners.spotlight: Struggled at big prices in all four 2yo runs, including a nursery at Musselburgh (1m, good to soft) in September; still unexposed but she's on the same mark as last time and has lots to prove on her return.
runners.comment: Didn't show much as a 2yo and has lots to prove on her return

races.race_name: Dr Keith Howard OBE Memorial Handicap

runners.name: He's An Angel
runners.spotlight: Hold-up performer who scored at Wolverhampton (6f, AW) in January but he's not hit the same level since and was always behind after a slow start at Southwell last month; comes with risks attached and he will need a good pace back at this trip; 2lb out of the weights.
runners.comment: Frequent slow starter and he has work to do from out of the weights back in trip

runners.name: Le Beau Garcon
runners.spotlight: Handles most ground but all of his four wins have been on good to firm; last success was at Ayr (5f) 12 months ago but that was on his reappearance and he's only 1lb higher for today's return; needs a close look after 221 days off.
runners.comment: Four wins at this trip and he has a good record when fresh; in the mix off workable mark

runners.name: Betweenthesticks
runners.spotlight: Returned from six months off with a win at Wolverhampton and he followed up with another narrow success at Pontefract (5f, good) 17 days ago; 3lb higher than for his front-running win last time but remains feasibly treated and is tactically versatile; key player for yard that won this in 2023 and 2025.
runners.comment: Has won his last two starts and yard won this in 2023 and 2025; key player
runners.owner: J A Swinburne & Mrs Ruth A Carr

runners.name: Wrestling Revenue
runners.spotlight: Turned things around with a win at Pontefract (5f, good) in October but he's been back in the doldrums in three subsequent runs; has plenty to prove again and needs another bolt from the blue.
runners.comment: Won at Pontefract in October but he's been out of sorts in three subsequent runs

runners.name: Glory Fighter
runners.spotlight: Admirable veteran who recorded his 11th win when making all at Nottingham (5f, good) last month; there wasn't much separating the first five but he's only 2lb higher here and should give it another good shot.
runners.comment: Recorded his 11th win when making all at Nottingham; respected off 2lb higher

runners.name: Mark's Choice
runners.spotlight: Ten-time course winner who ended last season with two wins (6f/5f) at Ripon; struggled on Thirsk reappearance last month but he doesn't have a great record when fresh and he's not ruled out back at his favoured track; acts on good to firm and soft.
runners.comment: Ten-time course winner who is not ruled back at this track on his second run after a break

runners.name: Impressor
runners.spotlight: Eight-time sprint winner who is suited by good to soft or slower; his last success was off this mark in September but he's been well held at Musselburgh (5f) and Thirsk (6f) in his two runs; has bit to prove again and wouldn't want the ground to dry out.
runners.comment: On last winning mark but he's been well held in his last two starts; down the list

runners.name: Vince L'Amour
runners.spotlight: Returned to form with a second at Musselburgh (5f, good to soft) last month but he failed to back that up over C&D (last of eight) two weeks ago; has something to prove again after that and he's still 3lb higher than for his last win.
runners.comment: Hard to predict and was last of eight over C&D two weeks ago; others preferred

runners.name: Hurt You Never
runners.spotlight: Prolific winner who is on a dangerous mark but she's been out of sorts since completing a double at Hamilton (5f, good) last August; has plenty to prove and needs to turn things around back in trip.
runners.comment: Prolific winner but she's lost her way and needs to turn things around

races.race_name: Sam Alexander Memorial Handicap

runners.name: Novamay
runners.spotlight: Won three in a row on turf (6f, good) last July and the first leg was over C&D; went up 25lb but she ended last season with a creditable fourth on AW at Newcastle; could have more to offer as a 4yo and needs a close look on her return.
runners.comment: Three turf wins last summer and she could have more to offer as a 4yo; respected on return

runners.name: Fortamour
runners.spotlight: Six-time course winner but well held in his three runs this year and latest was over C&D (good to soft) last month; has form figures of 1242 in this race in the last four years but he needs to turn things around off a sliding mark.
runners.comment: Has good record in this race in recent years but needs a major revival

runners.name: Grant Wood
runners.spotlight: Began this year with two Newcastle wins (6f, Tapeta) but he was only eighth of ten there last month; has won over C&D but has a patchy profile and needs to step up again on this return to turf.
runners.comment: Began this year with two Newcastle wins but was disappointing there latest; risks attached

runners.name: Charlie Mason
runners.spotlight: All five wins have been this trip but he had a near-miss over 5f at Wolverhampton in March; only 1lb higher today but he was a disappointing favourite back on turf at Doncaster (5f, good to soft) last time; has a bit to prove again after that and this is a step back up in grade.
runners.comment: Disappointing favourite at Doncaster latest and this is a step back up in grade

runners.name: Modern Times
runners.spotlight: Won a C&D maiden (good) last June on his second start for Tim Easterby; returned after a lengthy absence with a creditable fifth here (6f, good to soft) last month and ran another solid race at Pontefract last time; still looks on a workable mark and he shouldn't be far away.
runners.comment: C&D winner who has posted two solid efforts this season; shouldn't be far away

runners.name: U Sure Do
runners.spotlight: All 12 runs have been on turf and he won twice at Catterick (6f/7f, good) last season; 6lb higher than for his last success but he reappeared with a creditable fourth of 13 at Thirsk last month; should benefit from that run and he's in the mix on this drop back in trip; tongue-tie added.
runners.comment: Two wins last season and was good fourth on his Thirsk reappearance last month; in the mix

runners.name: It Just Takes Time
runners.spotlight: C&D winner in 2023 but all his subsequent wins have come over 7f+; on dangerous mark but he's faded at big prices in both runs this season and latest was over C&D (eighth of 16) three weeks ago; down the list.
runners.comment: Finished down the field in last three runs and latest was over C&D three weeks ago

runners.name: Beyond Borders
runners.spotlight: Scored off 3lb higher at Yarmouth (6f, good) last June but he's not come close to that form since and was seventh of nine on Leicester reappearance last month; plenty to prove and four of his five wins were as 2yo; cheekpieces added.
runners.comment: Won at Yarmouth last June but he's struggled since and has plenty to prove; new headgear

runners.name: Wreck It Ryley
runners.spotlight: 6yo who hit a personal best with his emphatic win at Leicester (6f, good) last month but he flopped off this revised mark there 12 days later; has won at Ripon but he's not easy to predict and needs to bounce back again after that last run.
runners.comment: Won by 6l at Leicester last month but flopped on his return visit 12 days later

runners.name: Musical Touch
runners.spotlight: Acts on good to firm and soft ground; won at Thirsk (6f, good) last spring and recorded his second AW win on his Southwell reappearance last month; up 4lb but he still looks feasibly treated and should get a good tow into this race; key player.
runners.comment: Won on Southwell reappearance last month; up 4lb but he's a big player again back on turf

runners.name: First Folio
runners.spotlight: All of his four wins have been at Yarmouth (6f, good/good to firm) and latest was in September 2024; well treated on old form but he had a mixed record last season and was well held in the autumn on his final two runs; has a bit to prove after six months off and others are preferred.
runners.comment: Yarmouth specialist who has not won since September 2024; looks opposable on return

races.race_name: Napoleons Casino And Restaurant Leeds Handicap

runners.name: No Knee Never
runners.spotlight: Rarely runs a bad race and he's won two of his last three starts, including an emphatic win at Wolverhampton (8.6f, AW) 11 days ago; has a penalty back on turf but he ran right up to his best last time and this new trip should be within range; respected.
runners.comment: Clearcut win at Wolverhampton last time and he's respected under a penalty

runners.name: Nanny Park
runners.spotlight: 1m AW winner for James Fanshawe as 2yo and ran some good races last year off higher marks than this; has form at up to 1m3f but struggled on stable debut at Doncaster last month and needs to leave that form well behind.
runners.comment: Just one win from 11 starts and she struggled on stable debut last month; down the list

runners.name: Garden Oasis
runners.spotlight: 14-time winner including seven times at Ripon; his last success was off 5lb higher last summer and he returned to form with a second at Pontefract (1m, good) last month; hasn't run over this far for a long time but he used to be effective at this trip and most of his wins have been in May-August.
runners.comment: Seven-time course winner who was second at Pontefract latest; respected back up in trip

runners.name: Velvet Whisper
runners.spotlight: Relatively lightly raced 4yo who ended last year with wins at Nottingham (1m2f, heavy) and Musselburgh (1m4f, soft); started this season with a low-key effort but she may have needed that run and still has scope for further progress; not ruled out dropped back in trip and grade.
runners.comment: Ended last year with two wins; well held on return but she could still have more to offer
runners.owner: C Wright, D Kilburn, A Dick And P Snook

runners.name: Star Cast
runners.spotlight: Three wins on AW at Newcastle (1m2f) since January including a cosy success last Friday; this is tougher under a penalty but she's still lightly raced on turf and was third in a big field at Doncaster (1m, good to soft) on her penultimate run; shortlisted.
runners.comment: 4yo who found more progress when winning at Newcastle last week; respected back on turf

runners.name: Triple Force
runners.spotlight: Had a highly productive time on turf last season including four wins (1m1f/1m2f) and four seconds; struggled in his hat-trick bid in November but that was on AW and he still looks feasibly treated on his penultimate form; interesting contender who needs a close look on return.
runners.comment: Four wins on turf last season and could find more progress as 5yo; interesting contender

runners.name: Dawn Of Liberation
runners.spotlight: Five-time winner, three times at around 1m2f (good/good to firm) last summer; well handicapped on his best form last season but he's been quiet in two runs this spring and needs to rediscover his spark.
runners.comment: Three wins last summer but he's been quiet in two runs this season; needs to step up

runners.name: Glistening Nights
runners.spotlight: All five wins have been at around 1m2f on good or quicker; last success was off 5lb lower but he reappeared with a fair third in Class 4 event over C&D (good to soft) last month; should go well on this drop back in grade and it would be a positive if the ground dries out.
runners.comment: Placed in a Class 4 over C&D on his reappearance last month; each-way claims again

runners.name: Poet's Dawn
runners.spotlight: 12-time turf winner including over C&D; hasn't made a serious impact in his two runs (1m) this season but he's now back on last winning mark and could be dangerous back up in trip; one of three runners for the yard.
runners.comment: 12-time turf winner who is on last winning mark and could be dangerous back up in trip

runners.name: Tiberio Force
runners.spotlight: Has gained both wins on turf, three years apart; his last success was over C&D last summer but he was well held in final three runs last year; 6lb out of the weights on his return and best watched in this competitive race.
runners.comment: Infrequent winner and this is tough from out of the weights on his return; opposable
runners.owner: Maj Green, S Croft & S Whitaker

runners.name: Bass Player
runners.spotlight: First win was on turf (7f) and last two have been on Tapeta (8.6f); reached the frame in two starts at Wolverhampton in December but he had a major blip there the following month; had another wind operation since and needs a major revival after four months off.
runners.comment: Had a major blip at Wolverhampton in January and needs to bounce back after wind surgery

runners.name: Jewel Maker
runners.spotlight: Veteran with 12 wins but he's lost his way and has struggled after slow starts in both runs this spring; has something to prove and he needs to turn things around back up in trip.
runners.comment: 12-time winner but he's lost his way and needs to turn things around

races.race_name: Ripon Races Ladies Day 18th June Handicap

runners.name: Bradbury
runners.spotlight: Unexposed gelding who ran into some trouble before finishing well for fourth in a Wolverhampton handicap (1m4f; tongue-tie added) 11 days ago; looks interesting off the same mark and this drop back in trip should be fine on pedigree (half-brother to two winners at up to 9.5f).
runners.comment: Unexposed gelding who was an eye-catching fourth at Wolverhampton; respected back in trip

runners.name: Port Darwin
runners.spotlight: Record of 1-10 but his win was off 4lb higher at Musselburgh (1m, good) last August; mixed record since but he returned to form with a clear second in first-time blinkers at Kempton (1m4f) last week; has claims if he can back that up and he's in the mix back in trip.
runners.comment: Turf winner last summer and runner-up at Kempton last week; in the mix back in trip

runners.name: Heated Moment
runners.spotlight: Six-race maiden who has not finished closer than 5l to a winner and was a fading fifth when upped to 1m2f on stable debut at Yarmouth (good) last month; mark continues to fall but she needs to find improvement from somewhere.
runners.comment: Six-race maiden who has not finished closer than 5l to a winner; down the list

runners.name: Mereside Princess
runners.spotlight: 11-race maiden but she's reached the frame in five handicaps including when fourth at Wetherby (1m, good to firm) last month; can take a strong hold but she continues to edge down the weights and could be a factor if she's settles at this new trip; blinkers reapplied.
runners.comment: 0-11 but she's on a reduced mark and could go well if she settles at this new trip

runners.name: Fuzeyya
runners.spotlight: Showed some ability in a Bath maiden (1m, good to soft) last September but she's struggled in her four other runs, including two AW handicaps (1m4f/1m2f) this spring; has lots to prove and needs a transformation back on turf; cheekpieces worn in last three runs are removed.
runners.comment: Has struggled in four of her five runs and needs a transformation back on turf

runners.name: Lady Vanguard
runners.spotlight: 320,000gns yearling; didn't show much in three qualifying runs but she ran well for a long way when fourth on handicap debut at Yarmouth (1m, good) last month; needs to build on that but it's still early days and she's open to more progress at this new trip.
runners.comment: Ran well for long way on handicap debut at Yarmouth (1m); player if she can build on that

runners.name: Riddikulus
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to a fairly useful winner (1m/1m2f) but she's not really progressed so far and has been beaten around 8l in both handicaps; returns after a short break and she needs improvement back up in trip.
runners.comment: Has good pedigree but she's not progressed so far and has been well held in both handicaps

runners.name: Vietnorm
runners.spotlight: Well held at big prices in three qualifying runs and same story when sixth of eighth on handicap debut at Wolverhampton (8.6f) in December; gelded since but he's needs to show a lot more at this new trip on his return.
runners.comment: Well held at big prices in all five runs; gelded and steps up to 1m2f on return

runners.name: Evergreen Emerald
runners.spotlight: Masar filly who was well held in three 2yo runs at around 7f but this is more realistic and she's a possible improver upped in trip on handicap debut; needs watching in market back from 226 days off.
runners.comment: Masar filly who is a possible improver upped to 1m2f on handicap debut; market useful

races.race_name: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Apprentice Handicap

runners.name: Havachoc
runners.spotlight: Won three times on the AW earlier in the year, including in a classified event here over 1m4f; third over this C&D in March and posted another solid effort when filling that same position at Beverley (1m4f, good to soft) 23 days ago; claims back on the AW.
runners.comment: Won three times on the AW this year and remains in good form; solid claims back on the AW

runners.name: Fine Print
runners.spotlight: Irish raider who has been well beaten in two starts at Dundalk (1m2f/1m4f) since returning from a 226-day break, but he won twice at this track last April, including over C&D, the most recent coming off a 3lb lower mark; interesting with cheekpieces back on.
runners.comment: Well beaten on last two starts but won here twice last year; cheekpieces are back on

runners.name: Apache Eagle
runners.spotlight: 0-16 but he was third in March and backed that up with another good effort, finishing second of 11 (also Southwell, 1m6f) last month; below that level over 2m at that track three days later but he may be capable of bouncing back returned to this shorter trip.
runners.comment: 0-16 but was placed on last two starts at 1m6f; below his best over 2m last time

runners.name: Rubellite
runners.spotlight: Three-time course winner, including one over C&D; runner-up to a progressive rival at Southwell (1m6f) on her stable debut in March and was a bit better than the bare result here over 1m4f 17 days ago having been short of room at a crucial stage; back up in trip.
runners.comment: Runner-up at Southwell on her stable debut and a bit better than the bare result latest

runners.name: Casual Encounter
runners.spotlight: 0-11 (hurdles/Flat); well held at Lingfield (1m5f, good) on her debut for this yard last August and it was the same story in four starts over hurdles; pulled up at Fakenham when last seen 138 days ago and others are preferred.
runners.comment: 0-11 (hurdles/Flat); pulled up at Fakenham when last seen 138 days ago; others preferred

runners.name: Lamlash
runners.spotlight: Pulled up over hurdles on his first start for this stable and he's failed to beat a rival home in each of his three starts on the Flat; best watched.
runners.comment: Has failed to beat a rival home in each of his three starts on the Flat; best watched
runners.owner: H Maye And The Joe Tickle Racing Club

runners.name: Fandabidozi
runners.spotlight: Doubled his tally over hurdles when winning at Bangor last April and he was placed there on his next start; disappointing at Aintree when last seen 357 days ago and he's best watched on his return to this sphere.
runners.comment: Disappointing at Aintree when last seen 357 days ago and he's best watched on his return
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Gwyn Evans

races.race_name: Download The At The Races App Restricted Maiden Fillies' Stakes (Horses In Bands B/C/D) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Miss Lizzy
runners.spotlight: Beat only one rival here over 5f on her debut but showed more when fading into fourth at Redcar (5f, good to firm; first-time tongue-tie); didn't seem to be crying out for the step up to 6f on that occasion but has claims if seeing it out.
runners.comment: Showed more at Redcar last time but didn't seem to be crying out for this longer trip

runners.name: Leucothea
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to 5f 2yo winner Wateen (RPR 83); sent off favourite and showed plenty on her debut following a slightly slow start at Bath (5.5f, firm) 11 days ago, finishing second of seven; leading contender now switched to the AW over this extra half furlong.
runners.comment: Sent off favourite and showed plenty when runner-up on debut at Bath (5.5f) 11 days ago

runners.name: Fire Thunder
runners.spotlight: April foal; 17,000euros yearling; sixth foal; half-sister to winners Fool For You (5f including 2yo/AW; RPR 97), Ardbraccan (7f-1m2f including 2yo/Germany; 83) and Bosphorus Prince (1m4f AW; 77); dam placed 6f/7f 2yo (including Listed; 82), out of sister to 7f Group 3 winner Indesatchel; speed in her pedigree and makes appeal on paper.
runners.comment: April foal; 17,000euros yearling; half-brother to three winners; makes appeal on paper

runners.name: Consultation
runners.spotlight: February foal; second foal; dam 7f AW winner (RPR 82), sister to useful 1m winner Assessment, closely related to 5f/6f winner Procedure, half-sister to useful 6f/7f winner Lockheed, out of 8.5f Group 3 winner; out of an AW winner and is one to monitor in the betting on her debut.
runners.comment: February foal; out of a 7f AW winner (RPR 82); one to monitor in the betting on her debut

runners.name: Freedom Flame
runners.spotlight: February foal; £40,000 yearling; eighth foal; half-sister to winners Prince Of The Dark (5.7f 2yo; RPR 102), Crimson Spirit (7f including 2yo; 96), Simplify (5f/6f including AW 2yo; 88), Crimson Angel (5f AW; 86) and Shimla Rolaan (7f/1m including AW; 69); dam 5f/6f winner (including 2yo; 92); stable had a filly win on her debut at Windsor on Monday; makes appeal.
runners.comment: February foal; £40,000 yearling; dam 5f/6f winner (including 2yo; 92) makes appeal

runners.name: Priscilla's Hope
runners.spotlight: Dam 6f winner (RPR 79); hinted at ability when third of six at Yarmouth (6f, good to firm) 17 days ago; the runner-up has won since but better is needed on this switch to the AW.
runners.comment: Hinted at ability when third of six at Yarmouth on her debut (runner-up has won since)

races.race_name: Race And Rest - Wolverhampton Holiday Inn Handicap

runners.name: Caraway
runners.spotlight: No better than sixth in her three qualifying runs (6f-1m) and she didn't take a step forward when fourth of seven on her handicap debut over this C&D (first-time hood) 32 days ago; hood is quickly discarded and she may yet to better.
runners.comment: Failed to step forward from her qualifying efforts when fourth over C&D on her h'cap debut

runners.name: Forever Perfect
runners.spotlight: Has shown improved form since stepping up to 1m4f, winning over this C&D last month before another good run here 11 days ago; due to be 2lb higher and can go well once again.
runners.comment: Has shown improved form since stepping up to 1m4f at this track; due to be 2lb higher

runners.name: Itsonlyrockandroll
runners.spotlight: Safely held on her first three starts and she was outpaced in the dash for home before finishing third behind Forever Perfect over C&D on her handicap debut 17 days ago; cheekpieces now go on and this well bred filly may do better.
runners.comment: Outpaced before finishing third behind Forever Perfect over C&D on her handicap debut

runners.name: Kakirra
runners.spotlight: Struggled in each of his three starts (all 7f), including at Kempton 51 days ago on his first start since being gelded; has the hood removed and steps up markedly in trip for his handicap debut; watch the betting.
runners.comment: Struggled in three starts over 7f; steps up markedly in trip for his handicap debut

runners.name: Song Of The Stars
runners.spotlight: Well held on her first three starts (1m) but her two starts in handicap company over this C&D haven't been without encouragement; cheekpieces are now applied.
runners.comment: Two respectable runs in handicap company over this C&D; cheekpieces are now applied

runners.name: Al Amirah
runners.spotlight: Beaten 9l or more at big price in novice/maiden events on her first four starts, and she hung badly right before finishing tailed off on her handicap debut here over 9.5f 28 days ago; steps back up in trip but is best watched.
runners.comment: Hung badly right and finished tailed off here on her debut 28 days ago; up in trip

runners.name: Tonyfromhr
runners.spotlight: Gelded prior to his handicap debut but failed to improve on what he had shown in maiden events, finishing seventh of nine at Kempton (1m4f; first-time tongue-tie); blinkers are now reached for.
runners.comment: Gelded prior to his handicap debut at Kempton but was well held; blinkers are now tried

races.race_name: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Maiden Fillies' Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Withtearsinmyeyes
runners.spotlight: Sent off 12-1 for last month's Nottingham debut (1m, good), but showed plenty of promise in finishing second of the 12 runners, splitting a couple of previous winners; that's the best form on offer and any improvement on it should make her hard to beat.
runners.comment: Runner-up on last month's Nottingham debut; any improvement should make her hard to beat

runners.name: Bintwafaa
runners.spotlight: 460,000euros yearling; half-sister to eight winners including Jan Brueghel (1m2f-14.5f including St Leger; RPR 126), Sovereign (8.5f 2yo/1m4f Irish Derby; 118) and Dawn Rising (1m4f-2m5f Flat including Group 3, 108; 2m4f hurdle); dam 7f 2yo winner (104); beautifully bred, but a hood enlisted for her debut does pose a question; market should reveal plenty.
runners.comment: Beautifully bred, but a hood is enlisted for her debut; market should reveal plenty

runners.name: Silver Lake
runners.spotlight: 420,000gns yearling; notable connections but out of the frame in both starts over 7f/1m at Newmarket; half-sister to an AW winner, but handicaps over further may be her thing.
runners.comment: Out of the frame in both starts at Newmarket; handicaps over further may be her thing

runners.name: Seet
runners.spotlight: 425,000gns yearling; showed her inexperience but also some ability when fifth of 11 on her Newbury debut (7f, good; 17-2) three weeks ago; her half-sister Kalpana was top-class on both turf and AW, so a much bigger effort seems likely.
runners.comment: Green when fifth on her Newbury debut, but a much bigger effort seems likely

runners.name: Lightkiss
runners.spotlight: Sent off 4-1 when fourth of seven on her Kempton debut (7f, Polytrack) last month; more will probably be needed.
runners.comment: Fourth of seven on her Kempton debut last month; more will probably be needed

runners.name: Sapphire Secret
runners.spotlight: 420,000gns yearling; 22-1 when well held on her Newbury debut (7f, good) three weeks ago, finishing just over 2l behind Seet; bred to be much better than that, though, including being a half-sister to a Group 3 AW winner.
runners.comment: Well held on her Newbury debut three weeks ago, but is bred to be much better than that

races.race_name: Free Tips Daily On attheraces.com Handicap

runners.name: Autumn Affair
runners.spotlight: Confirmed earlier promise when making a successful handicap debut at Lingfield (6f, Polytrack) in March; 3lb rise not excessive and the extra furlong should suit on pedigree; leading player.
runners.comment: Made a winning handicap debut at Lingfield in March; extra furlong should suit on pedigree

runners.name: Pints In Peace
runners.spotlight: Consistent since switching to the AW including when making a successful stable debut in a maiden over C&D in March; had 12 starts already, but he seems very likely to run his race again.
runners.comment: C&D winner; consistent since switching to AW but had 12 starts already; still respected

runners.name: Ahead Of Fashion
runners.spotlight: In the frame a few times, but 0-10 and recent efforts haven't been among his best; others preferred.
runners.comment: 0-10 and recent efforts haven't been among his best; others preferred

runners.name: Arctic Wind
runners.spotlight: Made the frame in three of his four starts on turf (5f/6f) for David Menuisier as a 2yo; changed hands for 9,000gns in October and makes his stable/handicap debut after seven months off having been gelded in the meantime; half-brother to a couple of AW winners and the market should be informative.
runners.comment: Makes his stable/handicap/AW debut after seven months off; market should be informative

races.race_name: attheraces.com/marketmovers Handicap

runners.name: Lessay
runners.spotlight: Not at his best of late, but has dropped 8lb lower than when successful over C&D in January; needs a resurgence, but is too well handicapped to ignore.
runners.comment: 8lb lower than when successful over C&D in January; too well handicapped to ignore

runners.name: Clover Time
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in five consecutive 0-65 handicaps over 6f on the AW between September and January, but not fared in better since and now 0-14; didn't run very well in one previous go over this far.
runners.comment: 0-14; didn't run very well in one previous go over this far

runners.name: Homme De Fer
runners.spotlight: Both wins in December including one over this trip; third in all four starts since his latest success and the return to 7f looks to be in his favour; solid contender.
runners.comment: C&D winner; third in last four starts and the return to 7f looks to be in his favour

runners.name: Faster Bee
runners.spotlight: Took him a long time to get off the mark, but has utilised forcing tactics to good effect over C&D this year (1241); 5lb higher than for his latest success, though, and he doesn't have a great draw.
runners.comment: Has utilised forcing tactics to good effect over C&D this year (1241), but drawn wide

runners.name: Ravenglass
runners.spotlight: Dual course winner (7f/8.6f) who was returning from six months off when beaten 2l into fourth of eight behind Bad Habits over here 17 days ago; could fare better in returning blinkers this time.
runners.comment: Off six months before fourth behind Bad Habits over C&D last time; could fare better

runners.name: Split Elevens
runners.spotlight: Winner of ten races on the AW including three over C&D; still 1lb below his last winning mark and recent efforts suggest he remains capable of playing a part.
runners.comment: Three wins over C&D and still 1lb below his last winning mark; capable of playing a part

runners.name: Bad Habits
runners.spotlight: Both wins over C&D, the latest 17 days ago with Faster Bee winning the other division in a faster time; he is only 3lb higher, but like that gelding is drawn higher this time; will need a bit more.
runners.comment: Won over C&D 17 days ago, but is 3lb higher and drawn wider this time

runners.name: Maury
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who finished unplaced in his first 11 starts, but improved to make a successful reappearance at Lingfield (7f, Polytrack) in March; not hard to pick holes in the form, though.
runners.comment: Made successful reappearance at Lingfield in March, but not hard to pick holes in the form

runners.name: Court Of Session
runners.spotlight: Not very consistent; still 3lb higher than for his easy win over C&D in January and his last three efforts leave plenty to be desired, as when making little impression at Kempton (6f, Polytack) on Wednesday.
runners.comment: 3lb higher than for his C&D win in January; last three efforts leave plenty to be desired

runners.name: Cooramook
runners.spotlight: Recorded yet another placing when third of ten at Lingfield (7f, Polytrack) 11 days ago, but that took his record to 0-17; drops to a 0-60 for the first time and should go well, but others are preferred for the win.
runners.comment: 0-17; drops to a 0-60 for the first time; should go well, but others preferred for the win

races.race_name: Free Bets On attheraces.com Handicap

runners.name: Enter Sandman
runners.spotlight: Steadily progressive and bids for a hat-trick after wins at Newcastle (5f, Tapeta) and Lingfield (6f, Polytrack); clearly thriving, but may struggle to confirm Newcastle running with Nana's Boy George.
runners.comment: Bids for a hat-trick; may struggle to confirm Newcastle running with Nana's Boy George

runners.name: Leonie
runners.spotlight: Showed promise in her first three starts and took a step forward when making a successful handicap debut at Kempton (6f, Polytrack) eight days ago; has a 6lb penalty to carry, but this is a slight drop in class; big player.
runners.comment: Made a successful handicap debut at Kempton eight days ago; big player despite 6lb penalty

runners.name: Rosieisme Darling
runners.spotlight: Both wins over 5f on Polytrack, but unplaced in all five goes at 6f; the most exposed in the field after 12 starts.
runners.comment: Both wins over 5f on Polytrack, but unplaced in five goes at 6f; relatively exposed

runners.name: Territorial Star
runners.spotlight: Placed in her first four starts, but hasn't gone on from there; didn't enjoy a clear run and was returning from 185 days when behind a couple of these at Newcastle (5f, Tapeta) last month, but more needed.
runners.comment: Placed in her first four starts, but hasn't gone on from there; more needed

runners.name: Nana's Boy George
runners.spotlight: Has improved since handicapping and only went down by a nose to Enter Sandman at Newcastle (5f, Tapeta) last month; now 7lb better off and shaped then as though a return to 6f would suit; major player.
runners.comment: Beaten a head by Enter Sandman at Newcastle last month and 7lb better off; major player

runners.name: Hellcat
runners.spotlight: Promise in first two starts for Seb Spencer over 5f at Thirsk a year ago, but has gone backwards since including on last month's stable debut/reappearance; dam won on the AW, but probably best watched.
runners.comment: Promise in her first two starts for Seb Spencer but has gone backwards since; best watched

races.race_name: Sauren Dachfonds Preis (ex Gerling-Preis) (Group 2) (Turf)
CAA Stellar Great Cheshire Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Respond
runners.spotlight: In good form on the AW since returning in February, easily justifying short odds at Chelmsford (1m2f) in March; no problem with the return to turf having won over this trip at Haydock (good) last August; can take a hold, but may settle better in this big field and he remains unexposed; a player.
runners.comment: Both wins over 1m2f on turf/AW; may settle better in this big field; remains unexposed

runners.name: Whip Cracker
runners.spotlight: Without a win since his second start as a 2yo, but several good efforts since as when beaten a length into third of 24 in the Newbury Spring Cup (1m, good ) last month; Ryan Moore keeps the ride and rain shouldn't be an issue, but the draw could have been kinder.
runners.comment: No win since second start as a 2yo, but several good efforts since; drawn wider than ideal

runners.name: Bragbor
runners.spotlight: Both wins have come on the AW at Newcastle (1m/1m2f) and was far from disgraced on his return in the Easter Classic there (1m2f) five weeks ago; makes his turf debut in a competitive race, albeit stall 1 has taken three of the last eight runnings.
runners.comment: Makes turf debut in a competitive race; stall 1 has taken three of the last eight runnings

runners.name: Triple Double A
runners.spotlight: Twice held off 1lb higher since making a successful return from eight months off at Newcastle (1m2f, AW) in February, including when behind two of these in the Rosebery; needs a resurgence and could probably do without the ground easing.
runners.comment: Twice held since making a successful return at Newcastle in February; wouldn't want rain

runners.name: Clouds Hill
runners.spotlight: Enjoyed the run of the race when making all to beat four rivals on his Yarmouth return (1m2f, good) eight days ago; has a 5lb penalty to carry and ground softer than good would be an unknown, but he remains unexposed after just five starts so makes the shortlist.
runners.comment: Made all on his Yarmouth return eight days ago; remains unexposed; makes the shortlist
runners.owner: Sheikh Hamdan bin Mohammed Al Maktoum

runners.name: Callianassa
runners.spotlight: All four wins have come over 7f/1m on the AW, the latest in February; back off the same mark as for that success, but she is 0-10 on turf and has never attempted this far before.
runners.comment: Back off last winning AW mark, but is 0-10 on turf and has never attempted this far before

runners.name: El Burhan
runners.spotlight: Has a fine strike-rate on turf (4-8) including a win at this three-day meeting (7.6f, good) a year ago; would have gone closer with a clearer run when fifth of 13 in the Rosebery at Kempton (1m3f, AW) on his reappearance (Respond third); still open to improvement and rain wouldn't be a problem; major player.
runners.comment: Has a fine strike-rate on turf (4-8); open to improvement and rain wouldn't be a problem

runners.name: Great David
runners.spotlight: Highly tried after making a winning reappearance at Nottingham (1m2f, good) a year ago; proven fresh, but hard to know how well treated he is for this return to handicap company.
runners.comment: Proven fresh, but hard to know how well treated he is for this return to handicap company

runners.name: Echalar
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced on turf; out of the frame in five starts since winning at Wolverhampton (7f, AW) in January; has plenty to prove over this trip back on grass and is 3lb out of the weights.
runners.comment: Has plenty to prove over this trip back on grass and is 3lb out of the weights

runners.name: Bravais
runners.spotlight: Winner in France and Ireland, but has beaten a total of one rival in four starts on the AW since joining this yard; down another 3lb, but hard to warm to unless the market suggests otherwise; stable also runs Echalar; non-runner 4.10 Chester Thursday.
runners.comment: Beaten just one rival in four starts on AW since joining yard; non-runner here yesterday

runners.name: Auld Toon Loon
runners.spotlight: All four wins at around this trip including one here (soft) in the summer of 2023; looked unlucky when beaten a head in this race off 3lb lower on his reappearance a year ago; won't mind what the weather does, but stall 14 isn't ideal.
runners.comment: Looked unlucky when beaten a head in this race a year ago, but stall 14 isn't ideal

runners.name: Janey Mackers
runners.spotlight: Twice highly tried after winning a handicap at Newbury (1m2f, good to soft) from 7lb lower in September; still unexposed, but she may need this after six months off and is drawn wide.
runners.comment: Still unexposed, but she may need this after six months off and is drawn wide

races.race_name: Peroni Nastro Azzurro 0.0% Handicap

runners.name: Albaydaa
runners.spotlight: Came on plenty for each 2yo run, making all in a 7f maiden on Wolverhampton AW in November; plenty to like about her reappearance in a 6f handicap at Newmarket (good) recently, where she initially failed to pick up but came home nicely to finish a close third of 13; should come on for the run and go well back over 7f; yard has won three of the last seven runnings.
runners.comment: Won 7f AW maiden on final 2yo start; staying-on 3rd in 6f Newmarket handicap latest

runners.name: May Angel
runners.spotlight: Ready winner of 7f novice on Southwell AW before highly tried in Group races on final 2yo starts; gelded and had wind surgery in the close season and returned with a useful effort to be third in a well-contested Newbury handicap (7f, good; just behind the penalised Colori Forever) last month; looks sure to be involved off the same mark.
runners.comment: Won 7f AW novice as 2yo; good 3rd in well-contested 7f Newbury handicap on return; solid

runners.name: Goldwork
runners.spotlight: Best 2yo form when winning a 6f novice at Goodwood last May and he was back near that level when a keeping-on third of eight on Kempton AW (6f) in April; heavy ground was probably against him when well held in his only 7f run but he still has stamina to prove and in tough company, too.
runners.comment: Won 6f novice at Goodwood last May; promising return on AW; stamina to prove at 7f

runners.name: Illy's Roo
runners.spotlight: All three wins over 7f, on turf and the AW, the latest on his yard debut in March; however, he's high in the weights now and his recent Newbury run (7f; 50-1) suggests another uphill battle awaits.
runners.comment: All 3 wins at 7f, on turf/AW; recent Newbury defeat suggests another uphill battle awaits

runners.name: Colori Forever
runners.spotlight: Impressive winner over 7f on Southwell AW reappearance; made favourite for a good 7f Newbury handicap since, when 6lb well in under a penalty, but failed to get into the action soon enough, finishing second to a more prominently ridden winner (May Angel just behind); still has potential despite running off his new mark and this stiff 7f can suit.
runners.comment: Impressive winner on AW comeback; creditable 2nd under penalty since; now 6lb higher

runners.name: Ay Up Duck
runners.spotlight: Unraced as a 2yo; improving with racing and he forged clear when getting off the mark on Southwell AW (back at 7f) recently; should progress further but he's taking on rivals who have already shown their mettle in turf handicaps and this is a tough start to his career on grass.
runners.comment: Forged clear on Southwell AW (7f) latest; faces a tough start to handicaps on turf debut

runners.name: Queen Tamara
runners.spotlight: Won 7f Chepstow maiden, then ran a stormer from the front in a 7f Newmarket handicap (good to firm) last September where she tended to change direction under pressure but showed no lack of fight in dividing subsequent Listed and Group-race winners; promising but this may be needed.
runners.comment: Smart run from the front when dividing subsequent Listed/Group winners on final 2yo start

runners.name: Angel Of Anfield
runners.spotlight: All turf races at sprint trips on good to firm and good, vigorously ridden when making all at Windsor last August from one who went on to better things; a similarly useful effort when runner-up on Kempton AW in October but he lacks a recent run and has a new trip to contend with.
runners.comment: All races at sprint trips, making all at Windsor in August; good AW run after; off since

runners.name: Aqua Bear
runners.spotlight: Had some useful form in Ireland as a 2yo but didn't need to improve on that when making a successful yard debut on Newcastle AW (7f) last November; gelded since; non-runner when due to make handicap debut in the 3.40 at Chester yesterday.
runners.comment: Winning yard debut on AW (7f) in November; non-runner in 3.40 at Chester yesterday

runners.name: Synners Kid
runners.spotlight: Greatly improved for the step up to 1m and switch to handicaps on final two starts for Joseph O'Brien, winning both attempts (yielding to soft/soft) last autumn; sold for 110,000gns since and gelded; has very different conditions today and goes without his regular tongue-tie.
runners.comment: Won both 1m handicaps for J O'Brien in the autumn; sold 110,000gns since; conditions query

races.race_name: Boodles Darley EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB/GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Joulany
runners.spotlight: Derby and Irish Derby entry; 800,000gns Sea The Stars yearling, brother to multiple Group 3 winner Al Aasy (1m2f-1m5f); turned over at odds-on in his two starts last autumn, but gave it a good shot when always-prominent third of ten in blanket finish to a strong novice at Newbury (1m2f, good) on reappearance; the hood worn last time is now removed; top on ratings here and likely to have come on from that run.
runners.comment: Top on ratings and likely to have come on from his reappearance third at Newbury
runners.owner: Al Shaqab Racing, Amo Racing & Valmont

runners.name: Galiyan
runners.spotlight: Closely related to 1m1f-1m2f winner Integrant (including Listed; RPR 108); 7-1 for novice at Newmarket (1m2f, good) three weeks ago but looked green on his way to 7l fourth of five; that's still good enough to enter serious calculations.
runners.comment: Green 4th at Newmarket three weeks ago but still good enough to enter serious calculations

runners.name: Glory Of The Seas
runners.spotlight: Sea The Stars colt who cost 550,000gns as a yearling; Dante and Derby entries have gone but he's still in the Irish Derby; that's after his fourth of eight in a strong-looking maiden at Newbury (1m3f, good; 4-1) three weeks ago on second start, in which he saw it out 4l better than Eben Al Khawaneej; major contender, type to improve with his racing.
runners.comment: Fourth in strong 1m3f Newbury maiden three weeks ago; major contender, type to improve

runners.name: Distorted Truth
runners.spotlight: Camelot filly who was a well-backed second of nine in maiden at Kempton (1m3f, AW) in January but a remote last of six when 18-1 for a novice at Southwell (1m3f, AW) in February; others seem more likely.
runners.comment: 2nd at Kempton in January but remote last of six at Southwell (also 1m3f, AW) in February

runners.name: Morningtoncrescent
runners.spotlight: 150-1 and on toes when 11l sixth of 14 to a 6l winner in maiden at Newbury (1m2f, good) three weeks ago, which was a lot better than debut; further progress is likely but this race may prove too tough.
runners.comment: 150-1 and on toes when sixth at Newbury three weeks ago; this race looks too tough
runners.owner: Emma Banks , Rocco, De Ramsey & Dobson

runners.name: Take A Chill Pill
runners.spotlight: 65,000gns yearling by Kingman out of Listed-placed 7f/1m winner (105); 18-1, just under 10l fifth of ten to an impressive winner in newcomers race at Newmarket (1m, good) three weeks ago, prominent long way; could do a lot better in due course but others may be more ready this time.
runners.comment: 18-1, just under 10l fifth in newcomers race at Newmarket three weeks ago; may do better

runners.name: Eben Al Khawaneej
runners.spotlight: Camelot colt (from speedy family) who looked backward and green for a strong-looking maiden race at Newbury (1m3f, good; 16-1) three weeks ago and ended up sixth of eight, with Glory Of The Seas 4l ahead; could emerge into something much better and he needs a second look, for same connections who won this with a twice-raced colt last year.
runners.comment: Backward and green on Newbury debut, 4l behind Glory Of The Seas; needs a second look

races.race_name: Deepbridge Huxley Stakes (Group 2)

runners.name: Lambourn
runners.spotlight: Very much at the top his generation last year, winning the Chester Vase at this meeting before going on to classic glory at Epsom and the Curragh (all three wins over 1m4f on good ground); he was undeniably disappointing in the Great Voltigeur (1m4f, good to firm) and St Leger (14.5f, soft), but this looks a good starting point for his 4yo career given the race conditions and he should take the beating if anything like back to his best; cheekpieces are given a go.
runners.comment: Dual Derby winner; this looks a good starting point for his 4yo career; cheekpieces on
runners.owner: Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith

runners.name: King's Gambit
runners.spotlight: No wins since landing two of his first four starts having been highly tried in the meantime, but he has a consistent record in Group/Listed company shy of the top level (22363522); again ran well when chasing home the smart Damysus on his return in the Earl Of Sefton at Newmarket (1m1f, good) last month; shortlisted with the run under his belt.
runners.comment: Has consistent record in Group/Listed company shy of the top level (22363522); shortlisted

runners.name: Sky Safari
runners.spotlight: Smart on the AW (1111121), her latest success coming in the Group 3 Winter Derby at Lingfield (1m2f) in February; her winning racecourse debut came on turf (7f, soft), but this looks a tough task on her return to grass.
runners.comment: Smart on the AW (1111121), but this looks a tough task on her return to grass

runners.name: Starford
runners.spotlight: Progressive in handicaps last season but took his form to a new level when getting the better of a stablemate in a Group 3 at the Curragh (1m2f, soft) last month; may yet be capable of even better, but he could do with some rain.
runners.comment: Won a Group 3 at the Curragh last month; capable of better, but could do with some rain

runners.name: Royal Rhyme
runners.spotlight: Did well early in his carerer including when beating three rivals in the 2024 Brigadier Gerard (1m2f, soft); 0-8 since, albeit twice placed at the highest level; pulled too hard when beaten over 6l into sixth of 12 behind Starford on his return in a Group 3 at the Curragh (1m2f, soft) last month, but that should have taken the freshness out of him; could probably do with the ground easing, though.
runners.comment: Pulled too hard on his reappearance but that should have taken the freshness out of him
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: High Stock
runners.spotlight: Highly tried after his winning Newmarket debut in April last year and ran well on a couple of occasions, as when beaten a neck in the Dee Stakes over C&D (good); has a bit to find to mix it with the majority of these, though.
runners.comment: Beaten a neck in last year's Dee Stakes but has a bit to find with the majority of these

runners.name: Ice Max
runners.spotlight: Ended last season with a pair of 1m victories, the latest in a Nottingham Listed event (soft); not totally disgraced when fifth of eight in a Group 3 at Dusseldorf (1m, heavy) on last month's return, but that does indicate his limitations; races beyond an extended 1m for the first time; stable also runs Royal Rhyme.
runners.comment: Fifth in Dusseldorf Group 3 on his return; races beyond an extended 1m for the first time
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Sparks Fly
runners.spotlight: Admirable mare who is 12-21 on turf including wins at Group 3/Listed level; has winning form over C&D, but will need to improve plenty on her reappearance effort at Saint-Cloud in March if she is to play a part here.
runners.comment: 12-21 on turf but will need to improve plenty on her reappearance effort in March

runners.name: Bay City Roller
runners.spotlight: Showed some high-class form last year including when beaten less than a length by Daryz in the Prix Egene Adam; hit the big time when making all to win the Group 1 Grosser Allianz Preis von Bayern by 7l at Munich (1m4f, soft) in November; his 5lb penalty won't make things easy and this should set him up nicely for bigger targets ahead, but he goes well fresh and shouldn't be underestimated.
runners.comment: Has a 5lb penalty to carry, but he goes well fresh and shouldn't be underestimated

races.race_name: Ladbrokes Chester Cup (Heritage Handicap) (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Berkshire Sundance
runners.spotlight: Missed most of last season but won on good/AW in four of his six starts since, the latest in clearcut fashion with first-time cheekpieces at Newcastle on AW Championships day; raised another 7lb, drawn wide and lacks any race experience at this track, but he's going from strength to strength.
runners.comment: Missed most of last season but won on good/AW in four of his six starts since; drawn wide
runners.owner: Berkshire Parts & Panels Ltd No1 Fanclub

runners.name: Peaky Blinder
runners.spotlight: 4yo who has had just six races, on last two occasions winning well in handicaps at Redcar (1m6f, good to soft) in October and Southwell (slowly run 2m, AW; gelded) four weeks ago; raised another 6lb and substantially in class, but he's probably going places and is one for the shortlist.
runners.comment: Six races, winning well last two; up another 6lb and substantially in class, but shortlist

runners.name: Chemistry
runners.spotlight: His time with Aidan O'Brien and Ivan Furtado was on the chequered side but three bits of useful form read well compared to this mark, including his staying-on fourth at Newcastle (2m, AW) in December off 5lb higher; made a successful yard/hurdles debut at Sedgefield four weeks ago, which is a light year away from this assignment but a case can be made.
runners.comment: Three bits of useful form read well compared to this mark and a case can be made for him
runners.owner: James Paul Services To Industry Ltd

runners.name: Blindedbythelights
runners.spotlight: Versatile tactically; his only two wins were in 2023 but this highly reliable handicapper has been runner-up in six of his seven races since, including in useful company and also over 2m when he wore cheekpieces on last three occasions (hung right and lost out to a rallying rival last time); now tries blinkers and has each-way claims from low draw.
runners.comment: Highly reliable and has been runner-up in six of his last seven races; different headgear

runners.name: Moon Over Miami
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 5yo who won the first three of his four races on turf, including a narrow rallying win at Newbury (2m, good) last April; absent afterwards until below-form joint-second favourite from the front at Musselburgh (1m6f) five weeks ago but he should be a lot more potent with today's trip and that run under his belt; interesting.
runners.comment: Lightly raced 5yo; should be a lot more potent again with last month's run under his belt

runners.name: Team Player
runners.spotlight: Led on the post here for the first of two 1m4f wins last summer for Gemma Tutty and, with tongue-tie enlisted, hit major new heights for new yard this year with his wins from off the pace at Newcastle (2m, AW) and Musselburgh (1m6f, soft); raised another 9lb, which demands more, but those last two wins were delivered in some style and he has to be considered seriously.
runners.comment: Raised another 9lb today, which demands more, but he won his last two starts in some style

runners.name: Spirit Mixer
runners.spotlight: One of three for the yard; won the Northumberland Plate at Newcastle (2m, AW) last June and has a 4-7 record at Chester, although his two performances in this race were muted (from double-figure draws); reappearance six weeks ago matched his best 2025 form but a bit extra will be needed.
runners.comment: Has a 4-7 record at Chester, although his two performances in this race were muted

runners.name: Galileo Dame
runners.spotlight: Yard runs three; well beaten in Listed races last autumn but, fit from hurdling, made a good Flat return when second at Navan (1m6f, heavy) at the end of March; that was both her Flat handicap debut and the furthest she's been in this sphere, which opens up possibilities for her as a Flat stayer.
runners.comment: Made a good return to the Flat when second at Navan (1m6f, heavy) at the end of March
runners.owner: Annus Mirabilis Synd'/Sean O'Driscoll/Middle Cow Unlimited

runners.name: Puturhandstogether
runners.spotlight: One of three for Joseph O'Brien; won 4yo handicap hurdle at 2025 Cheltenham Festival and also took a huge prize for last September's Irish Cesarewitch on Flat at the Curragh (2m1f, yielding); underwhelming in his three handicap hurdles in November-April, which is a concern, as is today's widest stall, but his Curragh win (bursting clear 1f out) was delivered with considerably more authority than the winning margin suggests; respected.
runners.comment: Underwhelming over hurdles recently but two big wins (hurdles/Flat) in 2025 demand respect

runners.name: Aimeric
runners.spotlight: Won sole start here, a 2022 maiden; smart at 1m3f-1m6f for Roger Varian and Karl Burke but reappearance was easily the best of three runs last year and, after a 21,000gns sale in November, well beaten at 11-10 for hurdles/yard debut two weeks ago; that's not the most promising stepping stone to this.
runners.comment: Formerly smart at 1m3f-1m6f; well beaten at 11-10 for hurdles/yard debut recently

runners.name: A Piece Of Heaven
runners.spotlight: One of three runners for the yard; lightly raced 8yo who was second in the 2m1f Irish Cesarewitch in 2024 and also in non-handicaps over 10.5f and 1m6f on his latest two starts this year; will probably be in good form but needs a career best.
runners.comment: Lightly raced 8yo; second in 2024 Irish Cesarewitch and also on his latest two starts

runners.name: Alphonse Le Grande
runners.spotlight: Held up; an excellent 2024 included wins in the Chester Plate on this card (his only run here) and the Cesarewitch at Newmarket; 2025 campaign was initially promising then frustrating, including with trouble in running, but ended up being disappointing; has a recent 1m2f run under his belt and would be competitive off this slightly reduced mark if back on song.
runners.comment: Won 2024 C&D Plate; promising, frustrating then disappointing in 2025, but not ruled out

runners.name: Duraji
runners.spotlight: Sole win (ten starts) for Dermot Weld was a maiden in 2024; has tried Group/Listed races but his most competitive show last year came when fourth of 14 in 1m6f handicap at Glorious Goodwood, off 3lb higher than today; tried a hood on AW debut at Lingfield in February and has since changed stables (same owners); has a bit to prove and such a wide draw is unlikely to help.
runners.comment: Just a maiden win for Dermot Weld; also fourth of 14 in 1m6f handicap at Glorious Goodwood

runners.name: Maxi King
runners.spotlight: Reliable sort with a good first-time-out record, and he looks well worth this first foray beyond 14.5f, but there is something to prove if the ground if firmer than good; promising 7lb claimer has his first ride at Chester.
runners.comment: Good first-time-out record; worth first foray beyond 14.5f; query on firmer than good

runners.name: Zanndabad
runners.spotlight: An unlucky, strong-finishing third in this race in 2024 (good to firm; his only run here) and has a 1lb lower mark today; flickers of that ability on occasions since, though had to have his sights lowered for two hurdle wins last autumn; tailed off at 40-1 in a Grade 1 novice hurdle four weeks ago, after a winter break, and could be more interesting this time.
runners.comment: Unlucky, strong-finishing third in this in 2024; 1lb lower today and some flickers since

runners.name: Lips Freedom
runners.spotlight: Three Flat wins when trained in Germany, in Italian 1m4f Listed race on last two occasions (August 2023 and 2024); slow to find his feet in Ireland but winning a 2m2f handicap hurdle on latest outing was a positive development in tongue-tie first time; he'd have had a much higher mark two years ago but others have more solid claims.
runners.comment: Hurdle win was a positive development in first tongue-tie but others have stronger claims

runners.name: Tashkhan
runners.spotlight: Fit from a mixed hurdles campaign; formerly very smart on Flat, including with a C&D handicap win in 2023 but he's not scored in this sphere since, had a disappointing 2025 and all wins have been on soft or heavy ground.
runners.comment: Fit from a mixed hurdles campaign; all wins have been on soft or heavy ground

races.race_name: Tom Jones Live At Chester Racecourse Handicap

runners.name: The Good Biscuit
runners.spotlight: Ended 2025 in top form and he has returned to action with two solid placed efforts; 2lb ahead of the handicapper and no obvious reason why he wouldn't give it another good shot.
runners.comment: Still improving and he is 2lb ahead of the handicapper; should make another bold bid

runners.name: Jonny Concrete
runners.spotlight: Ended 2025 with a couple of modest efforts but his form in the first half of the year brings him right into the reckoning; can go well fresh and he looks a leading contender eased in class.
runners.comment: Ended 2025 quietly but his best efforts last year give him good claims at this level

runners.name: Queue Dos
runners.spotlight: 4-10 on AW but 0-11 on turf; unlikely to be enhancing that record from the outside stall.
runners.comment: 4-10 on AW but 0-11 on turf; unlikely to be enhancing that record from the outside stall

runners.name: Clonmacash
runners.spotlight: Fully exposed 5yo but he recorded the best RPR of his 50-race career when winning at Dundalk last month (6f); while effective on turf, he looks high enough in the weights now returning to grass; well drawn but others appeal more for win purposes.
runners.comment: Career-best RPR when winning at Dundalk last month (6f); turf form is not quite as good

runners.name: Kirkdale
runners.spotlight: 7f turf win as a 2yo (soft ground); he has looked an improved performer in two runs this spring, coming from off the pace to win 6f handicaps at Wolverhampton and Southwell; drawn wide but likely to be dropped in and he can't be discounted granted normal luck in running.
runners.comment: He has looked an improved performer this spring, winning twice on AW (6f); tricky draw

runners.name: Hoodie Hoo
runners.spotlight: Yet to win a handicap but he went close at Nottingham last October and again finishing second on last month's reappearance at Lingfield (6f, AW); this is more competitive but he isn't fully exposed.
runners.comment: 0-8 in handicaps but he has threatened, finishing 2nd twice; still relatively low mileage
runners.owner: Ged Mason, Sir Alex Ferguson, Jim McGoff

runners.name: Aisling Oscar
runners.spotlight: He has been a revelation on AW for this yard, winning eight times since November; better over longer trips, yet to place in ten turf runs and drawn wide so this is unlikely to be his day.
runners.comment: Prolific on AW this winter but 0-10 on turf, drawn wide and best over further

runners.name: Rosenpur
runners.spotlight: Well suited by a left-hand turn, winning twice here over 5f last summer; promising seasonal return at Catterick (6f, good) but he wasn't in the same form at Musselburgh (5f, good) nine days ago; handy draw and he can't be discounted.
runners.comment: Conditions should suit and he isn't handicapped out of things; one to consider

runners.name: Secret Guest
runners.spotlight: He hasn't been at his best in recent starts but he has dipped to a dangerous mark and he has landed a good draw; this is a tough race in which to try and turn things around though.
runners.comment: Not at best in recent starts and looks vulnerable despite a reduced mark and good draw

runners.name: Kiniro
runners.spotlight: Won twice for Harry Charlton, both over 6f on Lingfield's AW track; has shown promise on turf; sold for 40,000gns in October; double-figure draw and revised mark both ask stiffer questions of him but interesting that Oisin Murphy, who rode him to success when last seen, is back in the saddle.
runners.comment: Two AW wins for Harry Charlton; sold 40,000gns; drawn wide but Oisin Murphy is booked

runners.name: Miraculous
runners.spotlight: Two C&D runs for Roger Varian yielded a win and a second; may have needed the outing on last month's stable debut and he's well treated if that run brings him on; more appealing than many.
runners.comment: C&D record reads 12; sharper for last month's stable debut; more appealing than many

runners.name: Paws For Thought
runners.spotlight: Six times a course winner, including over this trip; can go well fresh and not handicapped out of things but slower ground would have been preferable.
runners.comment: Six-time course winner; can go well fresh and feasibly weighted; slower ground ideal

runners.name: Bright
runners.spotlight: His last three wins have come on the AW at Newcastle but he is effective on turf and this mark shouldn't be beyond him; stall 15 is a severe hindrance though.
runners.comment: Not handicapped out of things but defying stall 15 would be a notable achievement

runners.name: Intervention
runners.spotlight: Won last season's Great St Wilfrid at Ripon off a 5lb higher mark; didn't run badly at Nottingham 12 days ago (5f, good) but he has a double-figure stall to contend with and could prove vulnerable.
runners.comment: On a dangerous mark but he has a wide stall to contend with and may prove vulnerable again

runners.name: Manila Scouse
runners.spotlight: On a competitive mark with a handy inside draw but he is returning from a seven month absence and his connections are also represented by Miraculous.
runners.comment: Good mark and well drawn but returning from seven months off; connections run two

races.race_name: Deepbridge Huxley Stakes (Group 2)

runners.name: Lambourn
runners.spotlight: Very much at the top his generation last year, winning the Chester Vase at this meeting before going on to classic glory at Epsom and the Curragh (all three wins over 1m4f on good ground); he was undeniably disappointing in the Great Voltigeur (1m4f, good to firm) and St Leger (14.5f, soft), but this looks a good starting point for his 4yo career given the race conditions and he should take the beating if anything like back to his best; cheekpieces are given a go.
runners.comment: Dual Derby winner; this looks a good starting point for his 4yo career; cheekpieces on
runners.owner: Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith

runners.name: King's Gambit
runners.spotlight: No wins since landing two of his first four starts having been highly tried in the meantime, but he has a consistent record in Group/Listed company shy of the top level (22363522); again ran well when chasing home the smart Damysus on his return in the Earl Of Sefton at Newmarket (1m1f, good) last month; shortlisted with the run under his belt.
runners.comment: Has consistent record in Group/Listed company shy of the top level (22363522); shortlisted

runners.name: Sky Safari
runners.spotlight: Smart on the AW (1111121), her latest success coming in the Group 3 Winter Derby at Lingfield (1m2f) in February; her winning racecourse debut came on turf (7f, soft), but this looks a tough task on her return to grass.
runners.comment: Smart on the AW (1111121), but this looks a tough task on her return to grass

runners.name: Starford
runners.spotlight: Progressive in handicaps last season but took his form to a new level when getting the better of a stablemate in a Group 3 at the Curragh (1m2f, soft) last month; may yet be capable of even better, but he could do with some rain.
runners.comment: Won a Group 3 at the Curragh last month; capable of better, but could do with some rain

runners.name: Royal Rhyme
runners.spotlight: Did well early in his carerer including when beating three rivals in the 2024 Brigadier Gerard (1m2f, soft); 0-8 since, albeit twice placed at the highest level; pulled too hard when beaten over 6l into sixth of 12 behind Starford on his return in a Group 3 at the Curragh (1m2f, soft) last month, but that should have taken the freshness out of him; could probably do with the ground easing, though.
runners.comment: Pulled too hard on his reappearance but that should have taken the freshness out of him
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: High Stock
runners.spotlight: Highly tried after his winning Newmarket debut in April last year and ran well on a couple of occasions, as when beaten a neck in the Dee Stakes over C&D (good); has a bit to find to mix it with the majority of these, though.
runners.comment: Beaten a neck in last year's Dee Stakes but has a bit to find with the majority of these

runners.name: Ice Max
runners.spotlight: Ended last season with a pair of 1m victories, the latest in a Nottingham Listed event (soft); not totally disgraced when fifth of eight in a Group 3 at Dusseldorf (1m, heavy) on last month's return, but that does indicate his limitations; races beyond an extended 1m for the first time; stable also runs Royal Rhyme.
runners.comment: Fifth in Dusseldorf Group 3 on his return; races beyond an extended 1m for the first time
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Sparks Fly
runners.spotlight: Admirable mare who is 12-21 on turf including wins at Group 3/Listed level; has winning form over C&D, but will need to improve plenty on her reappearance effort at Saint-Cloud in March if she is to play a part here.
runners.comment: 12-21 on turf but will need to improve plenty on her reappearance effort in March

runners.name: Bay City Roller
runners.spotlight: Showed some high-class form last year including when beaten less than a length by Daryz in the Prix Egene Adam; hit the big time when making all to win the Group 1 Grosser Allianz Preis von Bayern by 7l at Munich (1m4f, soft) in November; his 5lb penalty won't make things easy and this should set him up nicely for bigger targets ahead, but he goes well fresh and shouldn't be underestimated.
runners.comment: Has a 5lb penalty to carry, but he goes well fresh and shouldn't be underestimated

races.race_name: Sauren Dachfonds Preis (ex Gerling-Preis) (Group 2) (Turf)
